situations.title: Formal Writing & Funchal
situations.summary: Draft formal messages and discuss Funchal local geography.

variants.text: Venho por este meio informar que a fatura foi paga.
variants.translation: I am writing to inform you that the invoice has been paid.

variants.text: Venho por este meio solicitar a sua intervenção.
variants.translation: I am writing to request your intervention.

variants.text: Venho por este meio agradecer a sua pronta resposta.
variants.translation: I am writing to thank you for your prompt reply.

variants.text: Agradecia se pudesse enviar o comprovativo.
variants.translation: I would be grateful if you could send the proof.

variants.text: Agradecia se pudesse verificar o meu pedido.
variants.translation: I would be grateful if you could check my request.
I would be grateful if you could contact me tomorrow.

variants.note: A very common formal closing in Portugal.
Slightly less formal but still polite.

dialogues.title: Funchal Landmarks
dialogues.context: A tourist asks a local for directions to famous spots in Funchal.

lines.speaker: Turista
lines.text: Bom dia, desculpe. Onde fica a Sé?
lines.translation: Good morning, excuse me. Where is the Cathedral?

lines.speaker: Local
lines.text: A Sé? Pois, é fácil. É mesmo ali, na baixa. Siga em frente.
lines.translation: The Cathedral? Right, it's easy. It's right there, downtown. Go straight ahead.

lines.speaker: Turista
lines.text: E a zona velha? É perto do teleférico?
lines.translation: And the old town? Is it near the cable car?

lines.speaker: Local
lines.text: Sim, a zona velha é junto ao teleférico, mesmo ao lado. Não tem como enganar.
lines.translation: Yes, the old town is next to the cable car, right beside it. You can't miss it.

dialogues.title: Confirming a Payment by Phone
dialogues.context: A person calls a customer service to confirm a bill payment.

lines.speaker: Atendente
lines.text: Serviço de Apoio ao Cliente, bom dia. Em que posso ajudar?
lines.translation: Customer Support Service, good morning. How can I help?

lines.speaker: Cliente
lines.text: Bom dia. Venho por este meio confirmar o pagamento da fatura número dois, três, quatro, cinco.
lines.translation: Good morning. I am calling to confirm the payment of invoice number two, three, four, five.

lines.speaker: Atendente
lines.text: Um momento, por favor... Sim, está tudo em ordem. O pagamento foi registado.
lines.translation: One moment, please... Yes, everything is in order. The payment has been registered.

lines.speaker: Cliente
lines.text: Agradecia se pudesse enviar um comprovativo por email.
lines.translation: I would be grateful if you could send a proof by email.

lines.speaker: Atendente
lines.text: Pois, claro. Qual é o seu endereço de email, por favor?
lines.translation: Yes, of course. What is your email address, please?

roleplay.scenario: You are calling the Funchal Municipal Services to report a broken street light in your neighbourhood. You need to make a formal request and provide details.

nodes.npc_text: Serviços Municipais do Funchal, bom dia. Em que posso ajudar?
nodes.npc_translation: Funchal Municipal Services, good morning. How can I help?

options.text: Bom dia. Venho por este meio reportar uma avaria na iluminação pública.
options.translation: Good morning. I am calling to report a fault in the public lighting.
options.feedback: Excellent formal opening. Now provide more details about the problem.

options.text: Olá. A luz da rua está estragada.
options.translation: Hi. The street light is broken.
options.feedback: This is too informal for a municipal service. Try to use a more formal opening.

nodes.npc_text: Certo. E qual é a morada da avaria, por favor?
nodes.npc_translation: Understood. And what is the address of the fault, please?

options.text: A avaria é na Rua da Levada, número vinte e cinco. O poste está mesmo em frente à casa.
options.translation: The fault is on Rua da Levada, number twenty-five. The pole is right in front of the house.
options.feedback: Good, you provided the necessary details clearly.

options.text: É numa rua perto da Sé. Não sei o número.
options.translation: It's on a street near the Cathedral. I don't know the number.
options.feedback: You need to be more specific. Try to find the exact address or a clear landmark.

nodes.npc_text: Pode ser mais preciso, por favor? Precisamos da morada exata para enviar uma equipa.
nodes.npc_translation: Can you be more precise, please? We need the exact address to send a team.

options.text: Desculpe. É na Rua da Levada, número vinte e cinco. O poste está mesmo em frente à casa.
options.translation: Sorry. It's on Rua da Levada, number twenty-five. The pole is right in front of the house.
options.feedback: Much better! Providing a precise address is crucial.

options.text: Não consigo. Vou ligar mais tarde.
options.translation: I can't. I'll call later.
options.feedback: It's okay to end the call if you don't have the information, but try to be polite.

nodes.npc_text: Muito bem. Registámos o seu pedido. Uma equipa irá verificar a situação o mais breve possível. Agradecemos o seu contacto.
nodes.npc_translation: Very good. We have registered your request. A team will check the situation as soon as possible. We thank you for your contact.

options.text: Agradecia se pudesse enviar uma notificação quando estiver resolvido.
options.translation: I would be grateful if you could send a notification when it's resolved.
options.feedback: A good, polite follow-up request. Now conclude the call.

options.text: Obrigado.
options.translation: Thank you.
options.feedback: A simple 'thank you' is fine, but you could add a formal closing.

nodes.npc_text: De nada. Tenha um bom dia.
nodes.npc_translation: You're welcome. Have a good day.

options.text: Muito obrigado. Com os melhores cumprimentos, tenha um bom dia.
options.translation: Thank you very much. With best regards, have a good day.
options.feedback: A perfect, polite closing for a formal call.

mission.title: Report a Public Service Issue Formally
prep: Practice using 'Venho por este meio...' to state your purpose clearly.
Prepare to give specific details like an address or location.
Rehearse formal phrases like 'Agradecia se pudesse...' for requests.
Practice polite greetings and closings for a phone call.
fallback_phrases: Não percebi bem. Pode falar mais devagar?
Qual é o seu nome, por favor?
likely_responses: Em que zona da cidade é?

cultural_notes.title: Formalidade no Telefone em Portugal
cultural_notes.body: When calling official services or businesses in Portugal, it's common to maintain a formal tone. Always start with a 'Bom dia' or 'Boa tarde', and use 'o senhor' / 'a senhora' even if you don't know the person. Avoid overly casual language. Formal closings like 'Com os melhores cumprimentos' or 'Atenciosamente' are also appropriate, even on the phone, before saying 'Tenha um bom dia'.

cultural_notes.title: Estrutura de um Pedido Formal
cultural_notes.body: A formal request often follows a clear structure: 1. State the purpose directly using phrases like 'Venho por este meio informar que...' or 'Venho por este meio solicitar...'. 2. Provide all necessary details clearly and concisely. 3. Make any specific requests using 'Agradecia se pudesse...'. 4. Conclude with a polite thank you and a formal closing.

situations.title: Local Beaches & Hiking
situations.summary: Speak about Madeiran outdoor life with genuine local knowledge.

phrase_patterns.base: Para praia de areia, recomendo Calheta.

variants.text: Para praia de seixo, recomendo a Formosa.
variants.translation: For a pebble beach, I recommend Formosa.

variants.text: Para um bom trilho, recomendo a Levada do Rei.
variants.translation: For a good trail, I recommend Levada do Rei.
variants.note: Reuses 'trilho' vocabulary.

variants.text: Para ver uma cascata, recomendo a do Risco.
variants.translation: To see a waterfall, I recommend Risco's.
variants.note: Reuses 'cascata' vocabulary.

variants.text: Não te esqueças dos ténis de caminhada.
É bom levar um casaco, pode ficar fresco.
variants.translation: It's good to bring a jacket, it can get cool.

phrase_patterns.base: Em maio as flores estão em pleno.

variants.text: Em agosto as cascatas estão com mais água.
variants.translation: In August the waterfalls have more water.

variants.text: Em junho os trilhos são mais verdes.
variants.translation: In June the trails are greener.

variants.text: Em setembro o tempo está ótimo para banhos de sol.
variants.translation: In September the weather is great for sunbathing.

dialogues.title: Beach Recommendations
dialogues.context: A tourist asks a local for beach recommendations in Madeira.

lines.speaker: Turista
lines.text: Olá! Queria ir à praia. Qual recomendas?
lines.translation: Hello! I wanted to go to the beach. Which one do you recommend?

lines.speaker: Local
lines.text: Pois, para praia de areia, recomendo Calheta. É muito boa.
lines.translation: Well, for a sand beach, I recommend Calheta. It's very good.

lines.speaker: Turista
lines.text: E para praia de seixo? Há alguma boa?
lines.translation: And for a pebble beach? Is there a good one?

lines.speaker: Local
lines.text: Sim, a Formosa, perto do Funchal. Ou a do Seixal, no norte.
lines.translation: Yes, Formosa, near Funchal. Or Seixal's, in the north.

dialogues.title: Hiking Preparations
dialogues.context: Two friends are planning a hike on a levada.

lines.speaker: Amigo 1
lines.text: Então, vamos fazer aquele trilho da Levada do Alecrim amanhã?
lines.translation: So, are we doing that Levada do Alecrim trail tomorrow?

lines.speaker: Amigo 2
lines.text: Pois, sim! Mas leva sempre capa de chuva, o tempo muda rápido na serra.
lines.translation: Yes, sure! But always bring a rain jacket, the weather changes fast in the mountains.

lines.speaker: Amigo 1
lines.text: Ah, boa ideia! E os ténis de caminhada, claro.
lines.translation: Ah, good idea! And hiking shoes, of course.

lines.speaker: Amigo 2
lines.text: Isso mesmo! E água. Vamos ver uma cascata bonita lá.
lines.translation: That's right! And water. We're going to see a beautiful waterfall there.

roleplay.scenario: You are at a local information desk asking about a good hiking trail.

nodes.npc_text: Olá! Posso ajudar?
nodes.npc_translation: Hello! Can I help you?

options.text: Sim, queria saber de um bom trilho.
options.translation: Yes, I wanted to know about a good trail.
options.feedback: Good opening! You clearly state your need.

options.text: Onde posso encontrar uma praia de areia?
options.translation: Where can I find a sand beach?
options.feedback: You asked about a beach, but the scenario is about hiking. Try to stick to the topic.

nodes.npc_text: Para um trilho fácil e bonito, recomendo a Levada do Alecrim. Tem uma cascata no fim. É para amanhã?
nodes.npc_translation: For an easy and beautiful trail, I recommend Levada do Alecrim. It has a waterfall at the end. Is it for tomorrow?

options.text: Sim, é para amanhã. O que devo levar?
options.translation: Yes, it's for tomorrow. What should I bring?
options.feedback: Excellent, you confirmed and asked for important details.

options.text: Não, talvez noutro dia. Há mais cascatas?
options.translation: No, maybe another day. Are there more waterfalls?
options.feedback: You changed the plan but still asked a relevant question.

nodes.npc_text: Para praia de areia, recomendo Calheta. É muito popular. Quer saber mais sobre trilhos?
nodes.npc_translation: For a sand beach, I recommend Calheta. It's very popular. Do you want to know more about trails?

options.text: Sim, por favor. Queria mesmo um trilho.
options.translation: Yes, please. I really wanted a trail.
options.feedback: Good recovery, you redirected to the scenario's topic.

nodes.npc_text: Leva sempre capa de chuva e ténis de caminhada. E água, claro. O tempo pode mudar rápido na serra.
nodes.npc_translation: Always bring a rain jacket and hiking shoes. And water, of course. The weather can change quickly in the mountains.

options.text: Pois é, obrigado pelas dicas!
options.translation: That's right, thanks for the tips!
options.feedback: You acknowledged the advice and thanked them. Good ending.

options.text: Está bem. E onde fica o início do trilho?
options.translation: Okay. And where is the start of the trail?
options.feedback: You asked for more practical information. Very good!

nodes.npc_text: Sim, há muitas. A do Risco é famosa, mas o trilho é um bocado mais longo. Para ver muitas cascatas, o melhor é ir no inverno ou primavera.
nodes.npc_translation: Yes, there are many. Risco's is famous, but the trail is a bit longer. To see many waterfalls, it's best to go in winter or spring.

options.text: Obrigado pela informação!
options.translation: Thanks for the information!
options.feedback: You thanked the person. Good ending.

nodes.npc_text: O início é na Rabaçal, no concelho da Calheta. Precisa de autocarro ou vai de carro?
nodes.npc_translation: The start is in Rabaçal, in the municipality of Calheta. Do you need a bus or are you going by car?

options.text: Vou de carro. Obrigado!
options.translation: I'm going by car. Thank you!
options.feedback: You provided the information and thanked them. Good ending.

options.text: Preciso de autocarro, por favor.
options.translation: I need a bus, please.
options.feedback: You asked for more details, which is practical. Good ending.

mission.title: Plan a Madeiran Hike
prep: Ask a local for a good hiking trail.
Ask what kind of gear you should bring.
Ask about the best time of year to see waterfalls.
fallback_phrases: Pode falar mais devagar, por favor?

cultural_notes.title: Addressing Locals
cultural_notes.body: In Madeira, as in mainland Portugal, it's common to use 'tu' (informal 'you') with people your age or younger, or in casual settings. For older people, shopkeepers, or officials, use 'o senhor' (the gentleman) or 'a senhora' (the lady) with the third-person verb form for politeness. Avoid 'você' as it can sound distant or even rude in some contexts.

cultural_notes.title: Madeiran Weather and Hiking
cultural_notes.body: The weather in Madeira can be very unpredictable, especially in the mountains (serra). It's common for it to be sunny on the coast and rainy or foggy inland on the same day. Always check the forecast for the specific area you plan to hike and be prepared with appropriate clothing, including a rain jacket, even if the day starts sunny.

situations.summary: Consolidate everything from Week 7. Performance under pressure.

dialogues.title: Leaky Tap
dialogues.context: A resident calls a plumber because of a leaky tap in the kitchen.

lines.speaker: Cliente
lines.text: Bom dia, é da canalização 'Rápido & Bom'?
lines.translation: Good morning, is this 'Rápido & Bom' plumbing?

lines.speaker: Canalizador
lines.text: Sim, pois não. Em que posso ajudar?
lines.translation: Yes, how can I help you?

lines.speaker: Cliente
lines.text: Tenho uma torneira a pingar na cozinha. Preciso de um canalizador.
lines.translation: I have a tap dripping in the kitchen. I need a plumber.

lines.speaker: Canalizador
lines.text: Ah, pois. Uma torneira a pingar. É urgente? Temos um técnico livre p'ra amanhã de manhã.
lines.translation: Ah, right. A dripping tap. Is it urgent? We have a technician free for tomorrow morning.

lines.speaker: Cliente
lines.text: Amanhã de manhã está ótimo. É possível entre as nove e as dez?
lines.translation: Tomorrow morning is great. Is it possible between nine and ten?

lines.speaker: Canalizador
lines.text: Sim, posso agendar. Qual é a sua morada e o seu nome, por favor?
lines.translation: Yes, I can schedule that. What is your address and name, please?

dialogues.title: Missing Delivery
dialogues.context: A neighbour asks about a package, but it seems to be missing.

lines.speaker: Vizinha
lines.text: Bom dia, Inês. Recebeste alguma encomenda p'ra mim?
lines.translation: Good morning, Inês. Did you receive any package for me?

lines.speaker: Inês
lines.text: Bom dia, Dona Maria! Não, não recebi nada. Porquê?
lines.translation: Good morning, Dona Maria! No, I didn't receive anything. Why?

lines.speaker: Vizinha
lines.text: Pois é que a transportadora diz que entregou ontem. Era um livro.
lines.translation: Well, the delivery company says they delivered it yesterday. It was a book.

lines.speaker: Inês
lines.text: Ontem? Não vi ninguém da transportadora. Estive cá o dia todo. Que estranho!
lines.translation: Yesterday? I didn't see anyone from the delivery company. I was here all day. How strange!

lines.speaker: Vizinha
lines.text: Pois é. Vou ter de ligar p'ra eles. Obrigada na mesma, querida.
lines.translation: Indeed. I'll have to call them. Thanks anyway, dear.

roleplay.scenario: You are calling the electricity company because your last bill is much higher than usual, and you think there's a mistake. You need to explain the problem and ask them to check it.

nodes.npc_text: Boa tarde, EDP. Em que posso ajudar?
nodes.npc_translation: Good afternoon, EDP. How can I help you?

options.text: Boa tarde. A minha fatura da eletricidade está muito alta.
options.translation: Good afternoon. My electricity bill is very high.
options.feedback: Good start! You clearly state the problem.

options.text: Olá. Queria falar sobre a minha conta.
options.translation: Hello. I wanted to talk about my account.
options.feedback: A bit vague, but acceptable. Try to be more specific next time.

nodes.npc_text: Compreendo. Qual é o seu número de cliente, por favor?
nodes.npc_translation: I understand. What is your client number, please?

options.feedback: Direct and correct. Now explain the issue with the bill.

options.text: O meu número de cliente está na fatura. É 123456789.
options.translation: My client number is on the bill. It's 123456789.
options.feedback: Excellent, you're giving the information clearly.

nodes.npc_text: Obrigada. Pode explicar qual é o problema com a fatura?
nodes.npc_translation: Thank you. Can you explain what the problem is with the bill?

options.text: Sim. Normalmente pago 30 euros, mas esta fatura é de 80 euros. Acho que há um erro.
options.translation: Yes. I usually pay 30 euros, but this bill is 80 euros. I think there's a mistake.
options.feedback: Very clear explanation, comparing with your usual bill.

options.text: É muito mais alta do que o normal. Não percebo porquê.
options.translation: It's much higher than normal. I don't understand why.
options.feedback: Good, but try to be more specific if you can (e.g., give numbers).

nodes.npc_text: Certo. Vou verificar aqui no sistema. Um momento, por favor.
nodes.npc_translation: Okay. I'll check here in the system. One moment, please.

options.text: Pois não. Obrigada.
options.translation: Of course. Thank you.
options.feedback: Polite and appropriate response while waiting.

options.text: Está bem.
options.translation: Okay.
options.feedback: Simple and correct.

nodes.npc_text: Senhor/a, parece que houve uma leitura estimada que foi muito alta. Posso fazer um acerto ou enviar um técnico para verificar o contador.
nodes.npc_translation: Sir/Madam, it seems there was an estimated reading that was too high. I can make an adjustment or send a technician to check the meter.

options.text: Por favor, pode fazer o acerto. Não preciso de um técnico.
options.translation: Please, can you make the adjustment. I don't need a technician.
options.feedback: Good, you chose the simpler solution.

options.text: Prefiro que um técnico venha verificar o contador, por favor.
options.translation: I prefer that a technician comes to check the meter, please.
options.feedback: Good, you're asking for a more thorough check.

mission.title: Call the electricity company about an incorrect bill
prep: Find your client number on a previous bill.
Note down the amount of the current bill and your usual average bill.
Be ready to explain why you think the bill is wrong.
fallback_phrases: Não percebi, pode repetir, por favor?
Pode falar mais devagar, por favor?
Qual é o meu número de cliente?
likely_responses: Bom dia/Boa tarde, em que posso ajudar?
Qual é o seu número de cliente?
Qual é o problema com a fatura?

cultural_notes.title: Politeness in Service Interactions
cultural_notes.body: When interacting with service staff (e.g., plumbers, utility company representatives), it's customary to use polite forms like 'por favor' (please) and 'obrigado/a' (thank you). Even when expressing dissatisfaction, maintaining a polite tone is important for a smooth interaction. Avoid overly direct or demanding language.

cultural_notes.title: 'Pois Não' - A Versatile Response
cultural_notes.body: In Portugal, 'Pois não' is a very common and versatile phrase, especially in service contexts. It can mean 'Yes, how can I help you?' when answering the phone, or 'Of course' / 'Go ahead' when someone asks for permission or indicates they are about to do something for you. It conveys readiness and willingness to assist.

lines.speaker: Vendedor

lines.speaker: Cliente

lines.speaker: Vendedor

lines.speaker: Cliente

lines.speaker: Vendedor

lines.speaker: Cliente

cultural_notes.title: Market Etiquette in Madeira
cultural_notes.body: When shopping at a market stall in Madeira, it's common to greet the vendor ('Bom dia'/'Boa tarde') and ask about the freshness of produce ('Está fresco/a hoje?' or 'Estão frescos/as hoje?'). It's considered polite to ask the vendor to pick items for you rather than touching them yourself, especially for fruits and vegetables. Don't be afraid to ask for local specialties like 'semilhas' (potatoes) or 'peixe-espada' (black scabbard fish).

cultural_notes.title: Quantities and Payment
cultural_notes.body: Quantities are usually requested in 'quilos' (kilos), 'meio quilo' (half kilo), or 'dúzias' (dozens). For small items like a single avocado, you might ask for 'uma unidade' or 'um abacate'. After selecting your items, the vendor will usually ask 'Mais alguma coisa?' (Anything else?). When finished, say 'É tudo, obrigado/a' and ask 'Quanto é que fica?' to get the total. Cash is often preferred, but many stalls now accept card payments.

roleplay.scenario: You are at a fruit and vegetable stall in the market. You want to buy some fresh produce.

nodes.npc_text: Bom dia! Em que posso ajudar?
nodes.npc_translation: Good morning! How can I help?

options.translation: Good morning. I'd like a kilo of tomatoes, please.
options.feedback: Good start! You asked for a quantity of an item.

options.text: Olá. O que é que tem de fresco hoje?

options.feedback: Excellent! Asking about fresh produce is a great way to start.

nodes.npc_text: Um quilo de tomates, claro. São estes, estão ótimos. Mais alguma coisa?
nodes.npc_translation: A kilo of tomatoes, of course. These ones, they're great. Anything else?

options.text: Sim, queria também uma dúzia de ovos.
options.translation: Yes, I'd also like a dozen eggs.
options.feedback: Well done! You added another item with a different quantity.

options.text: Não, é tudo. Quanto é que fica?
options.translation: No, that's all. How much is it?
options.feedback: Perfect! You're ready to pay.

nodes.npc_text: Hoje temos umas bananas da Madeira fresquíssimas e semilhas acabadas de chegar. O que prefere?
nodes.npc_translation: Today we have very fresh Madeiran bananas and potatoes that just arrived. What do you prefer?

options.text: Vou levar meio quilo de bananas.
options.translation: I'll take half a kilo of bananas.
options.feedback: Good choice! You specified a quantity for a fresh item.

options.text: Quanto custa o quilo das semilhas?
options.translation: How much does a kilo of potatoes cost?
options.feedback: Great question! Always good to know the price.

nodes.npc_text: Uma dúzia de ovos. É tudo, então?
nodes.npc_translation: A dozen eggs. Is that all, then?

options.text: Sim, é tudo. Quanto é que fica?
options.translation: Yes, that's all. How much is it?
options.feedback: Excellent, ready to finalize the purchase.

nodes.npc_text: Meio quilo de bananas. Mais alguma coisa que precise?
nodes.npc_translation: Half a kilo of bananas. Anything else you need?

options.text: Não, obrigado/a. Quanto é?
options.translation: No, thank you. How much is it?
options.feedback: Good, you're moving to payment.

nodes.npc_text: As semilhas estão a um euro e vinte o quilo. Quer levar algumas?
nodes.npc_translation: The potatoes are one euro twenty a kilo. Do you want to take some?

options.text: Sim, queria um quilo, por favor.
options.translation: Yes, I'd like a kilo, please.
options.feedback: Good, you specified the quantity after asking the price.

options.text: Não, obrigado/a. Fico só com as bananas.
options.translation: No, thank you. I'll just take the bananas.
options.feedback: That's fine, you can decline politely.

nodes.npc_text: Um quilo de semilhas. É tudo?
nodes.npc_translation: A kilo of potatoes. Is that all?

options.text: Sim, é tudo. Quanto é que fica?
options.translation: Yes, that's all. How much is it?
options.feedback: Perfect, ready to pay.

nodes.npc_text: Certo, só as bananas então. Fica um euro e cinquenta.
nodes.npc_translation: Okay, just the bananas then. That's one euro fifty.

options.text: Aqui tem. Obrigado/a!
options.translation: Here you go. Thank you!
options.feedback: Great! You completed the transaction.

nodes.npc_text: Deixe ver... Fica cinco euros e vinte e cinco. Aceitamos MB Way ou dinheiro.
nodes.npc_translation: Let me see... That's five euros and twenty-five. We accept MB Way or cash.

options.text: Vou pagar em dinheiro.
options.translation: I'll pay in cash.
options.feedback: Good, you specified your payment method.

options.text: Posso pagar com cartão?
options.translation: Can I pay by card?
options.feedback: Good to check payment options.

nodes.npc_text: Sim, claro. Pode encostar o cartão aqui.
nodes.npc_translation: Yes, of course. You can tap your card here.

options.text: Obrigado/a! Tenha um bom dia.
options.translation: Thank you! Have a good day.
options.feedback: Polite ending to the transaction.

nodes.npc_text: Obrigado/a! Aqui está o seu troco.
nodes.npc_translation: Thank you! Here's your change.

options.text: Obrigado/a! Até à próxima.
options.translation: Thank you! See you next time.
options.feedback: Perfect! You completed the purchase.

cultural_notes.title: Politeness at the CTT (Post Office)
cultural_notes.body: When interacting with staff at the CTT, it's customary to start with a polite greeting like 'Bom dia' (Good morning) or 'Boa tarde' (Good afternoon). Always use 'por favor' (please) when making a request and 'obrigado/a' (thank you) afterwards. For politeness with staff, it's common to use the third person singular (e.g., 'Queria...' instead of 'Eu quero...') or address them as 'o senhor' / 'a senhora' (e.g., 'Pode ajudar-me, o senhor?'). Avoid using 'você' in these contexts, as it can sound too direct or even distancing in Portugal.

cultural_notes.title: Understanding 'Aviso de Receção' and 'Aviso de Tentativa de Entrega'
cultural_notes.body: An 'aviso de receção' is a notice you receive when a registered letter or parcel requires your signature upon collection. You must present this notice along with a valid form of identification (like your 'cartão de cidadão' - ID card) at the CTT to collect your item. An 'aviso de tentativa de entrega' means a courier or postal worker tried to deliver a parcel but you weren't home. This note will usually provide instructions on how to arrange a redelivery or where to pick up the parcel (often at a local CTT office or courier depot) and will include a tracking number.

roleplay.scenario: You received an 'aviso de tentativa de entrega' (missed delivery note) for a parcel. You are calling the courier service to arrange a new delivery.

nodes.npc_text: Transportadora X, boa tarde. Em que posso ajudar?
nodes.npc_translation: Courier X, good afternoon. How can I help?

options.text: Boa tarde. Recebi um aviso de tentativa de entrega.
options.translation: Good afternoon. I received a missed delivery note.
options.feedback: Good start! You clearly state the reason for your call.

options.text: Olá. Tenho um problema com uma encomenda.
options.translation: Hello. I have a problem with a parcel.
options.feedback: This is a bit too vague. Try to be more specific about why you're calling.

nodes.npc_text: Pois. Pode dar-me o número do aviso, por favor?
nodes.npc_translation: Right. Can you give me the notice number, please?

options.text: Sim, claro. É o número 123456789.
options.translation: Yes, of course. It's number 123456789.
options.feedback: Excellent! You provided the requested information politely.

options.text: Não sei. Onde é que está o número?
options.translation: I don't know. Where is the number?
options.feedback: The number is usually clearly printed on the delivery note. Try to find it before calling.

nodes.npc_text: Obrigado. Vejo aqui que a entrega não foi possível. Quer que tentemos outra vez ou prefere levantar a encomenda?
nodes.npc_translation: Thank you. I see here that delivery wasn't possible. Do you want us to try again or do you prefer to pick up the parcel?

options.text: Podem tentar entregar outra vez, por favor?
options.translation: Can you try to deliver again, please?
options.feedback: Good choice! You're asking for a redelivery.

options.text: Prefiro levantar a encomenda. Onde é que posso ir?
options.translation: I prefer to pick up the parcel. Where can I go?
options.feedback: Good choice! You're asking where to collect it.

nodes.npc_text: Claro. Em que dia e período é melhor para si? Manhã ou tarde?
nodes.npc_translation: Of course. What day and period is best for you? Morning or afternoon?

nodes.npc_text: Pode levantar a encomenda no nosso armazém, na zona industrial, a partir de amanhã. Precisa do aviso e do seu documento.
nodes.npc_translation: You can pick up the parcel at our warehouse, in the industrial zone, starting tomorrow. You need the notice and your ID.

nodes.npc_text: Desculpe, não consigo ajudar sem mais informações. Pode verificar o aviso?
nodes.npc_translation: Sorry, I can't help without more information. Can you check the notice?

situations.title: Booking Appointments by Phone
situations.summary: Phoning to book, confirm or move an appointment — doctor, hairdresser, mechanic: dates, times, spelling your name, and confirming details you did not catch.

phrase_patterns.base: Gostaria de marcar um/uma {tipo_compromisso}, por favor.
phrase_patterns.translation: I would like to book a {type_of_appointment}, please.

variants.text: Não percebi. Pode dizer de novo?
variants.translation: I didn't understand. Can you say it again?

dialogues.title: Booking a Doctor's Appointment
dialogues.context: A person is calling a medical clinic in Funchal to book an appointment with their doctor.

lines.speaker: Rececionista
lines.text: Clínica da Saúde, diga!
lines.translation: Health Clinic, speak!

lines.speaker: Cliente
lines.text: Boa tarde. Gostaria de marcar uma consulta com o Dr. Santos, por favor.
lines.translation: Good afternoon. I would like to book an appointment with Dr. Santos, please.

lines.speaker: Rececionista
lines.text: Boa tarde. Para quando seria? O Dr. Santos está disponível na próxima terça-feira.
lines.translation: Good afternoon. For when would it be? Dr. Santos is available next Tuesday.

lines.speaker: Cliente
lines.text: Terça-feira? Hmm... É possível para quinta-feira, dia vinte e três?
lines.translation: Tuesday? Hmm... Is it possible for Thursday, the twenty-third?

lines.speaker: Rececionista
lines.text: Dia vinte e três... sim, às dez da manhã. Serve-lhe?
lines.translation: The twenty-third... yes, at ten in the morning. Does that suit you?

lines.speaker: Cliente
lines.text: Sim, perfeito! O meu nome é Ana Costa.
lines.translation: Yes, perfect! My name is Ana Costa.

lines.speaker: Rececionista
lines.text: Ana Costa. Certo. Consulta marcada para quinta-feira, dia vinte e três, às dez da manhã. Obrigada!
lines.translation: Ana Costa. Right. Appointment booked for Thursday, the twenty-third, at ten in the morning. Thank you!

lines.speaker: Cliente
lines.text: Obrigada eu. Até lá!
lines.translation: Thank you. See you then!

cultural_notes.title: Phone Greetings in Portugal
cultural_notes.body: When answering the phone in Portugal, especially in a professional context, you'll often hear 'Diga!' (Speak!) or 'Está?' (Are you there?). As a caller, a simple 'Bom dia/Boa tarde' is appropriate. It's polite to state your purpose clearly and concisely.

cultural_notes.title: Spelling Names and Confirming Details
cultural_notes.body: It's common to be asked to spell your name, especially if it's not a very common Portuguese name. Don't be afraid to ask the other person to repeat or spell information if you don't catch it the first time. Phrases like 'Pode repetir, por favor?' (Can you repeat, please?) or 'Não percebi, pode soletrar?' (I didn't understand, can you spell it?) are very useful.

roleplay.scenario: You are calling a hairdresser in Funchal to book a haircut. You need to find an available time, give your name, and confirm the details.

nodes.npc_text: Bom dia, Cabeleireiro Estilo. Diga!
nodes.npc_translation: Good morning, Style Hairdresser. Speak!

options.text: Bom dia. Gostaria de marcar um corte de cabelo, por favor.
options.translation: Good morning. I would like to book a haircut, please.
options.feedback: Excellent start! Clear and polite.

nodes.npc_text: Certo. Para quando gostaria? Temos disponibilidade na próxima semana, a partir de terça-feira.
nodes.npc_translation: Right. For when would you like it? We have availability next week, starting from Tuesday.

options.text: É possível para quarta-feira, às três da tarde?
options.translation: Is it possible for Wednesday, at three in the afternoon?
options.feedback: Good, you're suggesting a specific day and time.

options.text: Que horas têm na terça-feira?
options.translation: What times do you have on Tuesday?
options.feedback: Asking for options is a good strategy.

nodes.npc_text: Quarta-feira às três... sim, está livre. Qual é o seu nome, por favor?
nodes.npc_translation: Wednesday at three... yes, it's free. What's your name, please?

options.text: O meu nome é Sofia Santos.
options.translation: My name is Sofia Santos.
options.feedback: Perfect, giving your full name clearly.

nodes.npc_text: Na terça-feira temos às dez da manhã ou às quatro da tarde. Qual prefere?
nodes.npc_translation: On Tuesday we have ten in the morning or four in the afternoon. Which do you prefer?

options.text: Às quatro da tarde, por favor. O meu nome é Carlos Pereira.
options.translation: At four in the afternoon, please. My name is Carlos Pereira.
options.feedback: Good choice and clear name.

nodes.npc_text: Sofia Santos / Carlos Pereira, certo. Pode soletrar o apelido, por favor?
nodes.npc_translation: Sofia Santos / Carlos Pereira, right. Can you spell the surname, please?

options.text: Claro. S-A-N-T-O-S. / P-E-R-E-I-R-A.
options.translation: Of course. S-A-N-T-O-S. / P-E-R-E-I-R-A.
options.feedback: Excellent, spelling it out clearly.

nodes.npc_text: Obrigada. Então, está marcada a sua visita para quarta-feira, dia vinte e três, às três da tarde. / terça-feira, dia vinte e dois, às quatro da tarde. Certo?
nodes.npc_translation: Thank you. So, your visit is booked for Wednesday, the twenty-third, at three in the afternoon. / Tuesday, the twenty-second, at four in the afternoon. Correct?

options.text: Sim, está certo. Muito obrigada!
options.translation: Yes, that's correct. Thank you very much!
options.feedback: Great job confirming the details and ending politely!

options.text: Não, desculpe. Pensei que fosse às duas da tarde.
options.translation: No, sorry. I thought it was at two in the afternoon.
options.feedback: Good, correcting a misunderstanding is important. The NPC would likely offer to check availability again.

Practice spelling your name and a few common Portuguese names.
Mentally prepare to ask for repetition if you don't understand.
fallback_phrases: Pode falar mais devagar, por favor?

goals: Initiate a phone call to book an appointment.
State your desired service and preferred time/date.
Provide your name and spell it if requested.
Confirm the appointment details.

review_items.prompt: Say in Portuguese: I would like to book a {type_of_appointment}, please.
review_items.answer: Gostaria de marcar um/uma {tipo_compromisso}, por favor.

Say in Portuguese: Can you spell it, please?
Say in Portuguese: My name is {full_name}.

situations.title: Describing Problems at Home
situations.summary: Something at home is broken, leaking or not working: describing the problem clearly (não funciona, está avariado, está a pingar), asking for help and understanding simple instructions.

dialogues.title: Calling a Plumber
dialogues.context: A resident calls a local plumbing service in Madeira because their tap is leaking.

lines.speaker: Serviço de Canalização
lines.text: Serviço de Canalização, boa tarde. Diga!
lines.translation: Plumbing Service, good afternoon. Speak!

lines.speaker: Cliente
lines.text: Boa tarde. A minha torneira da cozinha está a pingar imenso. Precisava de ajuda.
lines.translation: Good afternoon. My kitchen tap is leaking a lot. I needed help.

lines.speaker: Serviço de Canalização
lines.text: Pois, a pingar, diz? É na água fria ou na quente?
lines.translation: Right, leaking, you say? Is it the cold or hot water?

lines.speaker: Cliente
lines.text: É na água fria. Não para de pingar. Já tentei fechar bem, mas nada.
lines.translation: It's the cold water. It won't stop dripping. I already tried to close it tightly, but nothing.

lines.speaker: Serviço de Canalização
lines.text: Certo. Podemos enviar um canalizador amanhã de manhã, entre as nove e as onze. Serve-lhe?
lines.translation: Okay. We can send a plumber tomorrow morning, between nine and eleven. Does that suit you?

lines.speaker: Cliente
lines.text: Sim, amanhã de manhã está ótimo. Muito obrigada!
lines.translation: Yes, tomorrow morning is great. Thank you very much!

lines.speaker: Serviço de Canalização
lines.text: De nada. Até amanhã.
lines.translation: You're welcome. See you tomorrow.

dialogues.title: Reporting a Power Cut
dialogues.context: A resident calls the electricity company about a power outage in their apartment.

lines.speaker: Empresa de Eletricidade
lines.text: Boa noite, apoio ao cliente. Qual é o problema?
lines.translation: Good evening, customer support. What's the problem?

lines.speaker: Cliente
lines.text: Boa noite. A luz do meu apartamento não está a funcionar. Está tudo escuro.
lines.translation: Good evening. The light in my apartment isn't working. It's all dark.

lines.speaker: Empresa de Eletricidade
lines.text: Não tem luz de todo, ou só em algumas divisões? Já verificou o quadro elétrico?
lines.translation: You have no light at all, or only in some rooms? Have you checked the electrical panel?

lines.speaker: Cliente
lines.text: Não, não tenho luz em lado nenhum. O quadro elétrico parece estar bem, não saltou nada.
lines.translation: No, I don't have light anywhere. The electrical panel seems fine, nothing tripped.

lines.speaker: Empresa de Eletricidade
lines.text: Pois. Pode ser uma avaria na rua. Qual é a sua morada, por favor?
lines.translation: Right. It could be a fault on the street. What's your address, please?

lines.speaker: Cliente
lines.text: É na Rua da Calçada, número vinte e cinco. No Funchal.
lines.translation: It's on Rua da Calçada, number twenty-five. In Funchal.

lines.speaker: Empresa de Eletricidade
lines.text: Ok, vamos enviar uma equipa. Pode demorar um bocado, mas vamos tentar resolver o mais rápido possível.
lines.translation: Okay, we'll send a team. It might take a while, but we'll try to resolve it as quickly as possible.

cultural_notes.title: Calling for Services in Portugal
cultural_notes.body: When calling for a service (plumber, electrician, etc.), it's common to start with a polite greeting like 'Bom dia/Boa tarde/Boa noite' and then state your problem directly. Using 'precisava de ajuda' (I needed help) or 'precisava que viesse ver' (I needed you to come see) is a polite way to express your need without sounding demanding. Expect a 'Diga!' or 'Pois!' as a common response from the service provider.

cultural_notes.title: Describing Problems Clearly
cultural_notes.body: Be specific but concise when describing the issue. Phrases like 'está a pingar' (it's dripping), 'não está a funcionar' (it's not working), or 'está avariado/a' (it's broken) are very common. If it's a leak, specifying 'água' or 'gás' is crucial. Service providers might ask follow-up questions like 'onde é?' (where is it?) or 'há quanto tempo?' (for how long?).

roleplay.scenario: You call a local handyman service in Funchal because your bathroom light isn't working.

nodes.npc_text: Serviço de Reparações, boa tarde. Diga!
nodes.npc_translation: Repair Service, good afternoon. Speak!

options.text: Boa tarde. A luz da minha casa de banho não está a funcionar.
options.translation: Good afternoon. My bathroom light isn't working.
options.feedback: Excellent start! You clearly stated the problem.

options.text: Olá. A luz está avariada.
options.translation: Hello. The light is broken.
options.feedback: Good, but 'não está a funcionar' is a bit more common for lights. Also, 'Boa tarde' is better than 'Olá' for a service call.

nodes.npc_text: Pois. A luz da casa de banho, diz. Já verificou o disjuntor?
nodes.npc_translation: Right. The bathroom light, you say. Have you checked the circuit breaker?

options.text: Sim, já vi o quadro elétrico e está tudo bem. Não saltou nada.
options.translation: Yes, I already checked the electrical panel and everything is fine. Nothing tripped.
options.feedback: Perfect! You confirmed you checked and nothing is wrong with the breaker.

options.text: Não sei o que é um disjuntor.
options.translation: I don't know what a circuit breaker is.
options.feedback: Honest, but they might ask you to check it. 'Quadro elétrico' is the common term for the panel.

nodes.npc_text: Certo. Nesse caso, precisamos de enviar um eletricista. Podemos ir amanhã de manhã, entre as dez e o meio-dia?
nodes.npc_translation: Okay. In that case, we need to send an electrician. Can we come tomorrow morning, between ten and noon?

options.text: Sim, amanhã de manhã está bem para mim. Obrigada!
options.translation: Yes, tomorrow morning is fine for me. Thank you!
options.feedback: Great, you accepted the appointment politely.

options.text: Não, amanhã de manhã não posso. Pode ser à tarde?
options.translation: No, I can't tomorrow morning. Can it be in the afternoon?
options.feedback: Good, you're negotiating the time. Remember to be polite.

nodes.npc_text: Ah, pois. O disjuntor é no quadro elétrico. Se não sabe, é melhor o eletricista ir lá ver. Podemos ir amanhã de manhã, entre as dez e o meio-dia?
nodes.npc_translation: Ah, I see. The circuit breaker is in the electrical panel. If you don't know, it's better for the electrician to go there and check. Can we come tomorrow morning, between ten and noon?

options.text: Sim, amanhã de manhã está bem. Muito obrigada.
options.translation: Yes, tomorrow morning is fine. Thank you very much.
options.feedback: Good, you accepted the appointment.

nodes.npc_text: À tarde, diz? Hum... Podemos tentar entre as duas e as quatro da tarde. Serve-lhe?
nodes.npc_translation: In the afternoon, you say? Hmm... We can try between two and four in the afternoon. Does that suit you?

options.text: Sim, isso está perfeito. Até amanhã.
options.translation: Yes, that's perfect. See you tomorrow.
options.feedback: Excellent, you successfully rescheduled and confirmed.

nodes.npc_text: Pronto. Então fica combinado. Até amanhã.
nodes.npc_translation: Alright. So it's settled. See you tomorrow.

Onde é que é o problema?

situations.title: Talking About Your Week
situations.summary: Telling a neighbour or friend what you did this week: sequencing past events with the preterite and setting the scene with the imperfect, reacting to their week in return.

phrase_patterns.base: Então, como foi a tua semana?

variants.text: Como é que foi a tua semana?

phrase_patterns.base: Eu {fui ao sítio} e {fiz a atividade}.
phrase_patterns.translation: I {went to the place} and {did the activity}.

vocabulary.note: Common interjection for agreement or acknowledgement.
Common in Portugal, often replaces 'um pouco'.

dialogues.title: A Week's Chat
dialogues.context: Two neighbours, Maria and João, meet by chance on the street and chat about their week.

lines.speaker: Maria
lines.text: Olá, João! Então, como foi a tua semana?
lines.translation: Hi, João! So, how was your week?

lines.speaker: João
lines.text: Olá, Maria! Foi um bocado ocupada, mas boa. Fui ao Funchal e estive a resolver uns papéis.
lines.translation: Hi, Maria! It was a bit busy, but good. I went to Funchal and was sorting out some paperwork.

lines.speaker: Maria
lines.text: Ah, pois é. E correu tudo bem?
lines.translation: Ah, that's right. Did everything go well?

lines.speaker: João
lines.text: Sim, correu. Enquanto estava lá, encontrei o teu primo, o Rui!
lines.translation: Yes, it did. While I was there, I ran into your cousin, Rui!

lines.speaker: Maria
lines.text: A sério? Que coincidência! A minha semana foi mais calma, estive a limpar a casa e a ir ao mercado.
lines.translation: Really? What a coincidence! My week was calmer, I was cleaning the house and going to the market.

lines.speaker: João
lines.text: Que bom! Assim aproveitas para descansar um bocado.
lines.translation: That's good! That way you can rest a bit.

cultural_notes.title: Informal Address with Neighbours
cultural_notes.body: In Madeira, as in most of Portugal, it's very common to use the informal 'tu' form when speaking with neighbours, friends, and people you know well. This creates a sense of familiarity and community. Using 'o senhor/a senhora' in this context might sound too formal or distant.

cultural_notes.title: Common Interjections
cultural_notes.body: Portuguese conversations are rich with interjections. 'Pois é' is frequently used to express agreement, acknowledgement, or to simply keep the conversation flowing. 'Então' can mean 'so', 'then', or act as a general conversational opener, often used when asking 'How are you?' or 'What's up?'

roleplay.scenario: You meet your neighbour, Dona Alice, outside your apartment building or house. She asks how your week was, and you tell her about a couple of things you did.

nodes.npc_text: Olá! Então, como foi a tua semana? Espero que tenha sido boa!
nodes.npc_translation: Hello! So, how was your week? I hope it was good!

options.text: Olá, Dona Alice! Foi boa, obrigado. Fui ao mercado e depois estive a cozinhar.
options.translation: Hello, Dona Alice! It was good, thank you. I went to the market and then I was cooking.
options.feedback: Great use of 'fui' (preterite) and 'estive a cozinhar' (imperfect for ongoing action).

options.text: Foi um bocado cansativa, Dona Alice. Tive muito que fazer.
options.translation: It was a bit tiring, Dona Alice. I had a lot to do.
options.feedback: Good use of 'cansativa' and 'tive' (preterite). Now she'll ask for more details.

nodes.npc_text: Ah, que bom! E encontraste coisas boas no mercado? Eu adoro ir lá.
nodes.npc_translation: Ah, that's good! And did you find good things at the market? I love going there.

options.text: Sim, encontrei fruta fresca e uns legumes ótimos. Enquanto estava lá, vi a tua filha!
options.translation: Yes, I found fresh fruit and some great vegetables. While I was there, I saw your daughter!
options.feedback: Excellent! You used 'encontrei' (preterite) and 'estava lá' (imperfect) correctly.

nodes.npc_text: Pois é, às vezes as semanas são assim. Mas conseguiste resolver tudo?
nodes.npc_translation: Yeah, sometimes weeks are like that. But did you manage to resolve everything?

options.text: Sim, consegui resolver quase tudo. Só falta uma coisa para amanhã.
options.translation: Yes, I managed to resolve almost everything. Only one thing left for tomorrow.
options.feedback: Well done using 'consegui resolver' (preterite).

nodes.npc_text: A sério? Que engraçado! Ela disse-me que ia lá. E o que é que ela estava a fazer?
nodes.npc_translation: Really? How funny! She told me she was going there. And what was she doing?

options.text: Ela estava a comprar flores, para a casa, acho eu.
options.translation: She was buying flowers, for the house, I think.
options.feedback: Perfect use of 'estava a comprar' (imperfect) for an ongoing action.

nodes.npc_text: Ah, está bem. Pois, as coisas nunca acabam! Mas agora é fim de semana, p'ra descansar.
nodes.npc_translation: Ah, okay. Well, things never end! But now it's the weekend, for resting.

options.text: É verdade! Bom fim de semana, Dona Alice!
options.translation: That's true! Have a good weekend, Dona Alice!
options.feedback: Polite and natural ending to the conversation.

mission.title: Share Your Week's Highlights
prep: Think about 2-3 things you did this week. One simple activity, and maybe one where something 'happened' while you were doing something else.
Practice describing these events using the preterite ('fui', 'fiz', 'encontrei') and the imperfect ('estava a fazer').
Prepare to ask your friend/neighbour about their week in return.
likely_responses: Pois é, as semanas são assim.
E o que é que fizeste?

goals: Describe past events using the preterite tense.
Set the scene and describe ongoing actions in the past using the imperfect tense.
React to a friend's or neighbour's description of their week.

review_items.prompt: Say in Portuguese: So, how was your week?
review_items.answer: Então, como foi a tua semana?

review_items.prompt: Say in Portuguese: My week was {adjective}.
Say in Portuguese: I {went to the place} and {did the activity}.
review_items.answer: Eu {fui ao sítio} e {fiz a atividade}.

review_items.prompt: Say in Portuguese: While {I was doing}, {this happened}.

Recall the Portuguese for: tiring
Recall the Portuguese for: fun

situations.title: Plans & Arrangements
situations.summary: Making, confirming and changing plans: suggesting times, agreeing where to meet, moving something to another day, and confirming by a short message.

variants.note: More polite way to ask about availability.
Adds a layer of politeness and indirectness.

vocabulary.note: Used to confirm an agreement.
General term for a scheduled get-together.
Commonly used for making appointments or reservations.
Used in phrases like 'não me dá jeito' (it's not convenient for me).

dialogues.title: Scheduling a plumber
dialogues.context: A customer calls a plumbing service to schedule a repair in their home.

lines.speaker: Serviço
lines.text: Boa tarde, serviço de canalização. Em que posso ajudar?
lines.translation: Good afternoon, plumbing service. How can I help you?

lines.speaker: Cliente
lines.text: Boa tarde. Gostaria de marcar um canalizador para vir cá a casa. Tenho uma torneira a pingar.
lines.translation: Good afternoon. I'd like to schedule a plumber to come to my house. I have a dripping tap.

lines.speaker: Serviço
lines.text: Claro. Temos disponibilidade para amanhã de manhã, entre as nove e o meio-dia. Que tal?
lines.translation: Of course. We have availability for tomorrow morning, between nine and noon. How does that sound?

lines.speaker: Cliente
lines.text: Amanhã de manhã não me dá jeito. Podemos mudar para depois de amanhã, à tarde? Ou na sexta-feira?
lines.translation: Tomorrow morning doesn't suit me. Can we change to the day after tomorrow, in the afternoon? Or on Friday?

lines.speaker: Serviço
lines.text: Pois, depois de amanhã, quinta-feira, temos um horário livre às três da tarde. Serve?
lines.translation: Well, the day after tomorrow, Thursday, we have a free slot at three in the afternoon. Does that work?

lines.speaker: Cliente
lines.text: Sim, três da tarde está perfeito. Combinado.
lines.translation: Yes, three in the afternoon is perfect. Agreed.

lines.speaker: Serviço
lines.text: Muito bem. Confirmo a sua marcação para quinta-feira, às três da tarde. Obrigado.
lines.translation: Very good. I confirm your appointment for Thursday, at three in the afternoon. Thank you.

dialogues.title: Friends making plans
dialogues.context: Two friends, Ana and Pedro, are discussing plans for the weekend.

lines.speaker: Ana
lines.text: Então, Pedro, queres ir à levada no sábado de manhã?
lines.translation: So, Pedro, do you want to go to the levada on Saturday morning?

lines.speaker: Pedro
lines.text: No sábado? Ui, não posso. Já tenho um compromisso. Podemos ir no domingo?
lines.translation: On Saturday? Oh, I can't. I already have an engagement. Can we go on Sunday?

lines.speaker: Ana
lines.text: Domingo? Hum, sim, domingo dá-me jeito. Que tal às nove da manhã, lá no início da levada?
lines.translation: Sunday? Hmm, yes, Sunday suits me. What about nine in the morning, there at the start of the levada?

lines.speaker: Pedro
lines.text: Pois, nove é um bocado cedo. Que tal às dez? Assim temos mais tempo para o pequeno-almoço.
lines.translation: Well, nine is a bit early. What about ten? That way we have more time for breakfast.

lines.speaker: Ana
lines.text: Combinado! Às dez, então. Confirmo por mensagem depois.
lines.translation: Agreed! At ten, then. I'll confirm by message later.

lines.speaker: Pedro
lines.text: P'ra já! Até domingo!
lines.translation: See you! Until Sunday!

cultural_notes.title: Politeness in scheduling
cultural_notes.body: When making or changing plans, especially with service providers or people you don't know well, it's common to use polite forms. Phrases like 'Gostaria de...' (I would like to...) or 'Será que podemos...?' (Could we...?) are preferred over direct commands. When suggesting a time, 'Que tal...?' (What about...?) is a common and polite way to propose. For refusing a time, 'Não me dá jeito' (It doesn't suit me/it's not convenient) is a soft refusal.

cultural_notes.title: Confirming arrangements
cultural_notes.body: In Portugal, it's very common to confirm appointments or social plans with a short message (often via WhatsApp) closer to the date or time. A simple 'Confirmo a nossa reunião para as 10h' or 'Combinado para amanhã' is sufficient and appreciated, especially for professional or semi-professional arrangements.

roleplay.scenario: You need to reschedule a delivery of a new appliance (e.g., a frigorífico) that was planned for tomorrow morning. You call the delivery company.

nodes.npc_text: Boa tarde, Serviço de Entregas. Em que posso ajudar?
nodes.npc_translation: Good afternoon, Delivery Service. How can I help you?

options.text: Boa tarde. Liguei para mudar a minha entrega de amanhã.
options.translation: Good afternoon. I called to change my delivery for tomorrow.
options.feedback: Good start! You clearly stated your intention.

options.text: Quero mudar a entrega.
options.translation: I want to change the delivery.
options.feedback: A bit direct, but acceptable. Adding 'Gostaria de...' would be more polite.

nodes.npc_text: Certo. Qual é o seu número de encomenda, por favor? E para quando gostaria de mudar?
nodes.npc_translation: Okay. What's your order number, please? And for when would you like to change it?

options.text: O número é 12345. Podemos mudar para depois de amanhã, à tarde?
options.translation: The number is 12345. Can we change to the day after tomorrow, in the afternoon?
options.feedback: Excellent! You provided the info and suggested a new time.

options.text: Não posso amanhã. Tem disponibilidade na próxima semana?
options.translation: I can't tomorrow. Do you have availability next week?
options.feedback: Good alternative, asking for their general availability.

nodes.npc_text: Depois de amanhã, quinta-feira, à tarde... Sim, temos um horário livre entre as duas e as cinco. Serve-lhe?
nodes.npc_translation: The day after tomorrow, Thursday, in the afternoon... Yes, we have a slot free between two and five. Does that suit you?

options.text: Sim, perfeito! Combinado.
options.translation: Yes, perfect! Agreed.
options.feedback: Great! Clear and concise confirmation.

options.text: Às duas e as cinco está bem.
options.translation: Between two and five is fine.
options.feedback: Good, but 'perfeito' or 'combinado' adds a nice touch of agreement.

nodes.npc_text: Na próxima semana, temos na terça-feira de manhã, entre as nove e o meio-dia. Que tal?
nodes.npc_translation: Next week, we have Tuesday morning, between nine and noon. How does that sound?

options.text: Terça-feira de manhã, sim, está bem. Obrigado.
options.translation: Tuesday morning, yes, that's fine. Thank you.
options.feedback: Perfect, you've accepted and thanked them.

options.text: Não, terça-feira não posso. Tem outro dia?
options.translation: No, I can't on Tuesday. Do you have another day?
options.feedback: You're asking for more options. Good persistence!

nodes.npc_text: Hum, na próxima semana, só na sexta-feira, à tarde. Serve?
nodes.npc_translation: Hmm, next week, only on Friday, in the afternoon. Does that work?

options.text: Sim, sexta-feira à tarde. Combinado.
options.translation: Yes, Friday afternoon. Agreed.
options.feedback: Excellent, you found a suitable time and confirmed.

nodes.npc_text: Muito bem. Confirmo a sua entrega para {DIA_FINAL}, às {HORA_FINAL}. Alguma questão?
nodes.npc_translation: Very good. I confirm your delivery for {FINAL_DAY}, at {FINAL_TIME}. Any questions?

options.text: Não, está tudo bem. Obrigado/a.
options.translation: No, everything is fine. Thank you.
options.feedback: The call ends successfully.

Think of at least two alternative days/times you could propose.
Practice how to politely refuse a time that doesn't suit you.

situations.title: Week 8 Stress Test
situations.summary: Mixed real-time exchanges combining the whole of month 2: market shopping, a phone booking, describing a household problem, and narrating your week — under light pressure.

vocabulary.note: Madeiran regionalism for 'batatas'
Common term for espresso in Portugal, especially Lisbon. 'Café' also works.

dialogues.title: At the Market
dialogues.context: A quick exchange at the local market in Funchal, buying vegetables.

lines.speaker: Vendedor
lines.text: Diga, minha senhora! Precisa de alguma coisa?
lines.translation: Tell me, madam! Do you need anything?

lines.speaker: Learner
lines.text: Sim, queria um quilo de semilhas, por favor.
lines.translation: Yes, I'd like a kilo of potatoes, please.

lines.speaker: Vendedor
lines.text: Pois não. Aqui está. E mais alguma coisa?
lines.translation: Of course. Here you go. Anything else?

lines.speaker: Learner
lines.text: Não, é só isso. Quanto é?
lines.translation: No, that's all. How much is it?

lines.speaker: Vendedor
lines.text: São dois euros e cinquenta, minha senhora.
lines.translation: It's two euros fifty, madam.

dialogues.title: Calling a Plumber
dialogues.context: The learner is calling a plumbing service about a leak at home.

lines.speaker: Atendente
lines.text: Serviços de Canalização da Madeira, boa tarde. Em que posso ajudar?
lines.translation: Madeira Plumbing Services, good afternoon. How can I help?

lines.speaker: Learner
lines.text: Boa tarde. Preciso de um canalizador. Tenho uma fuga de água na cozinha.
lines.translation: Good afternoon. I need a plumber. I have a water leak in the kitchen.

lines.speaker: Atendente
lines.text: Uma fuga de água, entendi. É urgente?
lines.translation: A water leak, I understand. Is it urgent?

lines.speaker: Learner
lines.text: Sim, é um bocado urgente. A água está a pingar bastante.
lines.translation: Yes, it's a bit urgent. The water is dripping quite a lot.

lines.speaker: Atendente
lines.text: Pois, claro. Podemos enviar alguém hoje à tarde, entre as três e as cinco. Serve-lhe?
lines.translation: Yes, of course. We can send someone this afternoon, between three and five. Does that suit you?

lines.speaker: Learner
lines.text: Sim, serve. Muito obrigada!
lines.translation: Yes, that suits me. Thank you very much!

cultural_notes.title: Directness in Service Interactions
cultural_notes.body: In Portugal, especially in service settings like shops or markets, interactions are often more direct than in some other cultures. It's common to state your need clearly and concisely, for example, 'Queria um quilo de semilhas' (I'd like a kilo of potatoes) rather than elaborate indirect requests. 'Diga!' (Tell me!) is a common, polite way for a vendor to initiate a conversation, inviting you to state your needs.

cultural_notes.title: The Importance of 'Pois'
cultural_notes.body: 'Pois' and 'Pois é' are very common interjections in European Portuguese. They often express agreement, acknowledgement, or a slight pause for thought. 'Pois não' is a polite way to say 'of course' or 'you're welcome' when someone asks for something. Using these naturally will make your Portuguese sound much more authentic.

roleplay.scenario: You are calling a repair service because your washing machine is broken. You need to describe the problem and arrange a visit.

nodes.npc_text: Boa tarde, Reparações Rápidas. Em que posso ajudar?
nodes.npc_translation: Good afternoon, Quick Repairs. How can I help?

options.text: Boa tarde. A minha máquina de lavar está avariada.
options.translation: Good afternoon. My washing machine is broken.
options.feedback: Excellent start! Clear and direct.

options.text: Olá. Preciso de ajuda com a máquina.
options.translation: Hello. I need help with the machine.
options.feedback: Good, but 'avariada' is more precise for 'broken'.

nodes.npc_text: Avariada, pois. Qual é o problema exatamente? Ela não liga, ou não lava?
nodes.npc_translation: Broken, I see. What's the problem exactly? It doesn't turn on, or it doesn't wash?

options.text: Ela liga, mas não está a lavar a roupa.
options.translation: It turns on, but it's not washing the clothes.
options.feedback: Perfect use of 'estar a + infinitive' for ongoing action.

options.text: Não lava a roupa.
options.translation: It doesn't wash the clothes.
options.feedback: Good, but adding 'Ela liga, mas...' gives more detail.

nodes.npc_text: Entendido. Precisamos de enviar um técnico. Pode ser amanhã de manhã, entre as nove e o meio-dia?
nodes.npc_translation: Understood. We need to send a technician. Can it be tomorrow morning, between nine and noon?

options.text: Sim, amanhã de manhã está bem. Muito obrigada.
options.translation: Yes, tomorrow morning is fine. Thank you very much.
options.feedback: Clear acceptance. You're almost done!

options.text: Amanhã de manhã? Sim, pode ser.
options.translation: Tomorrow morning? Yes, that can be.
options.feedback: A little less formal, but still good.

nodes.npc_text: P'ra já. Então, amanhã de manhã, entre as nove e o meio-dia. Qual é a sua morada, por favor?
nodes.npc_translation: You're welcome. So, tomorrow morning, between nine and noon. What is your address, please?

options.text: É a Rua das Flores, número 10.
options.translation: It's Rua das Flores, number 10.
options.feedback: Great, you've provided the necessary information and completed the call.

roleplay.scenario: You need to call a plumber because your kitchen sink is blocked. You need to explain the problem and arrange a time for them to come.

nodes.npc_text: Bom dia, canalizador Silva, diga!
nodes.npc_translation: Good morning, Plumber Silva, speak!

options.text: Bom dia. Chamo-me [o seu nome]. A minha banca da cozinha está entupida.
options.translation: Good morning. My name is [your name]. My kitchen sink is blocked.
options.feedback: Great start, clearly state your name and the issue.

options.text: Olá. Tenho um problema com a água.
options.translation: Hello. I have a problem with the water.
options.feedback: Good attempt, but be more specific about the problem and introduce yourself.

nodes.npc_text: Um problema com a água? Pode ser mais específico, por favor? E como se chama?

options.text: Sim, a banca da cozinha está entupida. Chamo-me [o seu nome].

nodes.npc_text: Ah, a banca entupida. Pois, é um problema comum. Onde é a sua morada?

options.text: É na Rua [nome da rua], número dezasseis.
options.translation: It's on [street name] Street, number sixteen.
options.feedback: Excellent, providing the full address is essential.

options.text: Estou em casa de manhã, às sete.

nodes.npc_text: Às sete da manhã é um bocado cedo. Pode ser amanhã às nove e meia?

nodes.npc_text: Amanhã, depois das catorze horas. Está bem. Eu vou lá. Obrigado.

options.text: Obrigado eu. Até amanhã.

options.feedback: Excellent, you've successfully arranged the visit!

cultural_notes.title: Politeness in Service Interactions
cultural_notes.body: In Portugal, politeness is highly valued in service interactions. Always use 'por favor' or 'se faz favor' when asking for something. When addressing service staff or officials, 'o senhor'/'a senhora' (sir/madam) is generally preferred over 'você' to show respect, especially if you don't know the person. A simple 'bom dia' or 'boa tarde' to start the conversation is also crucial.

cultural_notes.title: The Versatility of 'Pois'
cultural_notes.body: 'Pois' and 'pois é' are very common interjections in European Portuguese, especially in Madeira. They can mean 'yes', 'indeed', 'that's right', 'of course', or simply acknowledge what the other person has said. It's a natural filler that helps conversations flow and shows you are listening, but it's not a direct translation of 'yes' in all contexts.

roleplay.scenario: You are calling a local handyman to fix a leaking tap in your kitchen. You need to explain the problem and arrange a time.

options.feedback: You got straight to the point, but 'queria marcar uma reparação' is more direct for scheduling.

nodes.npc_text: Claro. Qual é o problema?
nodes.npc_translation: Of course. What's the problem?

options.feedback: Excellent, you used 'estava' for a past ongoing state.

options.text: A torneira da cozinha pingou e continua a pingar.
options.translation: The kitchen tap dripped and continues to drip.
options.feedback: Good, but 'já estava assim há uns dias' sounds more natural for an ongoing issue.

nodes.npc_text: Percebo. Quando estaria disponível para eu ir lá ver?

options.text: Eu estaria em casa amanhã de manhã.

options.feedback: Perfect use of the conditional for hypothetical availability.

options.text: Posso estar em casa amanhã de manhã.

nodes.npc_text: Amanhã de manhã, então. Estive a pensar que talvez às 9h seria bom. Serve-lhe?
nodes.npc_translation: Tomorrow morning, then. I was thinking that maybe 9 am would be good. Does that suit you?

cultural_notes.title: The Versatility of 'Pois'
cultural_notes.body: 'Pois' and 'pois é' are extremely common interjections in European Portuguese, especially in Madeira. They can mean 'yes', 'indeed', 'that's right', 'of course', or simply acknowledge what the other person said, often showing agreement or understanding. It's a natural filler that helps conversations flow.

cultural_notes.title: Tense for Hypotheticals: The Conditional
cultural_notes.body: The conditional tense ('estaria', 'gostaria') is frequently used in Portuguese for polite requests, hypothetical situations, or to express what 'would' happen. It's a key tense for sounding natural when making plans or discussing possibilities, as seen in the roleplay when asking about availability.

roleplay.scenario: You are at a local café in Câmara de Lobos. You need to order a coffee from the barista, and then you see an old friend, Miguel, at another table.

nodes.npc_text: Bom dia! Em que posso ajudar?
nodes.npc_translation: Good morning! How can I help?

options.text: Bom dia, queria uma bica, por favor.
options.translation: Good morning, I'd like an espresso, please.
options.feedback: Correct! You used the polite 'queria' and 'bica' (espresso in Portugal).

options.text: Olá! Dá-me um café!
options.translation: Hi! Give me a coffee!
options.feedback: This is too informal and direct for a service interaction. Try to be more polite.

nodes.npc_text: Pois, aqui está. Mais alguma coisa? Ah, o Miguel está ali, se o quiser cumprimentar.
nodes.npc_translation: Here you go. Anything else? Oh, Miguel is over there, if you want to greet him.

options.text: Obrigado! Olá Miguel! Como estás?
options.translation: Thanks! Hi Miguel! How are you?
options.feedback: Excellent! You switched to the informal 'tu' for your friend.

options.text: Obrigado! Olá Miguel! Como está?
options.translation: Thanks! Hi Miguel! How are you?
options.feedback: Miguel is your old friend. 'Como está?' is too formal here. Try using 'tu'.

nodes.npc_text: Olá! Tudo bem, e tu? Há quanto tempo!
nodes.npc_translation: Hi! All good, and you? Long time no see!

options.text: Pois é! Tudo ótimo, obrigado!
options.translation: That's right! All great, thanks!
options.feedback: Perfect! You've navigated both registers successfully.

mission.title: Order a coffee and greet a friend
prep: Practice ordering a coffee politely using 'queria' and 'por favor'.
Rehearse informal greetings like 'Olá, como estás?' for friends.
Think about when to use 'o senhor/a senhora' (for service people) and 'tu' (for friends).

cultural_notes.title: The Nuance of 'Você' in Portugal
cultural_notes.body: While 'você' technically means 'you (formal)', in Portugal, it's often avoided in direct address. Using 'o senhor' (for men) or 'a senhora' (for women) is the standard polite way to address strangers, elders, or people in service roles. 'Você' can sometimes sound distancing or even slightly impolite, especially if used with someone you don't know well. Stick to 'o senhor/a senhora' for politeness, or simply use the third person verb conjugation without the pronoun (e.g., 'Como está?' instead of 'Como está você?').

cultural_notes.title: Plural Address: 'Vocês' vs. 'Os senhores/As senhoras'
cultural_notes.body: When addressing a group, 'vocês' is the common plural 'you'. It's generally neutral and can be used in most situations. However, in very formal contexts, or when addressing a group of respected individuals, 'os senhores' (for men or mixed groups) or 'as senhoras' (for women) might be used for heightened politeness, though this is less common in everyday interactions compared to the singular forms.

nodes.npc_translation: Right. Is it a regular cleaning or a deeper cleaning? And what's the address?

options.text: É uma limpeza regular, na Rua da Levada, número dez.
options.translation: It's a regular cleaning, on Levada Street, number ten.
options.feedback: Excellent, you provided all the requested information.

options.text: É uma limpeza profunda, no Funchal.
options.translation: It's a deep cleaning, in Funchal.
options.feedback: You specified the type of cleaning and location. Good.

options.text: Não percebi. Pode repetir?
options.translation: I didn't understand. Can you repeat?
options.feedback: It's always good to ask for clarification if you don't understand.

nodes.npc_text: Os preços dependem do tipo de limpeza e do tamanho do apartamento. Quantos quartos tem?
nodes.npc_translation: Prices depend on the type of cleaning and the size of the apartment. How many bedrooms does it have?

options.text: É um T2, dois quartos.
options.translation: It's a two-bedroom apartment, two bedrooms.
options.feedback: Good, 'T2' is a common way to refer to a two-bedroom apartment.

options.text: Não sei. Pode dar-me uma estimativa?
options.translation: I don't know. Can you give me an estimate?
options.feedback: Asking for an estimate is a good way to get an idea of the cost.

nodes.npc_text: Para um T2, uma limpeza regular custa cerca de quarenta euros. Uma limpeza profunda é mais cara.
nodes.npc_translation: For a two-bedroom apartment, a regular cleaning costs about forty euros. A deep cleaning is more expensive.

options.text: Certo, quero marcar uma limpeza regular.
options.translation: Okay, I want to book a regular cleaning.
options.feedback: You've decided on the service and are ready to book.

options.text: Obrigado/a, vou pensar e ligo mais tarde.
options.translation: Thank you, I'll think about it and call later.
options.feedback: It's fine to take time to decide. Remember to say 'obrigado' if you're male, 'obrigada' if female.

nodes.npc_text: Muito bem. Temos disponibilidade para a próxima terça-feira de manhã, às nove. Serve-lhe?
nodes.npc_translation: Very well. We have availability for next Tuesday morning, at nine. Does that suit you?

options.text: Sim, terça-feira às nove está bem.
options.translation: Yes, Tuesday at nine is fine.
options.feedback: You confirmed the appointment time.

options.text: Não, prefiro na quarta-feira.
options.translation: No, I prefer Wednesday.
options.feedback: It's good to state your preference if the first offer doesn't work.

nodes.npc_text: Quarta-feira, sim. Às dez da manhã?
nodes.npc_translation: Wednesday, yes. At ten in the morning?

options.text: Sim, perfeito.
options.translation: Yes, perfect.
options.feedback: Perfect! You've found a suitable time.

options.text: Não, prefiro à tarde.
options.translation: No, I prefer in the afternoon.
options.feedback: Keep negotiating until you find a time that works for you.

nodes.npc_text: À tarde na quarta-feira, temos às duas. É a nossa última vaga para essa semana.
nodes.npc_translation: Wednesday afternoon, we have at two. It's our last slot for that week.

options.text: Está ótimo, às duas na quarta.
options.translation: That's great, at two on Wednesday.
options.feedback: You've accepted the final offer. Good job!

options.text: Não, obrigado/a. Vou procurar noutro sítio.
options.translation: No, thank you. I'll look elsewhere.
options.feedback: It's okay to decline if the options don't suit you.

nodes.npc_text: Confirmado então! A limpeza será na sua morada. Alguma questão?
nodes.npc_translation: Confirmed then! The cleaning will be at your address. Any questions?

options.text: Não, está tudo bem. Obrigado/a.
options.translation: No, everything is fine. Thank you.
options.feedback: You've successfully booked the service!

options.text: Quanto tempo demora a limpeza?
options.translation: How long does the cleaning take?
options.feedback: It's good to ask about the duration.

nodes.npc_text: Para uma limpeza regular de um T2, cerca de duas a três horas.
nodes.npc_translation: For a regular cleaning of a two-bedroom apartment, about two to three hours.

options.text: Certo. Muito obrigado/a!
options.translation: Okay. Thank you very much!
options.feedback: You have all the information you need. Well done!

mission.title: Scheduling a Cleaning Service
prep: Practice asking for a cleaning service for your apartment.
Be ready to provide your address and preferred time/day.
Ask about the cost of the service.
likely_responses: Quando é que gostaria da limpeza?
É uma limpeza regular ou profunda?

cultural_notes.title: Answering the Phone in Portugal
cultural_notes.body: When answering the phone in Portugal, especially for businesses, you'll often hear 'Diga!' (Speak!) or 'Estou!' (I am!). As a caller, you typically start with 'Bom dia'/'Boa tarde' and state your purpose directly, often using a polite address like 'o senhor' or 'a senhora' if you don't know the person.

cultural_notes.title: Politeness with Service Workers
cultural_notes.body: When speaking to service workers (e.g., cleaners, plumbers, shop assistants), it is common and polite to use 'o senhor' / 'a senhora' even if they don't use it with you. This shows respect and maintains a formal-polite register. Avoid 'você' as a default polite address in these contexts, as it can sometimes sound distancing or overly direct in Portugal.
