situations.title: Making a Complaint Politely
situations.summary: Raising a problem — a wrong bill, poor service, a mistake — firmly but politely, the Portuguese indirect way.

phrase_patterns.base: Desculpe, mas acho que há um engano com {item}.
phrase_patterns.translation: Excuse me, but I think there's a mistake with {item}.

variants.text: Com licença, acho que houve um engano com {item}.
variants.translation: Excuse me, I think there was a mistake with {item}.

phrase_patterns.base: Gostaria de falar sobre {problema}, se faz favor.
phrase_patterns.translation: I would like to talk about {problem}, please.

slots.name: problema
options: um problema com o meu pedido

variants.translation: I wanted to talk about {problem}, please.
variants.note: Slightly less formal, 'queria' is common for polite requests.

phrase_patterns.base: Será que me pode ajudar com {questão}?

slots.name: detalhe
slots.description: The detail that is not understood
options: porque é que isto está aqui

dialogues.title: Restaurant Bill Mistake
dialogues.context: At a restaurant in Funchal, after a meal, the customer notices an extra item on the bill.

lines.speaker: Cliente
lines.text: Desculpe, o senhor podia vir aqui um momento, se faz favor?
lines.translation: Excuse me, could you come here for a moment, please?

lines.speaker: Empregado
lines.text: Pois não, diga. Há algum problema?
lines.translation: Yes, what can I do for you? Is there a problem?

lines.speaker: Cliente
lines.text: Acho que há um engano na fatura. Não me parece que tenhamos pedido esta poncha extra.
lines.translation: I think there's a mistake on the bill. I don't think we ordered this extra poncha.

lines.speaker: Empregado
lines.text: Ah, peço desculpa. Deixe-me ver. Pois é, parece que foi um erro nosso. Vou já corrigir a fatura.
lines.translation: Ah, I apologize. Let me see. Yes, it seems it was our mistake. I'll correct the bill right away.

lines.speaker: Cliente
lines.text: Muito obrigado, agradeço imenso.
lines.translation: Thank you very much, I appreciate it immensely.

cultural_notes.title: The Art of Polite Complaint in Portugal
cultural_notes.body: In Portugal, direct confrontation is generally avoided, especially in service interactions. When making a complaint, it's best to start with polite phrases like 'Desculpe' (Excuse me) or 'Com licença' (With your permission), followed by an indirect phrasing like 'Acho que há um engano...' (I think there's a mistake...) or 'Não estou a perceber...' (I'm not understanding...). Frame it as a question or an observation, rather than an accusation. Using the conditional ('poderia', 'gostaria') or imperfect ('queria') tenses makes requests softer and more polite. Maintaining a calm and respectful tone is key to achieving a positive outcome.

cultural_notes.title: Addressing Service Staff
cultural_notes.body: When addressing service staff, it's common to use 'o senhor' / 'a senhora' (the sir / the madam) as a polite form of address, especially if you don't know the person. This is more common and less distancing than 'você'. For example, 'O senhor podia ajudar-me?' (Could you help me, sir?) or 'A senhora sabe dizer-me...?' (Do you know how to tell me..., madam?). This shows respect and facilitates a good interaction, even when raising an issue.

roleplay.scenario: You've just received a delivery from a local grocery store in Madeira, but one of the items you ordered, a specific type of Madeiran banana, is missing from the bag, although it's on the receipt. You need to call the store to sort it out.

nodes.npc_text: Bom dia, Loja da Esquina, como posso ajudar?
nodes.npc_translation: Good morning, Corner Shop, how can I help?

options.text: Bom dia. Desculpe, mas acho que há um engano com a minha entrega.
options.translation: Good morning. Excuse me, but I think there's a mistake with my delivery.
options.feedback: Excellent start! You were polite and clear about the issue.

options.text: Olá. Recebi a minha encomenda e falta uma coisa.
options.translation: Hello. I received my order and something is missing.
options.feedback: A bit direct, but still understandable. Try to be a little more indirect next time.

nodes.npc_text: Pois não, diga. Qual é o problema com a entrega? Tem o número do pedido?
nodes.npc_translation: Yes, what can I do for you? What's the problem with the delivery? Do you have the order number?

options.text: Sim, o número é 12345. Pelo que vejo na fatura, paguei pelas bananas da Madeira, mas elas não vieram.
options.translation: Yes, the number is 12345. From what I see on the bill, I paid for the Madeiran bananas, but they didn't come.
options.feedback: Very good! You provided the necessary information and stated the problem clearly and politely.

options.text: Falta-me um cacho de bananas da Madeira. O meu número é 12345.
options.translation: I'm missing a bunch of Madeiran bananas. My number is 12345.
options.feedback: Clear and concise. Adding 'por favor' or a softer opening could make it even better.

nodes.npc_text: Certo. E o que é que falta, exatamente? Tem o número da sua encomenda?
nodes.npc_translation: Okay. And what exactly is missing? Do you have your order number?

options.text: Sim, o número é 12345. Não recebi as bananas da Madeira, mas estão na fatura.
options.translation: Yes, the number is 12345. I didn't receive the Madeiran bananas, but they are on the bill.
options.feedback: You clearly stated the problem and provided the order number. Good job.

nodes.npc_text: Ah, peço imensa desculpa por isso! Vou verificar aqui. Pois é, parece que houve um lapso. Quer que enviemos as bananas ou prefere que descontemos o valor na próxima compra?
nodes.npc_translation: Ah, I am so sorry about that! I'll check here. Yes, it seems there was an oversight. Would you like us to send the bananas or would you prefer us to discount the amount on your next purchase?

options.text: Se não for muito incómodo, preferia que enviassem as bananas, se faz favor.
options.translation: If it's not too much trouble, I'd prefer if you sent the bananas, please.
options.feedback: Polite and clear preference. Using 'se não for muito incómodo' is very good.

options.text: Acho que prefiro que enviem as bananas, sim.
options.translation: I think I prefer that you send the bananas, yes.
options.feedback: Clear choice. A little more politeness could be added, but still acceptable.

nodes.npc_text: Certo, vamos enviar as bananas o mais rapidamente possível. Devem chegar dentro de uma hora. Mais uma vez, pedimos desculpa pelo sucedido.
nodes.npc_translation: Okay, we'll send the bananas as quickly as possible. They should arrive within an hour. Once again, we apologize for what happened.

options.text: Muito obrigado pela sua ajuda. Tenha um bom dia.
options.translation: Thank you very much for your help. Have a good day.
options.feedback: Perfect ending! You thanked them and ended the call politely.

situations.title: Negotiating & Arranging
situations.summary: Agreeing prices, times and terms: getting a tradesperson to commit, pushing back on a quote, settling on a plan.

phrase_patterns.translation: Can you do the work by {date}?

slots.name: valor
slots.description: the amount or concept of cost

variants.text: É um bocado caro para mim.
variants.translation: It's a bit expensive for me.
variants.note: Common way to express something is too expensive without being too direct.

variants.translation: Can you make a better price?

vocabulary.note: Can refer to a written quote for services or a personal budget.
Often used for builders, but can be general for someone hired to do work.

vocabulary.translation: we have an agreement / it's settled

vocabulary.note: Common in Portugal, especially Madeira, for 'a little'.

dialogues.title: Plumber Quote
dialogues.context: A client is calling a plumber to fix a leaky tap and discuss the cost and timing for the repair.

lines.speaker: Cliente
lines.text: Bom dia. É da canalização, certo? Tenho uma torneira a pingar na cozinha.
lines.translation: Good morning. Is this the plumbing service, right? I have a tap dripping in the kitchen.

lines.speaker: Canalizador
lines.text: Bom dia! Sim, sou eu. Diga. Onde é que é o problema?
lines.translation: Good morning! Yes, it's me. Tell me. Where's the problem?

lines.speaker: Cliente
lines.text: É na cozinha. Quanto é que fica o arranjo? E quando é que consegue vir?
lines.translation: It's in the kitchen. How much will the repair be? And when can you come?

lines.speaker: Canalizador
lines.text: Olhe, só vendo no local. Mas p'ra já, posso ir aí amanhã de manhã, se for bem.
lines.translation: Look, I can only tell by seeing it on site. But for now, I can go there tomorrow morning, if that's fine.

lines.speaker: Cliente
lines.text: Amanhã de manhã, sim, pode ser. Mas preciso de ter uma ideia do preço, pelo menos aproximado.
lines.translation: Tomorrow morning, yes, that can work. But I need to have an idea of the price, at least approximate.

lines.speaker: Canalizador
lines.text: Pois, um arranjo desses, com material e tudo, deve ficar entre os 60 e os 80 euros. Mas só confirmo lá.
lines.translation: Well, a repair like that, with material and everything, should be between 60 and 80 euros. But I'll only confirm there.

lines.speaker: Cliente
lines.text: Certo. E se ficar um bocado mais caro, podemos negociar?
lines.translation: Okay. And if it's a bit more expensive, can we negotiate?

lines.speaker: Canalizador
lines.text: Pois, sempre se pode falar. Amanhã de manhã, então. Ficamos combinados.
lines.translation: Well, we can always talk. Tomorrow morning, then. It's settled.

cultural_notes.title: Negotiating Service Prices
cultural_notes.body: In Portugal, especially with independent tradespeople (like plumbers, electricians, handymen), it's often acceptable to politely try and negotiate the price for a service. It's not considered rude to ask for a 'melhor preço' (better price) or to discuss the quote. This is less common in larger companies or fixed-price retail settings.

cultural_notes.title: Time and Deadlines
cultural_notes.body: When arranging times with tradespeople, 'amanhã de manhã' (tomorrow morning) can sometimes mean 'sometime in the morning' rather than a precise hour. It's common to confirm the day and then re-confirm closer to the time, or to ask for a narrower time window if precision is important to you. Deadlines (`prazos`) can also be a bit flexible, so clear communication is key.

roleplay.scenario: You've asked a local gardener to give you a quote for trimming your hedge and cleaning your garden. The price is a bit higher than you expected, and you also need the work done by a specific day.

nodes.npc_text: Então, para podar as sebes todas e limpar o jardim, o orçamento fica em 180 euros. É um trabalho que leva um dia inteiro.
nodes.npc_translation: So, to trim all the hedges and clean the garden, the quote is 180 euros. It's a full day's work.

options.text: É um bocado caro. Não consegue fazer um preço melhor?
options.translation: It's a bit expensive. Can't you make a better price?
options.feedback: You've politely expressed concern about the price and asked for a discount. Good start!

options.text: O preço é aceitável, mas preciso que esteja feito até sexta-feira.
options.translation: The price is acceptable, but I need it to be done by Friday.
options.feedback: You've accepted the price but immediately set a condition for the deadline. Clear communication!

options.text: E se fizermos só a parte da frente? Quanto é que fica?
options.translation: What if we only do the front part? How much would that be?
options.feedback: You're proposing a compromise by reducing the scope of work to lower the cost. Good strategy!

nodes.npc_text: Olhe, o material e o transporte levam o seu custo. Posso fazer por 170 euros, mas menos que isso, não consigo. O trabalho é muito.
nodes.npc_translation: Look, the material and transport have their cost. I can do it for 170 euros, but less than that, I can't. It's a lot of work.

options.text: Certo, entendo. E se fizermos só a parte da frente? Quanto é que fica?
options.translation: Okay, I understand. What if we only do the front part? How much would that be?
options.feedback: You're pivoting to a different negotiation strategy by reducing the work scope.

options.text: OK, aceito os 170 euros, mas preciso de ter a certeza que fica pronto até sexta.
options.translation: Okay, I accept the 170 euros, but I need to be sure it's ready by Friday.
options.feedback: You've accepted the slightly reduced price and are now focusing on the deadline.

options.text: Hmm, mesmo assim, é muito. Agradeço, mas vou ter de procurar outra pessoa.
options.translation: Hmm, even so, it's too much. Thank you, but I'll have to look for someone else.
options.feedback: You've politely declined the offer. Sometimes, walking away is part of negotiation.

nodes.npc_text: Até sexta? Sim, consigo. Posso ir lá na quinta-feira, assim tenho tempo de acabar tudo com calma.
nodes.npc_translation: By Friday? Yes, I can. I can go there on Thursday, so I have time to finish everything calmly.

options.text: Perfeito! Então ficamos combinados para quinta-feira.
options.translation: Perfect! So it's settled for Thursday.
options.feedback: You've successfully agreed on a deadline. Well done!

options.text: E se não conseguir acabar até lá? O que acontece?
options.translation: And if you can't finish by then? What happens?
options.feedback: You're asking for clarification on what happens if the deadline is missed. Important for managing expectations.

nodes.npc_text: Só a parte da frente? Para podar e limpar essa zona, consigo fazer por 90 euros. Demora umas 4 horas.
nodes.npc_translation: Just the front part? To trim and clean that area, I can do it for 90 euros. It takes about 4 hours.

options.text: Esse preço parece-me bem para essa parte. Quando é que pode começar?
options.translation: That price seems good for that part. When can you start?
options.feedback: You've agreed to the reduced scope and price, now asking for a start date.

options.text: Mas se fizer tudo, consegue fazer um desconto maior no total?
options.translation: But if you do everything, can you give a bigger discount on the total?
options.feedback: You're trying to leverage the full scope for a better overall price. Good move!

nodes.npc_text: Pois, se por algum motivo não der, ligo-lhe logo. Mas sou de palavra, não se preocupe. Faço sempre os meus prazos.
nodes.npc_translation: Well, if for some reason it doesn't work out, I'll call you right away. But I keep my word, don't worry. I always meet my deadlines.

options.text: Pois, não queria que isso acontecesse. É importante que esteja pronto.
options.translation: Well, I wouldn't want that to happen. It's important that it's ready.
options.feedback: You've reinforced the importance of the deadline and accepted the verbal assurance.

options.text: Acho que não vamos chegar a um acordo. Obrigado na mesma.
options.translation: I don't think we'll reach an agreement. Thanks anyway.
options.feedback: You've decided not to proceed due to uncertainty about the deadline. This is a valid choice.

nodes.npc_text: Posso ir já amanhã de manhã, se quiser. Ou na quarta-feira.
nodes.npc_translation: I can go tomorrow morning, if you want. Or on Wednesday.

options.text: Ótimo! Amanhã de manhã, então. Ficamos combinados.
options.translation: Great! Tomorrow morning, then. It's settled.
options.feedback: You've successfully set a start date and confirmed the agreement. Well done!

nodes.npc_text: Para o trabalho todo, o melhor que consigo é mesmo os 170 euros, como lhe disse. É o meu preço justo.
nodes.npc_translation: For the whole job, the best I can do is really 170 euros, as I told you. It's my fair price.

options.text: OK, aceito os 170 euros para o trabalho completo. Mas preciso da sua garantia para o prazo até sexta.
options.translation: Okay, I accept the 170 euros for the complete job. But I need your guarantee for the deadline by Friday.
options.feedback: You've accepted the full scope price, but are now pushing for a firm deadline.

options.text: Não, não consigo. Vou ter de ficar só com a parte da frente pelo preço de 90 euros.
options.translation: No, I can't. I'll have to stick with just the front part for the 90 euro price.
options.feedback: You've decided to stick to the smaller scope of work. You know your budget!

nodes.npc_text: Muito bem, então! Até lá. Obrigado.
nodes.npc_translation: Very good, then! See you. Thank you.

nodes.npc_text: Certo, entendo. Se mudar de ideias, é só ligar. Bom dia.
nodes.npc_translation: Okay, I understand. If you change your mind, just call. Good day.

situations.title: Repairing Misunderstandings
situations.summary: When something is misheard or goes wrong mid-conversation: clarifying, apologising, and getting back on track.

variants.text: Pode dizer outra vez, por favor?
variants.translation: Can you say it again, please?

slots.name: item
slots.description: the item or concept being clarified

phrase_patterns.translation: Ah, now I understand. Thank you.

variants.translation: Right, now it makes sense. Thank you.

phrase_patterns.translation: I apologize, I made a mistake.

vocabulary.note: Common interjection in Portugal
Common in Portugal, especially Lisbon/Madeira

dialogues.title: At the Market Stall
dialogues.context: A tourist is buying vegetables at a market stall in Funchal. There's a slight misunderstanding about the quantity.

lines.speaker: Vendedor
lines.text: Diga! Precisa de ajuda?
lines.translation: Yes! Do you need help?

lines.speaker: Turista
lines.text: Sim, por favor. Queria dois quilos de semilhas.
lines.translation: Yes, please. I'd like two kilos of potatoes.

lines.speaker: Vendedor
lines.text: Dois quilos de cenouras, certo?
lines.translation: Two kilos of carrots, right?

lines.speaker: Turista
lines.text: Desculpe, não percebi. Quis dizer semilhas, não cenouras.
lines.translation: Sorry, I didn't understand. I meant potatoes, not carrots.

lines.speaker: Vendedor
lines.text: Ah, pois, semilhas! Entendido. P'ra já!
lines.translation: Ah, right, potatoes! Understood. Coming right up!

lines.speaker: Turista
lines.text: Obrigado.
lines.translation: Thank you.

cultural_notes.title: Politeness and Clarification
cultural_notes.body: When clarifying a misunderstanding in Portuguese, it's always polite to start with 'Desculpe' (Sorry/Excuse me) or 'Peço desculpa' (I apologize). Phrases like 'Não percebi' (I didn't understand) or 'Pode repetir?' (Can you repeat?) are direct but softened by 'por favor' (please). It's common to use 'Pois' or 'Ah, pois' to acknowledge understanding after a clarification, indicating agreement or realization.

cultural_notes.title: Madeiran Localisms
cultural_notes.body: In Madeira, 'semilhas' is the common word for potatoes, whereas in mainland Portugal, 'batatas' is more prevalent. Using local vocabulary like this shows respect for the local culture and will be appreciated. Also, 'P'ra já!' (short for 'Para já!') is a common, quick way for service staff to say 'Coming right up!' or 'Immediately!'.

roleplay.scenario: You are at a local pastelaria (pastry shop) in Funchal. You want to order a coffee and a typical Madeiran pastry, but the server mishears part of your order.

nodes.npc_text: Bom dia! O que vai ser?
nodes.npc_translation: Good morning! What will it be?

options.text: Bom dia. Queria uma bica e um bolo de mel.
options.translation: Good morning. I'd like an espresso and a honey cake.
options.feedback: Good clear start to your order. Remember 'bica' is common in Madeira.

nodes.npc_text: Uma bica e dois bolos de mel. É isso?
nodes.npc_translation: An espresso and two honey cakes. Is that right?

options.text: Desculpe, não percebi bem. Quis dizer 'um' bolo de mel, não 'dois'.
options.translation: Sorry, I didn't understand well. I meant 'one' honey cake, not 'two'.
options.feedback: Excellent! You used 'Desculpe, não percebi bem' and clarified the number directly.

options.text: Não, não. Só um bolo de mel.
options.translation: No, no. Only one honey cake.
options.feedback: This works, but 'Desculpe' or 'Peço desculpa' would make it more polite.

nodes.npc_text: Ah, pois! Um bolo de mel. Entendido! Já vai.
nodes.npc_translation: Ah, right! One honey cake. Understood! Coming up.

options.text: Obrigado/a.
options.translation: Thank you.
options.feedback: Simple and polite ending. You successfully corrected the order.

nodes.npc_text: De nada! Aqui está.
nodes.npc_translation: You're welcome! Here it is.

situations.title: Month 5 Stress Test
situations.summary: Problem-solving under pressure: complaints, negotiation and misunderstandings combined in messy real-life exchanges.

slots.name: o_meu_assunto
slots.description: the specific item with a mistake

variants.text: Parece que houve um engano com a minha conta.
variants.translation: It seems there was a mistake with my bill.

phrase_patterns.base: Não estou a perceber o que se {passa}.

variants.text: Não estou a perceber o que se está a passar com esta situação.
variants.translation: I'm not understanding what's happening with this situation.

phrase_patterns.translation: I would like to {resolve} this situation.

variants.text: Gostaria de resolver esta situação o mais rápido possível.
variants.translation: I'd like to resolve this situation as quickly as possible.

vocabulary.note: Commonly used in Portugal and Madeira to refer to one's tax identification number.

dialogues.title: High Water Bill
dialogues.context: At the Águas da Madeira (Water Company) office, a customer is trying to understand an unusually high water bill.

lines.speaker: Atendente
lines.text: Bom dia. Diga, em que posso ajudar?
lines.translation: Good morning. Tell me, how can I help?

lines.speaker: Cliente
lines.text: Bom dia. É sobre a minha fatura da água. Recebi esta aqui, e o valor está muito mais alto do que o normal. Não estou a perceber o que se passa.
lines.translation: Good morning. It's about my water bill. I received this one here, and the amount is much higher than usual. I'm not understanding what's happening.

lines.speaker: Atendente
lines.text: Pois, entendo. Pode dar-me o seu número de cliente, por favor?
lines.translation: Right, I understand. Can you give me your client number, please?

lines.speaker: Cliente
lines.text: Sim, claro. É o 123456789. Eu costumo pagar tipo 20 euros, e esta veio a 80! Houve algum engano?
lines.translation: Yes, of course. It's 123456789. I usually pay around 20 euros, and this one came to 80! Was there some mistake?

lines.speaker: Atendente
lines.text: Deixe-me verificar... Ah, sim. Pelos registos, parece que houve um consumo bastante elevado neste mês. O contador foi lido no dia 15.
lines.translation: Let me check... Ah, yes. According to the records, it seems there was a very high consumption this month. The meter was read on the 15th.

lines.speaker: Cliente
lines.text: Consumo elevado? Mas não estivemos a usar mais água do que o normal. Será que a leitura está correta? Gostaria de resolver esta situação.
lines.translation: High consumption? But we weren't using more water than usual. Is the reading correct? I'd like to resolve this situation.

lines.speaker: Atendente
lines.text: Posso abrir um processo de verificação da leitura ou podemos agendar a visita de um técnico para inspecionar o contador. Qual prefere?
lines.translation: I can open a process to verify the reading, or we can schedule a technician's visit to inspect the meter. Which do you prefer?

cultural_notes.title: Politeness in Complaints
cultural_notes.body: When making a complaint or raising an issue in Portugal, especially in official settings, it's crucial to maintain a polite and respectful tone. Using phrases like 'Gostaria de...' (I would like to...), 'Será que podemos...' (Can we...), and 'Por favor' (Please) softens your request and is more likely to yield a positive response than an aggressive or demanding approach. Even if you're frustrated, a calm demeanor is always preferred.

cultural_notes.title: Patience with Bureaucracy
cultural_notes.body: Dealing with bureaucracy in Portugal can sometimes be a slow process. It's common for things to take longer than expected, and you might need to visit an office multiple times or wait for documents. Patience and persistence, combined with politeness, are key. Avoid expecting immediate resolutions, and be prepared to follow up if necessary.

roleplay.scenario: You've received an electricity bill from EEM (Empresa de Eletricidade da Madeira) that seems unusually high, even though your consumption habits haven't changed. You go to their customer service office to clarify and hopefully resolve the issue.

nodes.npc_text: Bom dia. Diga, em que posso ajudar hoje?
nodes.npc_translation: Good morning. Tell me, how can I help today?

options.text: Bom dia. É sobre a minha fatura da eletricidade. Parece que houve um engano com o valor.
options.translation: Good morning. It's about my electricity bill. It seems there was a mistake with the amount.
options.feedback: Excellent start! You clearly state the purpose of your visit while remaining polite.

options.text: A minha conta da luz está errada. O que se passa?
options.translation: My electricity bill is wrong. What's happening?
options.feedback: This is direct, but a bit abrupt. Remember to use more polite phrasing for official complaints.

nodes.npc_text: Pois, entendo. Pode dar-me o seu número de cliente ou o número da fatura, por favor?
nodes.npc_translation: Right, I understand. Can you give me your client number or the invoice number, please?

options.text: Sim, claro. O número de cliente é o 987654321 e o da fatura é este.
options.translation: Yes, of course. The client number is 987654321 and the invoice number is this one.
options.feedback: Good, providing the requested information promptly helps move the process along.

nodes.npc_text: Obrigado. Deixe-me verificar... Ah, sim. Vejo que a fatura de este mês é de 120 euros. Pelos nossos registos, o consumo parece estar correto, com base na leitura do contador.
nodes.npc_translation: Thank you. Let me check... Ah, yes. I see this month's bill is 120 euros. According to our records, the consumption seems correct, based on the meter reading.

options.text: Mas isso não é possível! O meu consumo nunca foi tão alto. Não estou a perceber o que se passa.
options.translation: But that's not possible! My consumption has never been so high. I'm not understanding what's happening.
options.feedback: Good, you're expressing doubt and asking for clarification, which is key in a misunderstanding.

options.text: Será que a leitura do contador pode estar errada? Gostaria de resolver esta situação.
options.translation: Could the meter reading be wrong? I'd like to resolve this situation.
options.feedback: Excellent, you're politely questioning the data and stating your intention to resolve it.

nodes.npc_text: Pois, os nossos técnicos fizeram a leitura no dia 20. Por vezes, um eletrodoméstico avariado ou uma fuga podem causar um aumento. Será que podemos agendar uma visita técnica para verificar o contador e a instalação?
nodes.npc_translation: Well, our technicians took the reading on the 20th. Sometimes, a faulty appliance or a leak can cause an increase. Can we schedule a technician's visit to check the meter and the installation?

options.text: Sim, por favor. Isso seria ótimo. E se o problema não for meu, o que acontece?
options.translation: Yes, please. That would be great. And if the problem isn't mine, what happens?
options.feedback: You're accepting the proposed solution and also thinking ahead about potential outcomes.

options.text: Não tenho a certeza. Será que podemos encontrar uma solução sem ter de agendar uma visita?
options.translation: I'm not sure. Can we find a solution without having to schedule a visit?
options.feedback: You're trying to negotiate, which is good for L4. The agent might insist on the visit for verification.

nodes.npc_text: Se o técnico verificar que o contador está avariado ou a leitura foi incorreta, faremos o acerto na sua próxima fatura. Se for um problema na sua instalação, ele poderá aconselhá-lo sobre o que fazer. Qual é a sua disponibilidade para a visita?
nodes.npc_translation: If the technician verifies that the meter is faulty or the reading was incorrect, we will adjust your next bill. If it's a problem with your installation, he can advise you on what to do. What is your availability for the visit?

options.text: P'ra já, preciso de ver a minha agenda. Posso ligar mais tarde para agendar?
options.translation: For now, I need to check my schedule. Can I call back later to schedule?
options.feedback: A practical response. You're taking control of the next step.

options.text: Entendido. Muito obrigado pela sua ajuda. Vou aguardar a visita.
options.translation: Understood. Thank you very much for your help. I will await the visit.
options.feedback: A polite and conclusive response, showing you've understood the process.

situations.title: Returning a Faulty Purchase
situations.summary: Something you bought has failed: stating the fault, invoking the warranty (garantia), insisting on repair, replacement or refund when the first answer is no.

phrase_patterns.base: Comprei-o há {tempo} e está na garantia.
phrase_patterns.translation: I bought it {time ago} and it's under warranty.

variants.text: Comprei-a há {tempo} e está na garantia.
variants.translation: I bought it (feminine item) {time ago} and it's under warranty.
variants.note: Use for feminine items like 'máquina de café'.

slots.name: ação
options: ter o meu dinheiro de volta

vocabulary.note: Often shortened to 'talão' in Madeira and Portugal.

dialogues.title: Returning a Faulty Blender
dialogues.context: A customer is returning a blender that stopped working after a month to a small appliance shop in Funchal.

lines.speaker: Cliente
lines.text: Bom dia. Este liquidificador que comprei aqui há um mês e meio está avariado.
lines.translation: Good morning. This blender I bought here a month and a half ago is faulty.

lines.speaker: Empregado
lines.text: Ah, pois. O que é que se passa com ele?
lines.translation: Ah, right. What's wrong with it?

lines.speaker: Cliente
lines.text: Deixou de funcionar, pura e simplesmente. Não liga. Está na garantia.
lines.translation: It just stopped working, plain and simple. It doesn't turn on. It's under warranty.

lines.speaker: Empregado
lines.text: Tem o talão de compra, sim?
lines.translation: You have the receipt, right?

lines.speaker: Cliente
lines.text: Tenho, claro. Aqui está.
lines.translation: I do, of course. Here it is.

lines.speaker: Empregado
lines.text: Hum... um mês e meio... A avaria parece ser de fabrico. Quer que o enviemos para a assistência técnica, ou prefere trocar por um novo?
lines.translation: Hmm... a month and a half... The fault seems to be manufacturing. Do you want us to send it for technical support, or do you prefer to exchange it for a new one?

lines.speaker: Cliente
lines.text: Se puder ser, prefiro trocar. É mais rápido, não é?
lines.translation: If possible, I prefer to exchange. It's faster, isn't it?

lines.speaker: Empregado
lines.text: Sim, claro. Espere só um bocado que eu vou buscar um igual ao armazém.
lines.translation: Yes, of course. Just wait a bit while I get an identical one from the warehouse.

cultural_notes.title: The Importance of the 'Talão de Compra' (Receipt)
cultural_notes.body: In Portugal, keeping your receipt (talão de compra or fatura) is absolutely crucial for any return, exchange, or warranty claim. Stores will almost always ask for it, and without it, they are often reluctant or unable to help, even for faulty items. Always keep your receipts for significant purchases.

cultural_notes.title: Consumer Rights and Warranty in Portugal
cultural_notes.body: New goods in Portugal typically come with a 2-year legal warranty. Within this period, if an item develops a fault that is not due to misuse, consumers have the right to repair, replacement, or a refund. Sellers usually prefer to offer repair first, then replacement, and refund as a last resort. Be prepared to politely but firmly insist on your rights if initially refused, especially if the fault is clearly manufacturing-related and within the warranty period.

roleplay.scenario: You are returning a faulty coffee machine to a small appliance store in Funchal. It stopped heating water after two months. You have the receipt.

nodes.npc_text: Bom dia. Em que posso ajudar?
nodes.npc_translation: Good morning. How can I help?

options.text: Bom dia. Comprei esta máquina de café aqui há dois meses e está avariada.
options.translation: Good morning. I bought this coffee machine here two months ago and it's faulty.
options.feedback: Great start, clearly stating the item, purchase time, and problem.

nodes.npc_text: Avariada? O que é que se passa com ela?
nodes.npc_translation: Faulty? What's wrong with it?

options.text: Não está a aquecer a água. Está na garantia, claro.
options.translation: It's not heating the water. It's under warranty, of course.
options.feedback: Excellent, you explained the specific fault and immediately mentioned the warranty.

nodes.npc_text: Tem o talão de compra ou a fatura, por favor?
nodes.npc_translation: Do you have the receipt or invoice, please?

options.text: Sim, tenho. Aqui está o talão de compra.
options.translation: Yes, I do. Here is the receipt.
options.feedback: Perfect, providing the necessary proof of purchase.

nodes.npc_text: Pois, vejo aqui. Não podemos reembolsar, mas podemos enviar para a assistência técnica para reparação. Demora uns dias, talvez uma semana.
nodes.npc_translation: Right, I see here. We can't refund, but we can send it for technical support for repair. It takes a few days, maybe a week.

options.text: Uma semana é muito tempo. Não posso ficar sem máquina de café. Não podem trocar por uma nova?
options.translation: A week is too long. I can't be without a coffee machine. Can't you exchange it for a new one?
options.feedback: Good, you're pushing back on the repair time and suggesting an exchange.

options.text: Uma semana? E se não tiver reparação? Tenho direito a um reembolso, não tenho?
options.translation: A week? And what if it can't be repaired? I have a right to a refund, don't I?
options.feedback: Strong move, questioning the repair outcome and asserting your right to a refund.

options.text: Está bem, então. Envie para reparação.
options.translation: Alright then. Send it for repair.
options.feedback: This is an option, but you're not fully exploring your rights. You could try for a faster solution.

nodes.npc_text: Olhe, o que posso fazer é trocar por um modelo igual, se tivermos em stock. Se não, terá mesmo de ir para reparação.
nodes.npc_translation: Look, what I can do is exchange it for an identical model, if we have it in stock. If not, it will really have to go for repair.

options.text: Sim, trocar por um modelo igual seria o ideal. Agradeço.
options.translation: Yes, exchanging it for an identical model would be ideal. Thank you.
options.feedback: Excellent negotiation! You got a better outcome than just repair.

options.text: E se não tiverem em stock? Posso pedir o reembolso?
options.translation: And if you don't have it in stock? Can I ask for a refund?
options.feedback: Good follow-up, anticipating the next step and reiterating your right to a refund.

nodes.npc_text: Se não tivermos em stock, teremos de enviar para a marca para ver se a reparação é possível. Só depois disso é que se fala em reembolso.
nodes.npc_translation: If we don't have it in stock, we'll have to send it to the brand to see if repair is possible. Only after that can we talk about a refund.

options.text: Compreendo. Então, por favor, verifique se têm em stock primeiro.
options.translation: I understand. So, please check if you have it in stock first.
options.feedback: You've navigated a tricky situation well, accepting the process but prioritizing the best outcome.

options.text: Isso não é justo! Tenho direito a um reembolso já!
options.translation: That's not fair! I have a right to a refund now!
options.feedback: While you have rights, sometimes a more confrontational approach can be less effective. It's often better to go through the steps.

nodes.npc_text: P'ra já, vou ver se temos em stock. Um momento, por favor.
nodes.npc_translation: Right away, I'll see if we have it in stock. One moment, please.

nodes.npc_text: Lamento, mas sem o talão não posso ajudar.
nodes.npc_translation: I'm sorry, but without the receipt I cannot help.

Practice explaining the fault clearly and concisely in Portuguese.
Rehearse phrases for invoking the warranty and insisting on your consumer rights.
Mentally prepare for potential pushback and how to respond politely but firmly.
fallback_phrases: O que é que quer dizer com isso?
likely_responses: O que é que se passa com ele?
Não podemos fazer o reembolso, só a reparação.
Isso não está coberto pela garantia.
Vamos enviar para a assistência técnica.
Só podemos trocar se tivermos em stock.

goals: State a problem with a recently purchased item.
Invoke the item's warranty and provide proof of purchase.
Negotiate for repair, replacement, or a refund when facing initial resistance.
Understand and respond to common customer service responses regarding faulty items.

Say in Portuguese: I bought it {time ago} and it's under warranty.
review_items.answer: Comprei-o há {tempo} e está na garantia.

Say in Portuguese: I have the {proof of purchase}.

situations.title: The Bill Is Wrong
situations.summary: A charge you do not recognise — restaurant bill, utility invoice, subscription: querying line by line, staying polite under pushback, and agreeing the correction.

phrase_patterns.base: Penso que há um erro na {documento}.
phrase_patterns.translation: I think there's an error on the {document}.

variants.translation: There's an error on the bill.

variants.note: More direct way to dispute an item.

vocabulary.note: Used for restaurant/café bills. For utilities/general invoices, 'fatura'.

dialogues.title: Restaurant Bill Discrepancy
dialogues.context: You're at a restaurant in Funchal. The waiter brings the bill, and you notice an extra item you didn't order.

lines.speaker: Waiter
lines.text: Aqui está a sua conta, o senhor.
lines.translation: Here is your bill, sir.

lines.speaker: You
lines.text: Obrigado. Ah, desculpe, mas penso que há um erro na conta.
lines.translation: Thank you. Oh, excuse me, but I think there's an error on the bill.

lines.speaker: Waiter
lines.text: Um erro? Tem a certeza? Está tudo certo, tenho aqui o registo.
lines.translation: An error? Are you sure? Everything is correct, I have the record here.

lines.speaker: You
lines.text: Sim, tenho a certeza. Não reconheço este item, 'Bolo do Caco com alho'. Eu não o pedi.
lines.translation: Yes, I'm sure. I don't recognize this item, 'Bolo do Caco with garlic'. I didn't order it.

lines.speaker: Waiter
lines.text: Hmm, deixe-me verificar. Um momento, por favor.
lines.translation: Hmm, let me check. One moment, please.

lines.speaker: Waiter
lines.text: Pois é, tem razão. Peço desculpa, foi um engano da cozinha. Vou corrigir já.
lines.translation: Indeed, you are right. I apologize, it was a kitchen mistake. I'll correct it now.

lines.speaker: You
lines.text: Muito obrigado.
lines.translation: Thank you very much.

cultural_notes.title: Politeness in Disputing a Bill
cultural_notes.body: When disputing a bill in Portugal, it's crucial to remain polite and use formal address ('o senhor' / 'a senhora') unless you're explicitly invited to use 'tu'. Start with 'Desculpe, mas...' (Excuse me, but...) or 'Penso que há um erro...' (I think there's an error...). Avoid direct accusations. The goal is to clarify, not to confront. People generally respond well to a calm and respectful approach.

cultural_notes.title: Conta vs. Fatura
cultural_notes.body: In Portugal, 'conta' is typically used for the bill you receive at a restaurant, café, or bar. For utility bills (water, electricity), phone bills, or general invoices for services or purchases, the correct term is 'fatura'. If you need an official receipt with tax details, you'd ask for a 'fatura com número de contribuinte' (invoice with tax number).

roleplay.scenario: You are at a local café in Madeira. You ordered a bica and a pastel de nata. The bill arrives, and you see a 'sumo de laranja' (orange juice) that you did not order.

nodes.npc_text: Aqui está a sua conta, o senhor. Deu sete euros e cinquenta cêntimos.
nodes.npc_translation: Here is your bill, sir. That's seven euros and fifty cents.

options.text: Desculpe, penso que há um erro na conta.
options.translation: Excuse me, I think there's an error on the bill.
options.feedback: A polite and clear way to start the conversation.

options.text: Sete euros e cinquenta? Isso é muito!
options.translation: Seven euros and fifty? That's a lot!
options.feedback: While true, this is a bit abrupt. Try to be more specific and polite.

nodes.npc_text: Um erro? Tem a certeza? O que é que se passa?
nodes.npc_translation: An error? Are you sure? What's wrong?

options.text: Sim, tenho. Não reconheço o sumo de laranja nesta conta.
options.translation: Yes, I am. I don't recognize the orange juice on this bill.
options.feedback: Clearly states the issue. Good.

options.text: Eu só pedi uma bica e um pastel de nata.
options.translation: I only ordered a bica and a pastel de nata.
options.feedback: Also good, implies the extra item without explicitly naming it yet.

nodes.npc_text: Ah, pois é. Sumo de laranja... Deixe-me ver o que se passou. Um momento, por favor.
nodes.npc_translation: Ah, right. Orange juice... Let me see what happened. One moment, please.

options.text: Claro, sem problema.
options.translation: Of course, no problem.
options.feedback: Patient and understanding response.

options.text: Estou com pressa.
options.translation: I'm in a hurry.
options.feedback: While you might be, this can sound impolite and unhelpful in resolving the issue.

nodes.npc_text: Peço imensa desculpa, o senhor. Foi um engano, o sumo era para a mesa ao lado. Vou corrigir a conta agora mesmo. São três euros e vinte cêntimos.
nodes.npc_translation: I'm very sorry, sir. It was a mistake, the juice was for the table next door. I'll correct the bill right now. That's three euros and twenty cents.

options.text: Muito obrigado. Acontece.
options.translation: Thank you very much. It happens.
options.feedback: Gracious acceptance of the correction. Roleplay ends here.

options.text: Tudo bem. Aqui está o dinheiro.
options.translation: Alright. Here's the money.
options.feedback: Simple and effective. Roleplay ends here.

nodes.npc_text: Sim, mas o que é que está errado? Preciso de saber para verificar.
nodes.npc_translation: Yes, but what's wrong? I need to know to check.

options.text: Desculpe. Há um sumo de laranja que eu não pedi.
options.translation: Sorry. There's an orange juice that I didn't order.
options.feedback: Correcting your approach to be more specific and polite. Good recovery.

nodes.npc_text: Compreendo, mas preciso de um momento para verificar e corrigir. Não quero que pague a mais.
nodes.npc_translation: I understand, but I need a moment to check and correct it. I don't want you to pay extra.

options.text: Tem razão. Desculpe. Vou esperar.
options.translation: You're right. Sorry. I'll wait.
options.feedback: Acknowledging their point and agreeing to wait. Good.

Practice identifying specific items or values on a bill.
Mentally prepare to insist politely if there's initial pushback.

Pode explicar isso melhor, por favor?
likely_responses: Foi um engano da nossa parte.

goals: Successfully identify and articulate an error on a bill.
Politely request a check and correction of the bill.
Understand common responses when disputing a charge.
Maintain a polite and clear communication style under mild pressure.

review_items.answer: Penso que há um erro na {documento}.

review_items.prompt: Say in Portuguese: I don't recognize this {detail}.
Say in Portuguese: Can you check {detail}, please?
Say in Portuguese: I didn't {verb} {item}.

situations.title: The No-Show
situations.summary: The tradesperson did not come: calling to find out why, expressing inconvenience firmly but without burning the relationship, and pinning down a real new time.

phrase_patterns.translation: I would like to {action} a new {appointment_type}.

phrase_patterns.base: Que dia lhe dá mais jeito?

vocabulary.note: Common term in Portugal, not 'encanador'
Very common idiom in Portugal and Madeira

dialogues.title: Calling the Plumber
dialogues.context: You are calling 'Canalizações Rápidas' because the plumber didn't show up for your scheduled appointment this morning.

lines.speaker: Atendente
lines.text: Canalizações Rápidas, bom dia. Em que posso ajudar?
lines.translation: Rapid Plumbing, good morning. How can I help you?

lines.speaker: Cliente
lines.text: Bom dia. Liguei sobre uma marcação que tinha para hoje de manhã. O canalizador não apareceu.
lines.translation: Good morning. I called about an appointment I had for this morning. The plumber didn't show up.

lines.speaker: Atendente
lines.text: Ah, pois não. Lamento imenso. Pode-me dar o seu nome e a morada, por favor?
lines.translation: Oh, dear. I'm very sorry. Can you give me your name and address, please?

lines.speaker: Cliente
lines.text: Sim, sou a [Seu Nome] e a morada é Rua da Calçada, número 15. A marcação era para as nove.
lines.translation: Yes, I'm [Your Name] and the address is Rua da Calçada, number 15. The appointment was for nine o'clock.

lines.speaker: Atendente
lines.text: Um momento, por favor... Pois, vejo aqui. Parece que houve um problema com a agenda. Isto é um bocado inconveniente, claro.
lines.translation: One moment, please... Right, I see it here. It seems there was a problem with the schedule. This is a bit inconvenient, of course.

lines.speaker: Cliente
lines.text: Sim, é. Fiquei à espera a manhã toda. Gostaria de reagendar a visita o mais rapidamente possível.
lines.translation: Yes, it is. I waited all morning. I'd like to reschedule the visit as soon as possible.

lines.speaker: Atendente
lines.text: Claro. Temos uma vaga amanhã de manhã, entre as nove e as doze. Que dia lhe dá mais jeito?
lines.translation: Of course. We have an opening tomorrow morning, between nine and twelve. What day suits you best?

lines.speaker: Cliente
lines.text: Amanhã de manhã está bem. Podem confirmar por mensagem?
lines.translation: Tomorrow morning is fine. Can you confirm by message?

lines.speaker: Atendente
lines.text: Sim, enviamos já um SMS. Mais uma vez, as nossas desculpas pelo sucedido. Até amanhã.
lines.translation: Yes, we'll send an SMS now. Once again, our apologies for what happened. See you tomorrow.

cultural_notes.title: Indirectness in Portuguese Complaints
cultural_notes.body: When making a complaint in Portugal, especially over the phone, it's common to start with a polite greeting and state the problem clearly but often with a slightly softer tone than might be used in some other cultures. Phrases like 'isto é um bocado inconveniente' (this is a bit inconvenient) or 'fiquei à espera' (I was waiting) convey dissatisfaction without being overly aggressive. The expectation is that the service provider will acknowledge the inconvenience and offer a solution. Direct confrontation is generally avoided if possible, especially in initial interactions.

cultural_notes.title: Using 'Dar Jeito' for Scheduling
cultural_notes.body: 'Dar jeito' is a very common and natural way to ask about availability or convenience in Portugal and Madeira. Instead of asking 'Quando é que pode?' (When can you?), which is also correct, 'Que dia/hora lhe dá mais jeito?' (What day/time suits you best?) is often preferred as it emphasizes the convenience for the other person, making the interaction smoother and more polite. It's a key phrase for negotiating times.

roleplay.scenario: You are calling the 'Assistência Técnica de Eletrodomésticos' (Appliance Technical Assistance) because a technician was supposed to come yesterday to fix your fridge, but never arrived. You need to politely but firmly explain the situation and reschedule.

nodes.npc_text: Bom dia, Assistência Técnica. Em que posso ajudar?
nodes.npc_translation: Good morning, Technical Assistance. How can I help you?

options.text: Bom dia. Liguei porque o técnico não apareceu ontem para arranjar o meu frigorífico.
options.translation: Good morning. I called because the technician didn't show up yesterday to fix my fridge.
options.feedback: Excellent start! You clearly stated the problem and the missed appointment.

options.text: Onde está o meu técnico? Ninguém veio!
options.translation: Where is my technician? Nobody came!
options.feedback: This is a bit too direct. Try to be polite even when complaining. Start with a greeting and a softer approach.

nodes.npc_text: Lamento imenso! Pode-me dar o seu número de cliente ou nome, por favor?
nodes.npc_translation: I'm very sorry! Can you give me your client number or name, please?

options.text: Sim, o meu nome é [Seu Nome]. A marcação era para as quatro da tarde.
options.translation: Yes, my name is [Your Name]. The appointment was for four in the afternoon.
options.feedback: Good, you provided the requested information and added a key detail about the time.

options.text: Não tenho número de cliente. Apenas quero que o técnico venha!
options.translation: I don't have a client number. I just want the technician to come!
options.feedback: It's important to cooperate with the service representative to help them find your details. Try to provide your name at least.

nodes.npc_text: Um momento... Pois, vejo aqui que houve um problema com a rota. Peço desculpa. Isto é um bocado inconveniente, claro.
nodes.npc_translation: One moment... Right, I see here there was a problem with the route. I apologize. This is a bit inconvenient, of course.

options.text: Sim, é bastante inconveniente. O frigorífico está avariado e preciso de uma solução rapidamente.
options.translation: Yes, it's quite inconvenient. The fridge is broken and I need a solution quickly.
options.feedback: You've expressed the inconvenience clearly and the urgency of the problem. Well done.

options.text: Não faz mal, acontece.
options.translation: It's okay, it happens.
options.feedback: While polite, this might not convey the urgency of your situation. You want to make sure the problem is taken seriously.

nodes.npc_text: Compreendo perfeitamente. Gostaria de reagendar para quando? Temos uma vaga na próxima terça-feira de manhã.
nodes.npc_translation: I understand perfectly. When would you like to reschedule for? We have an opening next Tuesday morning.

options.text: Terça-feira de manhã dá-me jeito. Podem confirmar a hora exata mais perto do dia?
options.translation: Tuesday morning suits me. Can you confirm the exact time closer to the day?
options.feedback: Excellent! You confirmed the new time and asked for a specific detail, showing you're taking control.

options.text: Não, terça-feira não dá. Não têm nada para amanhã?
options.translation: No, Tuesday doesn't work. Don't you have anything for tomorrow?
options.feedback: Good, you're negotiating for a better time. Be prepared for them to check other options.

nodes.npc_text: Infelizmente, amanhã já estamos com a agenda cheia. Posso ver para quarta-feira à tarde, entre as duas e as cinco?
nodes.npc_translation: Unfortunately, tomorrow's schedule is already full. I can check for Wednesday afternoon, between two and five?

options.text: Quarta-feira à tarde está bem, sim. Obrigado.
options.translation: Wednesday afternoon is fine, yes. Thank you.
options.feedback: You've successfully negotiated a new time. Good job concluding the call politely.

options.text: Não, eu preciso mesmo para amanhã. Isto é inaceitável!
options.translation: No, I really need it for tomorrow. This is unacceptable!
options.feedback: While your frustration is valid, escalating the tone might not lead to a quicker solution. Try to find a compromise.

nodes.npc_text: Certo, fica agendado para terça-feira de manhã. Enviamos uma mensagem de confirmação. Mais uma vez, as nossas desculpas.
nodes.npc_translation: Okay, it's scheduled for Tuesday morning. We'll send a confirmation message. Once again, our apologies.

Mentally prepare to express inconvenience politely but firmly.
Practice asking 'Que dia lhe dá mais jeito?' and responding to suggested times.

Que dia/hora lhe dá mais jeito?

Express inconvenience and urgency politely yet firmly.
Negotiate and confirm a new appointment time with a service provider.

situations.title: Water Damage & the Insurance Call
situations.summary: Water is coming through the ceiling: describing the damage to the insurer, understanding the claims process (participação, peritagem), and coordinating with the upstairs neighbour.

phrase_patterns.base: Tenho uma fuga de água no {local}.
phrase_patterns.translation: I have a water leak in the {location}.

phrase_patterns.base: Precisava de fazer uma {ação} de seguro.
phrase_patterns.translation: I needed to make an insurance {action}.

variants.text: Precisava de abrir um processo de seguro.
variants.translation: I needed to open an insurance claim process.

phrase_patterns.translation: I already spoke with the {position} neighbour.

vocabulary.note: Refers to the act of reporting a claim to the insurer.
The process where an expert evaluates the damage.

dialogues.title: Reporting a Leak to the Insurance
dialogues.context: A customer is calling their insurance company to report water damage in their apartment.

lines.speaker: Agente
lines.text: Boa tarde, Seguradora Confiança, em que posso ajudar?
lines.translation: Good afternoon, Confiança Insurance, how can I help?

lines.speaker: Cliente
lines.text: Boa tarde, é que tenho uma fuga de água no tecto da sala e precisava de fazer uma participação.
lines.translation: Good afternoon, I have a water leak in the living room ceiling and I needed to make a claim.

lines.speaker: Agente
lines.text: Certo. Pode descrever os danos e indicar se sabe a origem da fuga?
lines.translation: Understood. Can you describe the damage and indicate if you know the source of the leak?

lines.speaker: Cliente
lines.text: Pois, no tecto já se vê uma mancha de humidade grande e está a pingar. Acho que vem do apartamento de cima.
lines.translation: Well, on the ceiling there's already a big damp stain and it's dripping. I think it's coming from the upstairs apartment.

lines.speaker: Agente
lines.text: Já falou com o seu vizinho de cima?
lines.translation: Have you already spoken with your upstairs neighbour?

lines.speaker: Cliente
lines.text: Sim, já falei. Ele disse que ia ver, mas a água continua a vir.
lines.translation: Yes, I already spoke. He said he would check, but the water keeps coming.

cultural_notes.title: Insurance Claims in Portugal
cultural_notes.body: In Portugal, when reporting an insurance claim, especially for property damage, you will typically 'fazer uma participação' (make a claim report). The insurance company will then often arrange for a 'peritagem' (expert assessment) to evaluate the damage and determine coverage. It's common to be asked about whether you've contacted neighbours if the damage appears to originate from an adjacent property.

cultural_notes.title: Addressing Neighbours for Property Issues
cultural_notes.body: It's customary and expected to first approach your neighbour directly if you suspect a problem like a water leak originates from their property. This informal communication often precedes or accompanies the formal insurance claim process. Maintaining good neighbourly relations is valued, and direct communication can sometimes resolve issues faster than involving insurers immediately.

roleplay.scenario: You are calling your insurance company (Seguradora Confiança) to report water damage in your apartment. Water is coming through the ceiling of your kitchen.

nodes.npc_text: Boa tarde, Seguradora Confiança, em que posso ajudar?
nodes.npc_translation: Good afternoon, Confiança Insurance, how can I help?

options.text: Boa tarde. Preciso de fazer uma participação por uma fuga de água.
options.translation: Good afternoon. I need to make a claim for a water leak.
options.feedback: A good, direct start. 'Participação' is the correct term for an insurance claim.

options.text: Olá. Tenho um problema com água no meu apartamento.
options.translation: Hello. I have a problem with water in my apartment.
options.feedback: This is okay, but 'fuga de água' and 'participação' are more specific and professional.

nodes.npc_text: Certo. Onde é a fuga e qual é o tipo de dano?
nodes.npc_translation: Understood. Where is the leak and what kind of damage is it?

options.text: É no tecto da cozinha. Já tem uma mancha de humidade grande e está a pingar.
options.translation: It's in the kitchen ceiling. It already has a big damp stain and it's dripping.
options.feedback: Excellent description of the damage. 'Mancha de humidade' and 'pingar' are very clear.

options.text: Está a cair água do tecto na minha cozinha.
options.translation: Water is falling from the ceiling in my kitchen.
options.feedback: Good, but you could add more detail about the visible damage, like a 'mancha de humidade'.

nodes.npc_text: E sabe de onde vem a água? Já falou com o seu vizinho de cima?
nodes.npc_translation: And do you know where the water is coming from? Have you already spoken with your upstairs neighbour?

options.text: Acho que vem do apartamento de cima. Já falei com ele, mas a água continua a vir.
options.translation: I think it's coming from the upstairs apartment. I already spoke with him, but the water keeps coming.
options.feedback: Good, you've addressed both parts of the question directly.

options.text: Sim, já falei com o meu vizinho de cima. Ele vai ver o que se passa.
options.translation: Yes, I already spoke with my upstairs neighbour. He's going to see what's happening.
options.feedback: This is fine, but if the water is still coming, it's good to mention that to the insurer.

nodes.npc_text: Certo. Então, vamos abrir a participação. Agora vamos precisar de enviar um perito para avaliar os danos. O senhor tem disponibilidade amanhã de manhã?
nodes.npc_translation: Understood. So, we will open the claim. Now we'll need to send an expert to assess the damages. Are you available tomorrow morning?

options.text: Sim, amanhã de manhã estou em casa. O seguro cobre a reparação da pintura?
options.translation: Yes, tomorrow morning I'll be home. Does the insurance cover the paint repair?
options.feedback: Excellent, confirming availability and asking a relevant question about coverage.

options.text: Sim, posso estar em casa. O que acontece a seguir?
options.translation: Yes, I can be home. What happens next?
options.feedback: Good, confirming. Asking 'o que acontece a seguir' is a natural way to understand the process.

nodes.npc_text: Sim, os danos na pintura causados pela fuga de água serão cobertos após a peritagem. O perito também vai contactar o seu vizinho para coordenar a reparação da origem.
nodes.npc_translation: Yes, paint damage caused by the water leak will be covered after the assessment. The expert will also contact your neighbour to coordinate the repair of the source.

options.text: Ótimo, muito obrigado. Então espero pelo perito amanhã. Tenha um bom dia.
options.translation: Great, thank you very much. So I'll wait for the expert tomorrow. Have a good day.
options.feedback: Perfect, you've confirmed the next steps and ended the call politely.

options.text: Pois, está bem. E se o meu vizinho não colaborar?
options.translation: Okay, that's fine. And what if my neighbour doesn't cooperate?
options.feedback: A valid concern, but the agent already mentioned the expert would contact them. For this interaction, it's best to confirm the immediate next step.

mission.title: Report Water Damage to Insurance
prep: Review vocabulary for describing water damage and insurance terms.
Practice asking about the claims process and coverage.
Mentally prepare to explain the situation clearly and concisely.
fallback_phrases: Pode explicar isso de outra forma?
likely_responses: Qual é o seu número de apólice?
Sabe qual é a origem da fuga?
Quando é que o perito pode ir à sua casa?

goals: Describe water damage and its suspected origin to an insurance agent.
Understand the key steps in making an insurance claim (participação, peritagem).
Ask about insurance coverage for specific types of damage.
Coordinate with an insurance company regarding a neighbour's involvement in a property issue.
