situations.title: Debating & Defending Opinions
situations.summary: Discussing a real topic with a Madeiran: agreeing, disagreeing, conceding and holding your ground with nuance.

variants.note: A softer way to express an opinion.
Indicates an opinion based on observation.

variants.text: Até percebo o seu lado, mas não me parece que seja assim tão simples.
variants.translation: I even understand your side, but it doesn't seem that simple to me.

variants.text: Sim, é verdade, mas por outro lado...
variants.translation: Yes, it's true, but on the other hand...

phrase_patterns.translation: You are right on that point, {detail}.

variants.text: Pois, isso é verdade, não tinha pensado nisso.
variants.translation: Well, that's true, I hadn't thought of that.

variants.text: Admito que o seu argumento é forte.
variants.translation: I admit your argument is strong.

slots.name: counter_argument
slots.description: the argument being presented in opposition
options: não podemos ignorar os impactos negativos

variants.text: Mesmo assim, não mudo a minha opinião sobre {topic}.
variants.translation: Even so, I'm not changing my opinion about {topic}.

variants.text: Por outro lado, não podemos ignorar que {fact}.
variants.translation: On the other hand, we cannot ignore that {fact}.

variants.text: Será que {statement} é realmente a melhor solução?

dialogues.title: Cruise Terminal Expansion
dialogues.context: Ana and Carlos, neighbours in Funchal, are discussing the proposed expansion of the cruise terminal and its impact on the island.

lines.speaker: Ana
lines.text: Então, Carlos, o que achas da expansão do terminal de cruzeiros? Parece-me que vai trazer imensos benefícios para a ilha.
lines.translation: So, Carlos, what do you think of the cruise terminal expansion? It seems to me it will bring immense benefits to the island.

lines.speaker: Carlos
lines.text: Pois, Ana, benefícios... Não tenho a certeza. Já temos turistas a mais em certas alturas, e isto pode ser prejudicial para os locais.
lines.translation: Well, Ana, benefits... I'm not sure. We already have too many tourists at certain times, and this could be detrimental to locals.

lines.speaker: Ana
lines.text: Prejudicial? Mas pensa no emprego, no comércio! É um investimento enorme que gera riqueza. A meu ver, é essencial para a nossa economia.
lines.translation: Detrimental? But think about jobs, about trade! It's a huge investment that generates wealth. In my view, it's essential for our economy.

lines.speaker: Carlos
lines.text: Concordo que cria empregos, sim, mas a que custo? Os preços das casas sobem, as ruas ficam cheias. A qualidade de vida dos residentes pode piorar, não achas?
lines.translation: I agree it creates jobs, yes, but at what cost? House prices go up, the streets get crowded. Residents' quality of life could worsen, don't you think?

lines.speaker: Ana
lines.text: Percebo o teu ponto de vista sobre os preços, é uma preocupação válida. No entanto, com um bom planeamento, podemos gerir isso. Não podemos fechar a porta ao progresso.
lines.translation: I understand your point of view about prices, it's a valid concern. However, with good planning, we can manage that. We can't close the door to progress.

lines.speaker: Carlos
lines.text: Progresso, sim, mas com cabeça. Ainda assim, penso que devíamos focar-nos mais em diversificar a economia, não depender tanto só do turismo. É uma perspetiva que defendo há anos.
lines.translation: Progress, yes, but with thought. Even so, I think we should focus more on diversifying the economy, not depending so much only on tourism. It's a perspective I've defended for years.

lines.speaker: Ana
lines.text: Admito que diversificar é importante. Mas o turismo já é uma realidade forte. Acho que temos de otimizar o que já temos e melhorar as infraestruturas. É a minha opinião.
lines.translation: I admit that diversifying is important. But tourism is already a strong reality. I think we have to optimize what we already have and improve the infrastructure. That's my opinion.

lines.speaker: Carlos
lines.text: Pois, Ana, vamos ver. Espero que tenhas razão e que não nos arrependamos daqui a uns anos.
lines.translation: Well, Ana, we'll see. I hope you're right and we don't regret it in a few years.

cultural_notes.title: Expressing Nuance in Madeiran Discussions
cultural_notes.body: In Madeira, as in mainland Portugal, discussions can be lively and passionate, but it's often valued to express nuance rather than taking extreme stances. Phrases like 'Concordo em parte,' 'Até percebo o seu ponto de vista,' or 'No entanto...' are common to show you've considered the other person's argument even if you ultimately disagree. Direct, aggressive confrontation is generally avoided in casual settings; instead, people tend to 'hold their ground' with polite but firm counter-arguments.

cultural_notes.title: The Role of 'Pois' and 'Pois é'
cultural_notes.body: The words 'Pois' or 'Pois é' are incredibly versatile in Portuguese. In a debate, they can indicate partial agreement ('Pois, é verdade'), reluctant concession ('Pois, tem razão'), or even a 'let's agree to disagree' sentiment ('Pois, vamos ver'). They often serve as a conversational filler that acknowledges what the other person said without necessarily endorsing it fully, allowing for a smoother transition to your own counter-argument.

roleplay.scenario: You're at your local 'café' in a Madeiran neighbourhood, talking to a neighbour, D.ª Emília, about the controversial proposal to build a new large hotel near a natural reserve.

nodes.npc_text: Bom dia! Então, já soube daquela ideia de construir um hotel enorme perto da Reserva Natural? Na minha opinião, é um disparate completo, vai estragar tudo!
nodes.npc_translation: Good morning! So, have you heard about that idea to build a huge hotel near the Natural Reserve? In my opinion, it's complete nonsense, it will ruin everything!

options.text: Concordo plenamente, D.ª Emília. Aquela zona devia ser protegida.
options.translation: I fully agree, D.ª Emília. That area should be protected.
options.feedback: You've clearly expressed agreement and reinforced her point. Good start!

options.text: Não tenho a certeza se concordo. Talvez traga benefícios para a economia local?
options.translation: I'm not sure if I agree. Maybe it will bring benefits to the local economy?
options.feedback: You've politely expressed disagreement and offered a potential counter-argument. Well done for challenging her view respectfully.

options.text: Não estou a par. O que é que dizem sobre o projeto?
options.translation: I'm not aware. What are they saying about the project?
options.feedback: Asking for more information is a good way to gather context before forming your opinion. Neutral and effective.

nodes.npc_text: Pois é, não sou só eu que penso assim! É uma vergonha. Aquela zona é tão bonita, devia ser protegida, não achas?
nodes.npc_translation: That's right, I'm not the only one who thinks so! It's a shame. That area is so beautiful, it should be protected, don't you think?

options.text: Exatamente! E os impactos ambientais seriam enormes, sem dúvida.
options.translation: Exactly! And the environmental impacts would be huge, no doubt.
options.feedback: You've reinforced your agreement with a strong argument. This leads to a shared conclusion.

options.text: Sim, mas ao mesmo tempo, o turismo é importante para a ilha. Não há um meio-termo?
options.translation: Yes, but at the same time, tourism is important for the island. Isn't there a middle ground?
options.feedback: You've introduced nuance by acknowledging the other side while still holding your ground on protection. Good for L5!

nodes.npc_text: Ah, não concorda? Mas como é que pode ser bom? Acha que não vai ter impacto no ambiente? É que eu não consigo ver os benefícios.
nodes.npc_translation: Oh, you don't agree? But how can it be good? Do you think it won't have an impact on the environment? Because I can't see the benefits.

options.text: Percebo a sua preocupação, mas o hotel criaria muitos postos de trabalho para os locais.
options.translation: I understand your concern, but the hotel would create many jobs for locals.
options.feedback: You've acknowledged her concern before presenting your counter-argument, showing respect while defending your view.

options.text: Ainda assim, penso que com um bom planeamento, os impactos podem ser minimizados.
options.translation: Even so, I think that with good planning, the impacts can be minimized.
options.feedback: You're holding your ground and offering a solution. This shows confidence in your opinion.

nodes.npc_text: Ora, dizem que é para o turismo, para criar empregos. Mas a que preço? É a única reserva que temos ali perto, e já está cheia de gente. Que mais quer saber?
nodes.npc_translation: Well, they say it's for tourism, to create jobs. But at what cost? It's the only reserve we have nearby, and it's already full of people. What else do you want to know?

options.text: Na minha opinião, a proteção ambiental devia vir primeiro que os lucros.
options.translation: In my opinion, environmental protection should come before profits.
options.feedback: You've now clearly stated your opinion, aligning with D.ª Emília's initial stance.

options.text: Compreendo, mas os empregos são importantes. Talvez haja um equilíbrio possível?
options.translation: I understand, but jobs are important. Maybe there's a possible balance?
options.feedback: You're trying to find a middle ground, acknowledging both sides of the argument. This shows good negotiation skills.

nodes.npc_text: Hum... Percebo o seu ponto de vista, que é preciso um equilíbrio. Mas é difícil encontrar esse equilíbrio quando se fala em lucros tão grandes. Não tenho a certeza que seja possível.
nodes.npc_translation: Hmm... I understand your point of view, that a balance is needed. But it's difficult to find that balance when talking about such large profits. I'm not sure it's possible.

options.text: Admito que é um desafio, mas é essencial tentar. Não podemos desistir de proteger a ilha.
options.translation: I admit it's a challenge, but it's essential to try. We can't give up on protecting the island.
options.feedback: You've acknowledged the difficulty but firmly reiterated your core value. This is a good way to conclude with a shared sentiment.

options.text: Pois, vamos ver o que acontece. Cada um tem a sua perspetiva, não é?
options.translation: Well, we'll see what happens. Everyone has their own perspective, don't they?
options.feedback: You've chosen to agree to disagree, acknowledging different perspectives. A common and polite way to end a debate.

nodes.npc_text: Pois, no fundo, acho que estamos de acordo que é uma situação complicada. Espero que as autoridades pensem bem nisto.
nodes.npc_translation: Well, deep down, I think we agree that it's a complicated situation. I hope the authorities think carefully about this.

nodes.npc_text: Bem, cada um tem a sua opinião, não é? Vamos ver o que acontece. Mas eu não vou mudar a minha ideia!
nodes.npc_translation: Well, everyone has their own opinion, don't they? We'll see what happens. But I'm not changing my mind!

mission.title: Debate a local issue with a Madeiran
prep: Choose a controversial local topic that interests you (e.g., tourism, environment, urban development).
Think of three arguments for and three against your own opinion on the topic.
Practice the phrases for agreeing, disagreeing, and conceding points with nuance.
fallback_phrases: É um assunto complicado, preciso de pensar mais sobre isso.
Não tenho uma opinião completamente formada, mas...
Percebo o que quer dizer, mas é difícil.
likely_responses: Até pode ser, mas o que é que fazemos com...?
Tem razão, isso é um bom ponto.

goals: Express your opinion clearly and with justification.
Agree and disagree with nuance, using appropriate expressions.
Defend your point of view and respond to counter-arguments respectfully.

review_items.prompt: Say in Portuguese: In my opinion, {topic} is {adjective}.
Say in Portuguese: I agree in part, but {point}.
Say in Portuguese: You are right on that point, {detail}.
Say in Portuguese: Even so, I think that {counter_argument}.
Recall the Portuguese for: point of view
Recall the Portuguese for: argument
Recall the Portuguese for: perspective
Recall the Portuguese for: to concede
Listen to the dialogue at natural speed and answer: what is being asked for?

situations.title: Full Immersion Final Test
situations.summary: The graduation exchange: an unscripted, multi-topic conversation drawing on everything — the integrated-resident test.

phrase_patterns.base: O que é que achas sobre {topic}?

variants.text: Qual é a sua opinião sobre {topic}?
E tu, o que dizes sobre {topic}?
variants.translation: And you, what do you say about {topic}?

phrase_patterns.translation: That's right, I agree with you, {reason}.

phrase_patterns.base: Olha, por acaso, nunca tinha pensado nisso dessa forma.
phrase_patterns.translation: Look, actually, I had never thought about it that way.

variants.text: É uma perspetiva diferente, não tinha considerado.
variants.translation: It's a different perspective, I hadn't considered it.

variants.text: Pois, é um bom ponto. Não tinha visto por esse lado.
variants.translation: Right, that's a good point. I hadn't seen it from that angle.

phrase_patterns.base: Há sempre prós e contras, não é verdade?
phrase_patterns.translation: There are always pros and cons, aren't there?

variants.text: Cada situação tem os seus aspetos positivos e negativos.
variants.translation: Every situation has its positive and negative aspects.
variants.note: More formal and explicit

vocabulary.note: Common in Portugal for a small coffee

vocabulary.translation: irrigation channel; walking trail alongside an irrigation channel
vocabulary.note: Specific to Madeira
Specific to Madeira
Standard pt-PT term
Common in spoken pt-PT

dialogues.title: A Chat at the Café
dialogues.context: You're having a coffee with a local acquaintance, discussing daily life in Funchal.

lines.speaker: Marta
lines.text: Então, como é que tem corrido a tua estadia cá na Madeira? Já te sentes mesmo em casa?
lines.translation: So, how has your stay here in Madeira been going? Do you really feel at home yet?

lines.speaker: Learner
lines.text: Sim, sinto-me muito bem, Marta. Adoro a ilha e as pessoas. Há sempre algo novo para descobrir.
lines.translation: Yes, I feel very good, Marta. I love the island and the people. There's always something new to discover.

lines.speaker: Marta
lines.text: Pois é, a Madeira é especial. Mas olha, o que é que achas do aumento do turismo? Achas que é bom para a ilha?
lines.translation: That's right, Madeira is special. But look, what do you think about the increase in tourism? Do you think it's good for the island?

lines.speaker: Learner
lines.text: Há sempre prós e contras, não é verdade? Por um lado, traz imensa receita, mas por outro, o Funchal está cada vez mais cheio.
lines.translation: There are always pros and cons, aren't there? On one hand, it brings a lot of revenue, but on the other, Funchal is getting more and more crowded.

lines.speaker: Marta
lines.text: Exatamente! É um bocado complicado gerir tudo isso. Mas pronto, faz parte.
lines.translation: Exactly! It's a bit complicated to manage all that. But well, it's part of it.

cultural_notes.title: Nuance and Indirectness in Conversation
cultural_notes.body: At L5, conversations often involve discussing topics with multiple facets, like the pros and cons of tourism. Portuguese speakers, especially in informal settings, may express opinions with a degree of nuance or indirectness rather than stark, direct statements. Phrases like 'Há sempre prós e contras' (There are always pros and cons) or 'É um bocado complicado' (It's a bit complicated) allow for a balanced perspective without sounding overly critical or confrontational. Agreement is often signaled with 'Pois é' or 'Exatamente'.

cultural_notes.title: The Role of 'Tu' vs. 'O Senhor/A Senhora'
cultural_notes.body: In Madeira and Portugal, the choice between informal 'tu' and formal 'o senhor/a senhora' is crucial. With peers, friends, or younger people, 'tu' is standard. However, when addressing someone older, in a service context, or in a more formal professional setting, 'o senhor/a senhora' is preferred. Using 'você' with strangers or elders can sound distancing or even impolite. This situation uses 'tu' as it's an informal acquaintance, but the roleplay might introduce a slightly more formal interaction.

roleplay.scenario: You are at a local market in Funchal, buying some fruit. The vendor, an older woman, starts a conversation with you about how things have changed on the island.

nodes.npc_text: Bom dia! Que frutas bonitas que escolheu. Já cá vive há muito tempo, não é? Como é que vê a Madeira a mudar com tanta gente nova e o turismo?
nodes.npc_translation: Good morning! What beautiful fruits you've chosen. You've been living here for a long time, haven't you? How do you see Madeira changing with so many new people and tourism?

options.text: Bom dia! Sim, já estou cá há uns anos. Acho que o turismo traz muita coisa boa, mas também desafios.
options.translation: Good morning! Yes, I've been here for a few years. I think tourism brings a lot of good things, but also challenges.
options.feedback: Excellent, a balanced and polite response. You acknowledge both sides.

options.text: Bom dia! Pois, a ilha está diferente. Para ser sincera, sinto falta da calma de antigamente.
options.translation: Good morning! Well, the island is different. To be honest, I miss the calm of the old days.
options.feedback: Good, you're expressing an honest opinion. Be prepared for the NPC to elaborate on that sentiment.

options.text: Ah, bom dia! A Madeira está sempre a melhorar, não é? É bom ver o progresso.
options.translation: Ah, good morning! Madeira is always improving, isn't it? It's good to see the progress.
options.feedback: A positive and slightly less nuanced response. The NPC might probe further.

nodes.npc_text: Pois é, desafios. Por exemplo, os preços das casas, estão um absurdo! E o trânsito no Funchal, nem se fala. Mas pronto, os turistas também compram as minhas frutas, não é?
nodes.npc_translation: That's right, challenges. For example, house prices, they're absurd! And the traffic in Funchal, don't even get me started. But well, tourists also buy my fruits, don't they?

options.text: É verdade, os preços são um problema. Mas talvez haja formas de gerir melhor o desenvolvimento.
options.translation: It's true, prices are a problem. But perhaps there are ways to better manage development.
options.feedback: Very good, you're offering a constructive thought without sounding preachy.

options.text: Sim, é uma questão complicada. Mas a beleza da ilha continua a ser única.
options.translation: Yes, it's a complicated issue. But the island's beauty remains unique.
options.feedback: A good way to acknowledge the problem while shifting to a positive aspect.

nodes.npc_text: Ah, sim, a calma... Lembro-me de quando o Funchal era mais sossegado. Agora é um corropio! Mas pronto, também há mais coisas para fazer, mais restaurantes...
nodes.npc_translation: Ah, yes, the calm... I remember when Funchal was quieter. Now it's a hustle and bustle! But well, there are also more things to do, more restaurants...

options.text: É um equilíbrio difícil, entre o progresso e a tradição. O que é que a senhora mais valoriza na Madeira de hoje?
options.translation: It's a difficult balance between progress and tradition. What do you value most about Madeira today?
options.feedback: Excellent, you're asking an open-ended question that invites the NPC to share more personal thoughts.

options.text: Pois é, há mais vida, mas também mais confusão. Mas os mercados como este continuam a ter o seu encanto.
options.translation: That's right, there's more life, but also more confusion. But markets like this still have their charm.
options.feedback: Good, you're acknowledging her point and bringing it back to the immediate context.

nodes.npc_text: Progresso, sim. Mas às vezes parece que esquecem as nossas raízes. Os jovens já não falam a sério sobre as levadas, é só telemóveis e internet. Mas pronto, é o mundo moderno, não é?
nodes.npc_translation: Progress, yes. But sometimes it seems they forget our roots. Young people don't talk seriously about the levadas anymore, it's just mobile phones and the internet. But well, it's the modern world, isn't it?

options.text: É verdade que as tradições são importantes. Mas talvez as novas gerações encontrem novas formas de as manter vivas?
options.translation: It's true that traditions are important. But perhaps new generations will find new ways to keep them alive?
options.feedback: A thoughtful and optimistic response that encourages further discussion.

options.text: Sim, os tempos mudam. Mas as levadas e a natureza da Madeira continuam a ser um grande atrativo, mesmo para os mais novos.
options.translation: Yes, times change. But the levadas and Madeira's nature continue to be a big attraction, even for the younger ones.
options.feedback: Good, you're highlighting a persistent positive aspect.

nodes.npc_text: Gerir... Pois, isso é que é o problema. Mas quem é que sabe gerir isto tudo? Os políticos? Ai, ai... Mas enfim, obrigada pela conversa e pelas suas compras. Volte sempre!
nodes.npc_translation: Manage... Well, that's the problem. But who knows how to manage all this? The politicians? Oh dear... But anyway, thank you for the conversation and your purchases. Come back always!

nodes.npc_text: Ah, sim, a beleza da ilha é inegável. É por isso que tanta gente vem cá, não é? E os nossos mercados, cheios de cor! Obrigada e volte sempre!
nodes.npc_translation: Ah, yes, the island's beauty is undeniable. That's why so many people come here, isn't it? And our markets, full of color! Thank you and come back always!

nodes.npc_text: O que mais valorizo? A nossa gente, a nossa forma de ser. Apesar de tudo, somos um povo acolhedor e isso não muda. Obrigada pela conversa, menina. Volte sempre!
nodes.npc_translation: What I value most? Our people, our way of being. Despite everything, we are a welcoming people and that doesn't change. Thank you for the conversation, dear. Come back always!

mission.title: Navigate a Multi-Topic Conversation with a Local
prep: Review vocabulary related to daily life, tourism, and local culture.
Practice expressing agreement, disagreement, and nuanced opinions politely.
Rehearse asking follow-up questions to keep a conversation flowing.
Pay attention to the use of 'o senhor/a senhora' for polite address.
fallback_phrases: Ah, sim, entendo o que quer dizer.
É uma boa questão, nunca tinha pensado nisso.
Pois, é um assunto complexo, não é?
likely_responses: Exatamente, é o que eu penso também.
Pois, mas também há o outro lado da moeda...
Ah, sim, mas ainda assim gosto muito da ilha.

goals: Engage in an unscripted conversation on multiple topics relevant to Madeiran life.
Express personal opinions and nuanced views on social and cultural changes.
Maintain conversational flow by asking relevant follow-up questions.
Demonstrate appropriate register and politeness with an older local.

review_items.prompt: Say in Portuguese: What do you think about {topic}?
review_items.answer: O que é que achas sobre {topic}?

review_items.prompt: Say in Portuguese: That's right, I agree with you, {reason}.
Say in Portuguese: Look, actually, I had never thought about it that way.
review_items.answer: Olha, por acaso, nunca tinha pensado nisso dessa forma.

review_items.prompt: Say in Portuguese: There are always pros and cons, aren't there?
review_items.answer: Há sempre prós e contras, não é verdade?

Recall the Portuguese for: irrigation channel; walking trail alongside an irrigation channel
Recall the Portuguese for: traditional Madeiran alcoholic drink
Recall the Portuguese for: to manage, to run
Listen to the dialogue at natural speed and answer: what is being asked for?

situations.title: Humour, Irony & Taking a Joke
situations.summary: Resident-level humour: understanding irony and understatement, gentle teasing between friends, laughing at yourself, and landing a joke without translating one.

phrase_patterns.translation: Are you kidding me? / Are you messing with me?

variants.note: A slightly softer way to ask if someone is joking.
Adding 'não estás?' seeks confirmation.

phrase_patterns.translation: No way! / Of course not! / No such thing!

variants.note: Emphasizes the denial or dismissal.

phrase_patterns.base: É brincadeira, não leves a mal.
phrase_patterns.translation: It's a joke, don't take it the wrong way.
What a coincidence! / Just my luck!

vocabulary.translation: to joke, to tease, to make fun of
to take offense, to take something the wrong way
for a laugh, just for fun
vocabulary.note: Contraction of 'para rir'.

dialogues.title: Bolo do Caco Disaster
dialogues.context: Nuno and Sofia are friends having a bica at a café in Funchal. Nuno is gently teasing Sofia about her attempt at making bolo do caco.

lines.speaker: Nuno
lines.text: Então, Sofia, já te aventuraste a fazer mais bolo do caco? Ou ainda estamos a recuperar do último 'experimento'?
lines.translation: So, Sofia, have you ventured to make more bolo do caco? Or are we still recovering from the last 'experiment'?

lines.speaker: Sofia
lines.text: Ó Nuno, estás a gozar comigo! Não foi assim tão mau, um bocado... rústico, talvez. Mas comestível!
lines.translation: Oh Nuno, you're kidding me! It wasn't that bad, a bit... rustic, maybe. But edible!

lines.speaker: Nuno
lines.text: Comestível, sim. Mas p'ra cortar, era preciso um machado, não era? Dava p'ra usar como arma branca, isso sim.
lines.translation: Edible, yes. But to cut it, you needed an axe, didn't you? You could use it as a weapon, that's for sure.

lines.speaker: Sofia
lines.text: Qual quê! Estás a exagerar! Foi só a primeira vez. Da próxima, faço um p'ra ti, e depois falamos. Vais ver!
lines.translation: No way! You're exaggerating! It was just the first time. Next time, I'll make one for you, and then we'll talk. You'll see!

lines.speaker: Nuno
lines.text: Pois é! Já estou a tremer de medo! Mas olha, é brincadeira, não leves a mal. É que a imagem da tua massa indomável não me sai da cabeça.
lines.translation: Oh yeah! I'm already shaking with fear! But look, it's a joke, don't take it the wrong way. It's just that the image of your untameable dough won't leave my head.

lines.speaker: Sofia
lines.text: Eu sei, eu sei. Mas um dia apanhas uma surpresa. E agora, paga a bica, que já gozaste o suficiente!
lines.translation: I know, I know. But one day you'll get a surprise. And now, pay for the espresso, you've teased enough!

dialogues.title: The 'Quiet' Party
dialogues.context: The learner is chatting with Dona Fátima, a neighbor, about a recent block party that wasn't exactly quiet.

lines.speaker: Dona Fátima
lines.text: Bom dia! Então, o senhor esteve na festa de sábado, não esteve? Que noite tão... sossegada, não acha?
lines.translation: Good morning! So, you were at Saturday's party, weren't you? Such a... quiet night, don't you think?

lines.speaker: Learner
lines.text: Bom dia, Dona Fátima! Sossegada? Nem de propósito! Acho que os vizinhos do outro lado da ilha também ouviram a música!
lines.translation: Good morning, Dona Fátima! Quiet? No way! I think the neighbors on the other side of the island also heard the music!

lines.speaker: Dona Fátima
lines.text: Ah, pois é! A minha filha disse que parecia que a casa dela estava a vibrar. Não é que seja má disposição, mas o subentendido era que ia acabar mais cedo.
lines.translation: Ah, that's right! My daughter said it felt like her house was vibrating. It's not that I'm in a bad mood, but the implied meaning was that it would end earlier.

lines.speaker: Learner
lines.text: Sim, percebo perfeitamente. Mas foi p'ra rir, no fim de contas. Toda a gente se divertiu imenso.
lines.translation: Yes, I understand perfectly. But it was for a laugh, after all. Everyone had immense fun.

lines.speaker: Dona Fátima
lines.text: Pois é, é o que importa. Desde que não se repita todas as semanas, está tudo bem. Qual quê! É bom ter vida na rua.
lines.translation: That's right, that's what matters. As long as it doesn't happen every week, it's all good. No way! It's good to have life on the street.

cultural_notes.title: The Art of Gentle Teasing (Gozar)
cultural_notes.body: In Portuguese culture, particularly among friends and family, 'gozar' (to tease or joke) is often a sign of affection and familiarity. It's common for people to gently poke fun at each other, and being able to 'apanhar a piada' (get the joke) and respond in kind is an important social skill. Taking things 'a mal' (the wrong way) can be seen as a lack of understanding or being overly sensitive. The key is to recognize the intent behind the words, which is usually playful rather than malicious.

cultural_notes.title: Understatement and Irony
cultural_notes.body: Portuguese conversation often relies on understatement and implied meaning (subentendido). Phrases like 'Não é que seja mau, mas...' (It's not that it's bad, but...) can subtly convey that something is actually quite bad. Similarly, ironic comments, often delivered with a straight face, are common. For instance, describing a very loud party as 'sossegada' (quiet) is a classic example. Recognizing these nuances is crucial for deeper integration into social interactions and appreciating the local sense of humour.

roleplay.scenario: You're at your friend Miguel's house for dinner. You recently went on a challenging hike along a levada together, and you struggled a bit more than Miguel. He's now playfully teasing you about your 'hiking prowess' in front of his wife, Ana.

nodes.npc_text: Então, Ana, nem imaginas o que foi a nossa última levada. O nosso amigo aqui, coitado, parecia que estava a fazer uma maratona com peso às costas! Quase que o levava ao colo!
nodes.npc_translation: So, Ana, you can't imagine what our last levada was like. Our friend here, poor thing, looked like he was running a marathon with weights on his back! I almost carried him!

options.text: Oh Miguel, estás a gozar! Não foi assim tão mau, só um bocado... desafiante.
options.translation: Oh Miguel, you're kidding! It wasn't that bad, just a bit... challenging.
options.feedback: Good! You've acknowledged the teasing and downplayed your struggle with a touch of humour.

options.text: Não achei piada nenhuma. Foi difícil, sim, mas não precisavas de exagerar.
options.translation: I didn't find it funny at all. It was difficult, yes, but you didn't need to exaggerate.
options.feedback: This response is a bit too serious. In Portugal, taking teasing 'a mal' can be seen as a social misstep. Try to respond with lightness.

nodes.npc_text: Desafiante, qual quê! Parecia que ias desmaiar a cada subida! A Ana, se fosse contigo, já tinha chegado ao topo e voltado!
nodes.npc_translation: Challenging, no way! You looked like you were going to faint with every climb! Ana, if she was with you, she would have already reached the top and come back!

options.text: Pois é, mas ainda cá estou. E da próxima vez, levo-te eu ao colo, vais ver!
options.translation: That's right, but I'm still here. And next time, I'll carry you, you'll see!
options.feedback: Excellent! You've teased back playfully, showing you can give as good as you get. This is a sign of good integration.

options.text: Não é que seja fácil, mas cada um tem o seu ritmo, Miguel.
options.translation: It's not that it's easy, but everyone has their own pace, Miguel.
options.feedback: This is a polite and reasonable response, but it misses an opportunity to engage in the playful banter. A bit more humour would be ideal here.

nodes.npc_text: Oh, desculpa lá! Era só p'ra rir, não leves a mal! Pensei que apanhavas a piada. Mas pronto, não falo mais da tua 'performance' atlética.
nodes.npc_translation: Oh, sorry about that! It was just for a laugh, don't take it the wrong way! I thought you'd get the joke. But okay, I won't talk about your athletic 'performance' anymore.

options.text: Não, está tudo bem. Eu percebi que era brincadeira, só estava a fazer-me de difícil.
options.translation: No, it's all good. I understood it was a joke, I was just playing hard to get.
options.feedback: Good recovery! You've shown you understood the humour and can laugh at yourself, which is highly valued.

options.text: Sim, é melhor. Vamos falar de outra coisa.
options.translation: Yes, that's better. Let's talk about something else.
options.feedback: While this ends the conversation, it's a bit abrupt and doesn't fully recover the playful atmosphere. Try to re-engage with humour.

nodes.npc_text: Haha! Essa quero eu ver! Mas pronto, é bom ver que ainda tens energia p'ra me gozar. Ana, serve mais vinho p'ra este campeão!
nodes.npc_translation: Haha! I want to see that! But okay, it's good to see you still have energy to tease me back. Ana, pour more wine for this champion!

nodes.npc_text: Pois é, tens razão. Mas olha, foi divertido na mesma! Da próxima vez, escolhemos uma levada mais 'ao teu nível'!
nodes.npc_translation: That's right, you're right. But look, it was fun anyway! Next time, we'll choose a levada more 'at your level'!

nodes.npc_text: Ah, pois é! Quase que caía na tua! Mas é bom ver que apanhas a piada. Então, e o que é que achaste da vista lá de cima, pelo menos?
nodes.npc_translation: Ah, that's right! I almost fell for it! But it's good to see you get the joke. So, what did you think of the view from up there, at least?

nodes.npc_text: Claro, claro. Desculpa lá a brincadeira. Mas a sério, foi bom ter-te connosco. E a vista valeu a pena, não valeu?
nodes.npc_translation: Of course, of course. Sorry about the joke. But seriously, it was good to have you with us. And the view was worth it, wasn't it?

mission.title: The Playful Resident
prep: Observe a conversation between Portuguese speakers and try to identify instances of gentle teasing or ironic comments.
Practice one of the 'gozar' or 'brincadeira' phrases from this lesson in your head.
Think of a light-hearted, self-deprecating comment you could make about a minor mistake or struggle you've had recently.
fallback_phrases: Não leves a mal, é brincadeira.
Eu percebi que era uma piada.

goals: Identify and understand gentle teasing and ironic comments in Portuguese conversations.
Respond appropriately to teasing, demonstrating an understanding of the humour and ability to laugh at yourself.
Use phrases related to humour and playful interaction (e.g., 'Estás a gozar?', 'Qual quê!', 'É brincadeira').
Engage in light-hearted banter with friends or acquaintances in Madeira, showing cultural integration.

review_items.prompt: Say in Portuguese: Are you kidding me? / Are you messing with me?
Say in Portuguese: No way! / Of course not! / No such thing!
Say in Portuguese: It's a joke, don't take it the wrong way.
review_items.answer: É brincadeira, não leves a mal.

review_items.prompt: Say in Portuguese: What a coincidence! / Just my luck!

Recall the Portuguese for: joke, fun, play
Recall the Portuguese for: irony

situations.title: The Art of Indirectness
situations.summary: Reading between the lines: what "talvez", "vamos ver" and "depois combinamos" really mean, softening requests and refusals, and hearing the no inside a yes.

variants.note: Slightly more formal, less common in casual speech.

phrase_patterns.translation: I don't know if I can {today}.

variants.note: Using 'estar' for a temporary state.

vocabulary.note: Common in Portugal for softening statements.
A common intensifier in pt-PT.
Common interjection for agreement or acknowledgement.
Very versatile, often used to close a topic or accept a situation.

dialogues.title: Levada Invitation
dialogues.context: Two friends, Patrícia and Sofia, are chatting. Patrícia invites Sofia for a levada walk, but Sofia wants to decline politely.

lines.speaker: Patrícia
lines.text: Então, Sofia! Queres vir connosco fazer a levada das 25 Fontes no sábado? Vai ser fixe!
lines.translation: So, Sofia! Do you want to come with us to do the 25 Fontes levada on Saturday? It's going to be great!

lines.speaker: Sofia
lines.text: Ai, pois é, a levada... Olha, talvez. Tenho umas coisas p'ra resolver este fim de semana, um bocado apertado. Vamos ver como corre.
lines.translation: Oh, right, the levada... Look, maybe. I have some things to sort out this weekend, a bit tight. Let's see how it goes.

lines.speaker: Patrícia
lines.text: Ah, ok, sem problema! Se mudares de ideias, diz-me.
lines.translation: Ah, okay, no problem! If you change your mind, let me know.

lines.speaker: Sofia
lines.text: Sim, sim, depois combinamos. Obrigada pelo convite, beijinhos!
lines.translation: Yes, yes, we'll arrange it later. Thanks for the invitation, kisses!

dialogues.title: Computer Help
dialogues.context: Daniel is at home when his older neighbour, Dona Maria, asks for help with her computer.

lines.speaker: Dona Maria
lines.text: Olá, Daniel! Estás bom? Olha, podias dar-me uma ajuda com o meu computador? Está a fazer umas coisas estranhas e não percebo nada.
lines.translation: Hello, Daniel! Are you well? Look, could you help me with my computer? It's doing strange things and I don't understand anything.

lines.speaker: Daniel
lines.text: Pois, Dona Maria... é que hoje estou com imenso trabalho. Não sei se consigo. É um bocado complicado agora. Talvez amanhã de manhã?
lines.translation: Well, Dona Maria... it's just that today I have a lot of work. I don't know if I can. It's a bit complicated right now. Maybe tomorrow morning?

lines.speaker: Dona Maria
lines.text: Pronto, pronto. Vê lá então. Se não der, peço à minha neta.
lines.translation: Alright, alright. See about it then. If it doesn't work out, I'll ask my granddaughter.

lines.speaker: Daniel
lines.text: Sim, depois combinamos. Desculpe lá qualquer coisa.
lines.translation: Yes, we'll arrange it later. Sorry about anything.

cultural_notes.title: The Portuguese 'No' in a 'Maybe'
cultural_notes.body: In Portuguese culture, especially in Madeira, direct refusal can sometimes be perceived as impolite. Instead, people often use indirect phrases like 'talvez' (maybe), 'vamos ver' (let's see), or 'depois combinamos' (we'll arrange it later) to decline or postpone an invitation or request indefinitely. The key is to 'read between the lines' – if someone uses these phrases without offering a concrete alternative, it often means 'no' or 'not really interested'.

cultural_notes.title: Softening Language
cultural_notes.body: Portuguese speakers frequently use softening language to make interactions smoother. This includes words like 'um bocado' (a bit) to downplay difficulty, 'imenso' (a lot) as a gentle intensifier, and interjections like 'pois é' (that's right) to acknowledge or agree without necessarily committing. Mastering these subtle cues is crucial for effective communication at an integrated resident level.

roleplay.scenario: You are at a local café, enjoying a bica. Your neighbour, Dona Fátima, approaches you and asks for a favour – help with her computer – that you cannot commit to right now. You need to decline politely and indirectly, leaving a vague possibility open.

nodes.npc_text: Olá, meu querido! Estás bom? Olha, preciso de uma ajuda com o computador, sabes? Está a fazer umas coisas estranhas... Podias dar uma vista de olhos?
nodes.npc_translation: Hello, my dear! Are you well? Look, I need some help with the computer, you know? It's doing strange things... Could you take a look?

options.text: Ai, Dona Fátima, pois é... Não sei se consigo hoje. Estou com imenso trabalho.
options.translation: Oh, Dona Fátima, that's right... I don't know if I can today. I have a lot of work.
options.feedback: Good start! You've used indirect language and a vague reason to soften your refusal.

options.text: Sim, claro! Quando é que precisa?
options.translation: Yes, of course! When do you need it?
options.feedback: That's a direct commitment! In Portugal, it's common to soften such acceptances, or decline indirectly if you're not sure. You've now committed to something you might not be able to do.

options.text: Não, não posso.
options.translation: No, I can't.
options.feedback: That's very direct and can be seen as impolite in this context. Try to soften your refusal.

nodes.npc_text: Ah, pois... É que é urgente, sabes? Não há mesmo um minutinho?
nodes.npc_translation: Ah, right... It's just that it's urgent, you know? Not even a minute?

options.text: Olha, talvez amanhã de manhã? Mas sem compromisso, p'ra já. Tenho de ver a minha agenda.
options.translation: Look, maybe tomorrow morning? But without commitment, for now. I have to check my schedule.
options.feedback: Excellent! You've maintained the indirectness, offered a vague possibility, and explicitly stated 'sem compromisso'.

options.text: Não, já disse que não consigo.
options.translation: No, I already said I can't.
options.feedback: While you're sticking to your refusal, the tone is still quite firm. You could soften it further.

options.text: Sim, ok, venho já.
options.translation: Yes, okay, I'm coming now.
options.feedback: You gave in! The goal was to maintain indirectness. Now you're committed to something you might not have time for.

nodes.npc_text: Pronto, pronto. Então, vamos ver. Se não der, peço ao meu neto. Obrigada na mesma!
nodes.npc_translation: Alright, alright. So, we'll see. If it doesn't work out, I'll ask my grandson. Thanks anyway!

options.text: Sim, depois combinamos. Desculpe lá qualquer coisa.
options.translation: Yes, we'll arrange it later. Sorry about anything.
options.feedback: Excellent! You successfully declined indirectly, maintained politeness, and left a vague possibility open without committing. This is a very natural way to handle such a situation in Madeira.

options.text: Não se preocupe, eu vejo isso amanhã.
options.translation: Don't worry, I'll look at it tomorrow.
options.feedback: You've committed again! The phrase 'não se preocupe' followed by 'eu vejo isso amanhã' sounds like a firm promise. Try to maintain the 'sem compromisso' attitude.

mission.title: Navigating a Social Obligation
prep: Rehearse phrases for indirect refusal or postponement, such as 'talvez', 'vamos ver', and 'depois combinamos'.
Practice using softening words like 'um bocado' and 'imenso' to make your statements less direct.
Think of polite ways to end a conversation or topic without making a firm commitment.
fallback_phrases: Não sei se consigo, p'ra já.
Tenho de ver a minha agenda.
likely_responses: Se não der, peço a outra pessoa.

goals: Understand and use indirect language to soften requests and refusals in social situations.
Identify implicit 'no's within polite, vague responses from native speakers.
Practice common Madeiran expressions for navigating social interactions with nuance.

review_items.prompt: Say in Portuguese: Maybe we'll arrange it {later}.
Say in Portuguese: Let's see how {it goes}.
Say in Portuguese: I don't know if I can {today}.
Say in Portuguese: It's a bit {complicated}.
Recall the Portuguese for: to arrange, to agree
Recall the Portuguese for: a bit, a little
Recall the Portuguese for: a lot, immensely
Recall the Portuguese for: that's right, indeed

situations.title: Reacting to Stories Like a Native
situations.summary: Back-channelling at full speed: ai é?, não acredito!, ora essa!, ainda bem — timing your reactions, asking the question the teller wants, and passing the story on.

slots.name: Detalhe
slots.description: A specific detail from the story to ask about

variants.note: A common expression of surprise or disbelief.

slots.name: Motivo
slots.description: Reason for disbelief or a follow-up question

variants.note: Used to express mild disbelief, disagreement, or to dismiss something.

slots.name: Resultado
slots.description: The positive outcome of the situation

vocabulary.note: Common term for espresso in Portugal, especially in Lisbon and Madeira.
Madeiran regional word for 'potato' (batata).

vocabulary.translation: to take offense / to take badly
vocabulary.note: Idiomatic expression.

vocabulary.translation: to pass the word / to spread the news
vocabulary.note: Common idiom for sharing information.
Used to describe the act of acquiring new information.
Common expression for something annoying or bothersome.

dialogues.title: The Van Incident
dialogues.context: Two friends, Ricardo and Sofia, are having a coffee (bica) at a local café in Funchal. Ricardo is telling Sofia about a recent mishap with his work van.

lines.speaker: Ricardo
lines.text: Então, não vais acreditar no que me aconteceu ontem com a carrinha da empresa.
lines.translation: So, you won't believe what happened to me yesterday with the company van.

lines.speaker: Sofia
lines.text: Ai é? Conta lá! O que se passou?
lines.translation: Oh really? Tell me! What happened?

lines.speaker: Ricardo
lines.text: Estava a subir a estrada das Romeiras, e de repente, ouço um barulho estranho no motor. Pensei: 'Pronto, é agora'.
lines.translation: I was going up the Romeiras road, and suddenly, I hear a strange noise in the engine. I thought: 'Right, this is it'.

lines.speaker: Sofia
lines.text: Não acredito! E o que fizeste?
lines.translation: I don't believe it! And what did you do?

lines.speaker: Ricardo
lines.text: Estacionei como pude, liguei para o chefe. Ele disse p'ra ver se era só a correia. Mas não, era mais grave. Teve de vir o reboque!
lines.translation: I parked as best I could, called the boss. He said to see if it was just the belt. But no, it was more serious. The tow truck had to come!

lines.speaker: Sofia
lines.text: Ora essa! Que chatice! E a carrinha, já está arranjada?
lines.translation: Oh, come on! What a nuisance! And the van, is it fixed already?

lines.speaker: Ricardo
lines.text: Ainda não, está na oficina. Mas ainda bem que não foi na autoestrada, podia ter sido bem pior.
lines.translation: Not yet, it's at the garage. But it's a good thing it wasn't on the motorway, it could have been much worse.

lines.speaker: Sofia
lines.text: Pois é, tens razão. Que alívio! Queres mais uma bica?
lines.translation: That's true, you're right. What a relief! Do you want another espresso?

cultural_notes.title: The Art of Back-Channelling
cultural_notes.body: In Portuguese conversations, especially informal ones, active listening is often demonstrated through frequent 'back-channelling' – short verbal or non-verbal responses that show you are engaged. Phrases like 'Ai é?', 'Não acredito!', 'Pois é', 'A sério?' are essential for affirming the speaker, expressing surprise, or inviting them to continue. Timing these interjections naturally is key to sounding like a native speaker and showing genuine interest.

cultural_notes.title: Expressing Disbelief and Empathy
cultural_notes.body: When someone shares a surprising or unfortunate story, it's common to express a degree of disbelief ('Não acredito!', 'Não me digas!') or empathy ('Que chatice!', 'Ora essa!'). These aren't necessarily literal statements of disbelief, but rather ways to acknowledge the impact of the story and validate the speaker's experience. Finishing with 'Ainda bem!' when a positive resolution is mentioned shows shared relief and support.

roleplay.scenario: You're at a local mercado (market) in Funchal, buying some fresh fruit. The vendor, Dona Rosa, a friendly older woman, starts telling you about a peculiar incident involving a tourist and a very large banana. You need to react naturally, show interest, and keep the conversation flowing.

nodes.npc_text: Bom dia! Tudo bem? Vai levar estas maçãs 'Bravo de Esmolfe'? São uma delícia! Ah, e não vais acreditar no que me aconteceu ontem aqui com um turista...
nodes.npc_translation: Good morning! All good? Are you taking these 'Bravo de Esmolfe' apples? They are delicious! Oh, and you won't believe what happened to me yesterday here with a tourist...

options.text: Bom dia, Dona Rosa! Sim, estas maçãs são ótimas. Diga lá, o que se passou com o turista?
options.translation: Good morning, Dona Rosa! Yes, these apples are great. Tell me, what happened with the tourist?
options.feedback: Excellent start! You acknowledge her greeting, compliment the product, and directly invite her to tell the story.

options.text: Ah, sim, bom dia. Pode ser um quilo. Turistas... o que fizeram agora?
options.translation: Ah, yes, good morning. A kilo please. Tourists... what did they do now?
options.feedback: A bit dismissive of the tourist part, but still leads to her telling the story. Try to show more direct interest next time.

nodes.npc_text: Então, um senhor, inglês, queria uma banana. Mas não era uma banana qualquer! Apontou para aquela banana gigante, da variedade 'Maçã', que está ali. Eu disse-lhe que era mais para cozinhar, mas ele insistiu que queria comer assim mesmo. E eu disse: 'Olhe, é grande, mas se quer...' Ele pagou, e saiu a comer a banana como se fosse um gelado!
nodes.npc_translation: So, a gentleman, English, wanted a banana. But not just any banana! He pointed to that giant banana, of the 'Maçã' variety, that's over there. I told him it was more for cooking, but he insisted he wanted to eat it just like that. And I said: 'Look, it's big, but if you want...' He paid, and left eating the banana as if it were an ice cream!

options.text: Não acredito! A sério? E ele comeu-a toda, assim crua?
options.translation: I don't believe it! Seriously? And he ate it all, just raw?
options.feedback: Perfect! You've used a strong reaction and a follow-up question, showing genuine surprise and engagement.

options.text: Ai é? Que engraçado! E ele gostou?
options.translation: Oh really? How funny! And he liked it?
options.feedback: Good, you're reacting, but 'Não acredito' or 'Não me digas' would show more impact for such a story.

nodes.npc_text: Sim, comeu-a toda! Com um sorriso na cara! Eu fiquei a olhar, sem saber se ria ou se lhe dizia que ia ficar com a barriga a doer. Mas ele parecia feliz, ora essa!
nodes.npc_translation: Yes, he ate it all! With a smile on his face! I just watched, not knowing whether to laugh or tell him he'd get a tummy ache. But he seemed happy, oh well!

options.text: Ora essa! Que figura! Ainda bem que ele gostou, mas é uma banana enorme para comer assim!
options.translation: Oh, come on! What a character! It's a good thing he liked it, but it's a huge banana to eat like that!
options.feedback: Excellent! You've used 'Ora essa' for mild disbelief/amusement and 'Ainda bem' for the positive outcome, plus a natural comment.

options.text: Pois, é um bocado estranho. Mas cada um é como é, não é?
options.translation: Well, it's a bit strange. But each to their own, isn't it?
options.feedback: This is a bit more neutral. While true, you could have shown more animated reaction to the 'figure' he made.

nodes.npc_text: É verdade! Mas pronto, são as histórias do dia a dia no mercado. Quer mais alguma coisa? Umas semilhas frescas para o jantar?
nodes.npc_translation: That's true! But well, these are the daily stories at the market. Do you want anything else? Some fresh potatoes for dinner?

options.text: Não, obrigada, Dona Rosa. Mas foi uma história muito engraçada! Vou passar a palavra aos meus amigos. Até amanhã!
options.translation: No, thank you, Dona Rosa. But that was a very funny story! I'll pass the word on to my friends. See you tomorrow!
options.feedback: Perfect ending! You thanked her, complimented the story, mentioned passing it on, and said goodbye naturally.

options.text: Não, obrigada. Fico-me pelas maçãs. Adeus.
options.translation: No, thank you. I'll stick with the apples. Goodbye.
options.feedback: While polite, this is a very abrupt ending. You missed an opportunity to acknowledge the story and show continued warmth.

mission.title: Share a Story and React
prep: Think of a short, interesting or funny anecdote from your week.
Rehearse telling it in Portuguese, incorporating some pauses for reactions.
Practice reacting to potential stories from a friend using 'Ai é?', 'Não acredito!', 'Ora essa!', 'Ainda bem!' and follow-up questions.
fallback_phrases: Desculpe, pode falar um pouco mais devagar?
likely_responses: E então, o que é que fizeste?

goals: React spontaneously and appropriately to stories using a range of back-channelling phrases.
Ask relevant follow-up questions to show interest and keep the conversation flowing.
Understand and use common Madeiran expressions of surprise, disbelief, and relief.
Navigate a conversation where you are both a listener and a potential teller of anecdotes.

Say in Portuguese: It's a good thing that {Outcome}.

situations.title: Formal Letters & the Requerimento
situations.summary: Writing to officialdom: the structure of a requerimento, formal openings and closings (Exmo. Senhor, Com os melhores cumprimentos), stating a request precisely, and reading replies.

variants.note: Use for a female recipient

slots.name: ação
options: solicitar a V. Exa. uma cópia do documento
requerer a emissão de uma licença

phrase_patterns.base: Agradeço a V. Exa. a atenção dispensada.
phrase_patterns.translation: I thank Your Excellency for the attention given.

slots.name: documento
options: cópia do meu cartão de cidadão

vocabulary.note: A formal written request to an official body.
Used as a formal honorific, short for 'Excelentíssimo/a'.
A formal administrative decision or ruling.
Services provided by the local city council (Câmara Municipal).

dialogues.title: Discussing a Formal Letter
dialogues.context: At a local café in Funchal, two neighbours, one local and one a resident for some time, are discussing a letter received from the Câmara Municipal.

lines.speaker: Amiga (local)
lines.text: Então, como é que correu com o despacho da Câmara? Deferiram o teu pedido, ou indeferiram?
lines.translation: So, how did it go with the City Council's decision? Did they grant your request, or deny it?

lines.speaker: Residente (learner)
lines.text: Pois, recebi a carta, mas estou um bocado confuso com a linguagem. Diz 'foi deferido', o que significa que foi aceite, certo?
lines.translation: Well, I received the letter, but I'm a bit confused by the language. It says 'foi deferido', which means it was accepted, right?

lines.speaker: Amiga (local)
lines.text: Certo, sim! 'Deferido' quer dizer que aceitaram o teu requerimento. Parabéns! É sempre bom quando as coisas correm bem com os serviços camarários.
lines.translation: That's right, yes! 'Deferido' means they accepted your application. Congratulations! It's always good when things go well with municipal services.

lines.speaker: Residente (learner)
lines.text: Ufa! Que bom! E agora, preciso de responder ou está tudo tratado? A carta termina com 'Com os melhores cumprimentos'.
lines.translation: Phew! That's good! And now, do I need to reply or is everything handled? The letter ends with 'Com os melhores cumprimentos'.

lines.speaker: Amiga (local)
lines.text: Não, não precisas de responder. Essa é só a forma formal de terminar a carta. Se tiveres de fazer mais alguma coisa, eles dizem. Mas se deferiram, é seguir em frente.
lines.translation: No, you don't need to reply. That's just the formal way to end the letter. If you have to do anything else, they'll tell you. But if they granted it, just move forward.

lines.speaker: Residente (learner)
lines.text: Entendido. Da próxima vez que precisar de escrever um requerimento, já sei que devo começar com 'Exmo. Senhor' ou 'Exma. Senhora' e ser bem claro no pedido.
lines.translation: Understood. Next time I need to write an application, I know I should start with 'Exmo. Senhor' or 'Exma. Senhora' and be very clear in the request.

cultural_notes.title: The 'Requerimento' in Portuguese Bureaucracy
cultural_notes.body: A 'requerimento' is a formal written application or petition used to address official entities (e.g., city councils, government departments, courts). It's a standard and expected way to make formal requests, complaints, or provide information. The structure is usually quite rigid: formal address (e.g., 'Exmo. Senhor Presidente da Câmara'), identification of the applicant, a clear statement of the request or purpose, supporting documentation (anexos), and a formal closing. Knowing how to draft and interpret these is crucial for administrative tasks in Portugal.

cultural_notes.title: Formal Address and Closings
cultural_notes.body: In formal correspondence, especially with official bodies, specific honorifics and closing expressions are mandatory. 'Exmo. Senhor/Senhora' (Excelentíssimo/a Senhor/a) is the standard opening, often followed by the recipient's title. 'V. Exa.' (Vossa Excelência) is a formal pronoun used to refer to the person addressed. Standard formal closings include 'Com os melhores cumprimentos' (With best regards) or 'Com a mais elevada consideração' (With the highest consideration). Using these correctly demonstrates respect and competence in a formal setting.

roleplay.scenario: You are at the Câmara Municipal (City Council) information desk in Funchal. You need to submit a formal request for a minor permit, perhaps for a small renovation or a change to your property. You want to ensure you have all the correct information and documentation.

nodes.npc_text: Bom dia. Em que posso ser útil?
nodes.npc_translation: Good morning. How can I help you?

options.text: Bom dia. Gostaria de saber como devo proceder para apresentar um requerimento para uma pequena obra.
options.translation: Good morning. I'd like to know how I should proceed to submit an application for a small construction project.
options.feedback: Excellent, you clearly stated your purpose using formal language.

options.text: Olá. Preciso de uma licença para umas obras em casa.
options.translation: Hi. I need a permit for some work at home.
options.feedback: This is a bit too informal for the context. Try to use more precise and formal vocabulary like 'requerimento' and 'proceder'.

nodes.npc_text: Certo. Para uma pequena obra, terá de preencher um requerimento formal e anexar a documentação necessária. Já tem o formulário?
nodes.npc_translation: Understood. For a small construction project, you'll need to fill out a formal application and attach the necessary documentation. Do you already have the form?

options.text: Não, ainda não tenho. Onde posso obter o formulário e que documentação é necessária?
options.translation: No, I don't have it yet. Where can I get the form and what documentation is needed?
options.feedback: Good, you're asking for clarification on the next steps and required documents.

options.text: Qual formulário? Que documentos?
options.translation: Which form? What documents?
options.feedback: While direct, this is a bit abrupt. Try to phrase your questions more politely, perhaps starting with 'Poderia indicar...' or 'Gostaria de saber...'.

nodes.npc_text: Pode descarregar o formulário no nosso site ou levantar uma cópia aqui. Quanto à documentação, geralmente pedimos cópia do cartão de cidadão, comprovativo de morada e uma breve descrição da obra. Poderá ser necessário um projeto, dependendo da natureza da intervenção.
nodes.npc_translation: You can download the form from our website or pick up a copy here. Regarding documentation, we usually ask for a copy of your citizen card, proof of address, and a brief description of the work. A project plan might be necessary, depending on the nature of the intervention.

options.text: Agradeço a informação. Devo endereçar o requerimento ao Exmo. Senhor Presidente da Câmara ou a outro departamento?
options.translation: Thank you for the information. Should I address the application to the President of the City Council or another department?
options.feedback: Excellent, you're showing awareness of formal address and seeking to ensure correct protocol.

options.text: Ok, entendi. Para quem é o requerimento?
options.translation: Okay, I understood. Who is the application for?
options.feedback: Good, you're asking a relevant question. To make it more formal, you could say 'A quem devo endereçar o requerimento?'.

nodes.npc_text: Para este tipo de assunto, deve endereçar ao Exmo. Senhor Chefe da Divisão de Urbanismo. Ele é o responsável por deferir ou indeferir estes pedidos. Não se esqueça de assinar no final.
nodes.npc_translation: For this type of matter, you should address it to the Head of the Urban Planning Division. He is responsible for granting or denying these requests. Don't forget to sign at the end.

options.text: Muito obrigado pela sua ajuda e pela clareza das suas explicações. Com os melhores cumprimentos.
options.translation: Thank you very much for your help and the clarity of your explanations. With best regards.
options.feedback: Perfect! You used a polite and formal closing, showing appreciation. This concludes the interaction effectively.

options.text: Ok, obrigado. Já posso ir fazer isso.
options.translation: Okay, thanks. I can go do that now.
options.feedback: While polite, 'Com os melhores cumprimentos' would be a more appropriate formal closing in this context.

mission.title: Drafting a Requerimento for a Local Issue
prep: Identify a hypothetical local issue in Madeira that would require a formal request to the Câmara Municipal (e.g., requesting a tree trimming, reporting a public light outage, asking for a copy of a property document).
Draft the opening of your requerimento using 'Exmo. Senhor/Senhora' and the appropriate title.
Formulate the body of your request clearly and concisely, using formal language.
Include a sentence mentioning any hypothetical 'anexos' (attachments).
Conclude with a formal closing, such as 'Com os melhores cumprimentos'.
fallback_phrases: Peço desculpa, pode explicar de outra forma?
Não percebi bem o que devo fazer.
likely_responses: Qual é o assunto do seu pedido?
O prazo de resposta é de X dias.

goals: Understand the structure and purpose of a 'requerimento' in Portuguese administrative contexts.
Formulate and interpret formal openings and closings in written communication.
Express a precise request using appropriate formal vocabulary and syntax.
Identify key terms and outcomes in official replies (e.g., 'deferir', 'indeferir', 'despacho').
Navigate a formal interaction at a public service counter with confidence.

review_items.prompt: Say in Portuguese: Dear Sir/Madam {position},
Say in Portuguese: I hereby {action}.
Say in Portuguese: I thank Your Excellency for the attention given.
review_items.answer: Agradeço a V. Exa. a atenção dispensada.

Recall the Portuguese for: application / formal petition
Recall the Portuguese for: Your Excellency (abbreviation)
Recall the Portuguese for: to grant / to approve (a request)
Recall the Portuguese for: to deny / to reject (a request)

situations.title: Reading the Contract
situations.summary: A lease or service contract in front of you: the clauses that matter (prazo, rescisão, fiador), asking for plain-language explanations, and querying a clause before signing.

phrase_patterns.base: Gostaria de pedir um esclarecimento sobre a cláusula {numero}.
phrase_patterns.translation: I would like to ask for a clarification about clause {number}.

variants.text: Poderia, por favor, esclarecer esta cláusula?
variants.translation: Could you please clarify this clause?

phrase_patterns.base: O que significa exatamente o termo '{termo}' nesta secção?
phrase_patterns.translation: What exactly does the term '{term}' mean in this section?

variants.text: Podia explicar-me o que quer dizer '{termo}'?
variants.translation: Could you explain to me what '{term}' means?

phrase_patterns.base: Qual é o prazo de duração deste contrato de {tipo_contrato}?
phrase_patterns.translation: What is the duration of this {contract_type} contract?

phrase_patterns.base: E em relação à rescisão antecipada, quais são as condições?
phrase_patterns.translation: And regarding early termination, what are the conditions?

variants.text: Se eu quiser rescindir antes do tempo, como é que funciona?
variants.translation: If I want to terminate early, how does it work?

phrase_patterns.base: É obrigatório ter um fiador para este tipo de contrato?
phrase_patterns.translation: Is it mandatory to have a guarantor for this type of contract?

variants.translation: Do I really need a guarantor?
variants.note: More direct, less formal

vocabulary.note: A specific article or condition in a contract.
The period of time for which a contract is valid.
The act of ending a contract.
A person who guarantees to pay a tenant's rent if they default.
Money paid as a guarantee against damage or non-payment.

dialogues.title: Lease Contract Discussion
dialogues.context: You are at a real estate agency in Funchal, reviewing a lease contract for an apartment. You're speaking with the agent, who is explaining some clauses.

lines.speaker: Agente Imobiliário
lines.text: Então, como vê, o contrato tem uma duração de três anos, renovável automaticamente por iguais períodos, a não ser que haja denúncia.
lines.translation: So, as you see, the contract has a duration of three years, automatically renewable for equal periods, unless there is a termination notice.

lines.speaker: Arrendatário
lines.text: Pois, estou a ver. E em relação à cláusula quinta, sobre a rescisão antecipada, quais são as condições exatas?
lines.translation: Right, I see. And regarding clause five, about early termination, what are the exact conditions?

lines.speaker: Agente Imobiliário
lines.text: A rescisão antecipada pelo arrendatário, após os primeiros seis meses, exige um pré-aviso de 120 dias, por carta registada. Antes disso, há uma penalização.
lines.translation: Early termination by the tenant, after the first six months, requires 120 days' notice, by registered letter. Before that, there's a penalty.

lines.speaker: Arrendatário
lines.text: Certo. E o que significa exatamente 'prazo de fidelização' na cláusula sétima? É diferente da duração do contrato?
lines.translation: Okay. And what exactly does 'loyalty period' mean in clause seven? Is it different from the contract duration?

lines.speaker: Agente Imobiliário
lines.text: Ah, sim. O prazo de fidelização é o período mínimo em que o contrato não pode ser rescindido sem penalização. Neste caso, é de 12 meses. É um bocado diferente do prazo total.
lines.translation: Ah, yes. The loyalty period is the minimum period during which the contract cannot be terminated without penalty. In this case, it's 12 months. It's a bit different from the total term.

lines.speaker: Arrendatário
lines.text: Percebo. E quanto ao fiador? É estritamente necessário ou há alternativas?
lines.translation: I understand. And what about the guarantor? Is it strictly necessary or are there alternatives?

lines.speaker: Agente Imobiliário
lines.text: Para este imóvel, a proprietária exige fiador. Em alternativa, poderíamos negociar um aumento da caução, mas é menos comum. Diga-me se tem dificuldades.
lines.translation: For this property, the owner requires a guarantor. Alternatively, we could negotiate an increased deposit, but it's less common. Tell me if you have difficulties.

cultural_notes.title: Understanding Contracts in Portugal
cultural_notes.body: In Portugal, as in many European countries, contracts (especially for long-term services like leases, internet, or mobile phones) often include 'prazos de fidelização' (loyalty periods) during which early termination incurs penalties. It's crucial to understand these terms. Asking for clarification on any clause is not only acceptable but expected, especially if the language is formal or technical. Don't be afraid to ask 'O que significa...?' or 'Poderia esclarecer...?'

cultural_notes.title: The Role of a 'Fiador'
cultural_notes.body: A 'fiador' (guarantor) is a common requirement for lease agreements in Portugal, particularly for tenants without a stable work history in the country or without a significant upfront payment. The fiador is legally responsible for the tenant's obligations if they fail to meet them. If you don't have a fiador, sometimes an increased security deposit ('caução') can be negotiated, but this depends on the landlord and agency.

roleplay.scenario: You are at a utility company office in Funchal, trying to sign up for a new internet and TV package. The service agent has presented you with the contract, and you're reviewing it before signing. You notice a clause about 'instalação' (installation) that seems unclear.

nodes.npc_text: Aqui está o contrato, como combinado. Peço que o leia com atenção antes de assinar. Se tiver alguma dúvida, diga, por favor.
nodes.npc_translation: Here is the contract, as agreed. Please read it carefully before signing. If you have any questions, please say so.

options.text: Obrigado. Estou a ler. Em relação à cláusula sobre a instalação, gostaria de um esclarecimento.
options.translation: Thank you. I'm reading. Regarding the clause about installation, I would like a clarification.
options.feedback: Excellent start, polite and direct to the point.

options.text: Hum, isto é um bocado confuso. O que é esta parte da instalação?
options.translation: Hmm, this is a bit confusing. What's this part about installation?
options.feedback: Acceptable, but 'um bocado confuso' is less formal than 'gostaria de um esclarecimento'.

nodes.npc_text: Claro. Qual é a sua dúvida específica sobre a instalação? Refere-se à taxa ou ao prazo?
nodes.npc_translation: Of course. What is your specific question about the installation? Does it refer to the fee or the deadline?

options.text: Refere-se à taxa. A cláusula diz 'custos de instalação podem ser aplicados'. Isso significa que podem ou que serão aplicados?
options.translation: It refers to the fee. The clause says 'installation costs may be applied'. Does that mean they may or that they will be applied?
options.feedback: Very good, you've identified the ambiguity and asked for a precise explanation.

options.text: Não percebi bem se tenho de pagar a instalação. É grátis ou não?
options.translation: I didn't quite understand if I have to pay for the installation. Is it free or not?
options.feedback: Clear question, but slightly less formal than option 1. Still effective.

nodes.npc_text: Ah, percebo a sua dúvida. 'Podem ser aplicados' significa que, para clientes novos, a instalação é gratuita. Apenas em casos de instalações especiais ou re-instalações é que há custos. Para si, será gratuita.
nodes.npc_translation: Ah, I understand your question. 'May be applied' means that, for new customers, installation is free. Only in cases of special installations or re-installations are there costs. For you, it will be free.

options.text: Muito obrigado pelo esclarecimento. Fico mais tranquilo assim. Posso continuar a ler.
options.translation: Thank you very much for the clarification. I feel more at ease now. I can continue reading.
options.feedback: Excellent, showing appreciation and readiness to proceed. This is a good terminal point.

options.text: Ok, entendi. Então não preciso de me preocupar com isso.
options.translation: Okay, I understood. So I don't need to worry about that.
options.feedback: Good, you've confirmed understanding. A bit less formal than option 1, but still effective.

mission.title: Deciphering a Service Contract
prep: Find a real-life service contract (e.g., mobile phone, internet, gym membership) in Portuguese, or ask a Portuguese friend to share one.
Identify three key clauses that you find important or potentially confusing (e.g., duration, cancellation, fees).
Formulate questions in Portuguese for each clause, using phrases like 'Gostaria de um esclarecimento sobre...', 'O que significa o termo...?', or 'Quais são as condições para...?'
Practice asking these questions aloud, imagining you are speaking to a service agent.
fallback_phrases: Desculpe, não percebi bem. Poderia repetir, por favor?
Poderia explicar isso de outra forma?
Não entendi o que significa essa parte.
likely_responses: Isso aplica-se apenas em casos específicos.

goals: Understand common legal and administrative terms in contracts.
Be able to ask for clarification on specific clauses in a formal context.
Identify and query ambiguous or potentially problematic terms before signing a contract.
Feel confident navigating administrative interactions involving formal documents.

review_items.prompt: Say in Portuguese: I would like to ask for a clarification about clause {number}.
review_items.answer: Gostaria de pedir um esclarecimento sobre a cláusula {numero}.

review_items.prompt: Say in Portuguese: What exactly does the term '{term}' mean in this section?
review_items.answer: O que significa exatamente o termo '{termo}' nesta secção?

review_items.prompt: Say in Portuguese: What is the duration of this {contract_type} contract?
review_items.answer: Qual é o prazo de duração deste contrato de {tipo_contrato}?

review_items.prompt: Say in Portuguese: And regarding early termination, what are the conditions?
review_items.answer: E em relação à rescisão antecipada, quais são as condições?

review_items.prompt: Recall the Portuguese for: clause
Recall the Portuguese for: term / duration
Recall the Portuguese for: termination / rescission
Recall the Portuguese for: guarantor
