situations.title: Gossip & Discretion
situations.summary: Village dynamics: recognising a conversa fiada, deflecting questions you would rather not answer, keeping confidences, and staying warm without feeding the mill.

phrase_patterns.base: Não estou a par dos {detalhes}, para ser sincero/a.
phrase_patterns.translation: I'm not aware of the {details}, to be honest.

variants.text: Não estou a par, para ser sincero/a.
variants.translation: I'm not aware, to be honest.

phrase_patterns.translation: I prefer not to {comment} on that.

phrase_patterns.base: São assuntos {pessoais}, não me parece bem falar.
phrase_patterns.translation: They are {personal} matters, I don't think it's right to talk about them.

phrase_patterns.base: Obrigado/a pela preocupação, mas está tudo bem.
phrase_patterns.translation: Thanks for your concern, but everything is fine.

vocabulary.note: Common idiom in Portugal, especially in smaller communities.
Literally 'to give rope'.
Common Madeiran reduction of 'para já'.

dialogues.title: Café da Aldeia
dialogues.context: Two neighbours, Dona Lúcia and Maria, are at the local café. Dona Lúcia is known for her 'conversa fiada'.

lines.speaker: Dona Lúcia
lines.text: Então, Maria, já soube da Ana, a filha da Teresa? Diz que arranjou um novo namorado, mas ninguém o conhece...
lines.translation: So, Maria, have you heard about Ana, Teresa's daughter? They say she got a new boyfriend, but nobody knows him...

lines.speaker: Maria
lines.text: Pois, ouvi dizer qualquer coisa. Mas não estou a par dos detalhes, Dona Lúcia. São assuntos dela, não é?
lines.translation: Well, I heard something. But I'm not aware of the details, Dona Lúcia. They're her matters, aren't they?

lines.speaker: Dona Lúcia
lines.text: Ah, pois... Mas dizem que ele é de fora, e que a Teresa não gostou muito. É verdade?
lines.translation: Ah, well... But they say he's from outside, and that Teresa didn't like him much. Is it true?

lines.speaker: Maria
lines.text: Olhe, Dona Lúcia, prefiro não comentar sobre isso. Se a Ana quiser falar, ela fala, certo? Como é que está o seu neto, p'ra já?
lines.translation: Look, Dona Lúcia, I prefer not to comment on that. If Ana wants to talk, she'll talk, right? How's your grandson doing, for now?

lines.speaker: Dona Lúcia
lines.text: Ah, o João? O João está bem, está a estudar muito, graças a Deus... Mas voltando à Ana...
lines.translation: Ah, João? João is fine, he's studying a lot, thank God... But back to Ana...

lines.speaker: Maria
lines.text: Diga! Preciso de ir, Dona Lúcia. Tenho umas coisas p'ra fazer em casa. Até já!
lines.translation: Say! I need to go, Dona Lúcia. I have some things to do at home. See you later!

cultural_notes.title: The Art of Discretion in Madeiran Communities
cultural_notes.body: In smaller communities, like many villages in Madeira, 'conversa fiada' (gossip) is a common social activity. While it can be a way to connect, it can also lead to uncomfortable situations or spread misinformation. Locals often develop a subtle art of deflecting questions about others' private lives without appearing rude or uncooperative. Direct refusal can be seen as impolite, so indirectness, changing the subject, or expressing a lack of knowledge are preferred strategies. Maintaining discretion is highly valued, especially regarding family or personal matters.

cultural_notes.title: Avoiding 'Dar Corda' to Gossip
cultural_notes.body: To 'dar corda' (literally 'to give rope') to gossip means to encourage it or provide more fuel for it. When confronted with 'conversa fiada', a common strategy is to avoid 'dar corda' by not offering new information, not confirming rumours, and gently steering the conversation away. This allows one to remain part of the social fabric without actively participating in or spreading potentially harmful information. Phrases like 'Não estou a par dos detalhes' or 'Prefiro não comentar' are polite ways to signal discretion.

roleplay.scenario: You're at the local 'mercearia' (small grocery store) in your village. The owner, a friendly but curious man named Senhor João, tries to get information about your neighbour, Miguel, who recently started a new, somewhat mysterious, job.

nodes.npc_text: Olá! Como é que está? Então, o Miguel, seu vizinho, anda muito ocupado, não é? Dizem que arranjou um emprego novo, mas ninguém sabe bem o que é...
nodes.npc_translation: Hello! How are you? So, Miguel, your neighbour, is very busy, isn't he? They say he got a new job, but nobody really knows what it is...

options.text: Pois, Senhor João, ele está a trabalhar muito. Mas não sei muito sobre o que ele faz, para ser sincero.
options.translation: Well, Senhor João, he's working a lot. But I don't know much about what he does, to be honest.
options.feedback: Excellent! You've deflected politely by stating lack of knowledge without being abrupt.

options.text: Sim, ele está a trabalhar. Não sei se posso falar sobre o trabalho dele.
options.translation: Yes, he's working. I don't know if I can talk about his job.
options.feedback: A bit too direct, implying you DO know something but won't share. This might pique his curiosity more.

options.text: Ele é que sabe da vida dele. Não me meto nisso.
options.translation: He knows his own life. I don't get involved in that.
options.feedback: This is a bit blunt and might be perceived as rude or dismissive in a small community context.

nodes.npc_text: Ah, entendo. Mas ele parece contente, não parece? E com quem é que ele anda a trabalhar? É cá na zona?
nodes.npc_translation: Ah, I understand. But he seems happy, doesn't he? And who is he working with? Is it around here?

options.text: Parece-me que sim, ele está bem. Mas são assuntos pessoais dele, não é? Não me parece bem falar.
options.translation: It seems so, he's fine. But they are his personal matters, aren't they? I don't think it's right to talk about it.
options.feedback: Very good! You've reiterated discretion and framed it as a matter of principle.

options.text: Não sei. Ele não me contou os detalhes. Mas diga-me, Senhor João, como é que está a sua horta este ano?
options.translation: I don't know. He didn't tell me the details. But tell me, Senhor João, how's your vegetable garden this year?
options.feedback: Excellent! You've deflected and skillfully changed the subject to something the NPC is likely to enjoy talking about.

nodes.npc_text: Então, sabes de alguma coisa! Anda, conta lá. Não faz mal, somos vizinhos!
nodes.npc_translation: So, you know something! Come on, tell me. It's fine, we're neighbours!

options.text: Não, a sério, não sei nada de especial. Prefiro não comentar sobre a vida dos outros.
options.translation: No, seriously, I don't know anything special. I prefer not to comment on other people's lives.
options.feedback: Good recovery! You've firmly but politely re-established your boundary.

nodes.npc_text: Ah, está bem, está bem... Mas não precisas de ser tão sério/a. É só conversa, não é?
nodes.npc_translation: Ah, okay, okay... But you don't need to be so serious. It's just talk, isn't it?

options.text: Peço desculpa, Senhor João. Não queria ser rude. É que são assuntos dele.
options.translation: I'm sorry, Senhor João. I didn't mean to be rude. It's just that they are his matters.
options.feedback: Good apology and clarification. It's important to repair social ties.

nodes.npc_text: Ah, a minha horta! Este ano, com a chuva que caiu, as abóboras estão enormes! Queres levar umas?
nodes.npc_translation: Ah, my garden! This year, with all the rain, the pumpkins are huge! Do you want to take some?

options.text: Claro, Senhor João! Muito obrigado! Que maravilha!
options.translation: Of course, Senhor João! Thank you very much! How wonderful!
options.feedback: Perfect! You successfully diverted the conversation and strengthened your relationship with the neighbour.

nodes.npc_text: Está bem, está bem. Entendo. Cada um com a sua vida, não é? Mas se souberes de algo, diz-me!
nodes.npc_translation: Okay, okay. I understand. Everyone with their own life, right? But if you know anything, tell me!

options.text: Sim, claro. Mas acho que o Miguel prefere manter as coisas mais privadas. Até à próxima, Senhor João!
options.translation: Yes, of course. But I think Miguel prefers to keep things more private. See you next time, Senhor João!
options.feedback: You've ended the conversation gracefully, reiterating the importance of privacy. Well done!

mission.title: Navigating Village Gossip with Grace
prep: Rehearse the phrases for deflecting questions and expressing discretion.
Observe how locals in Madeira handle 'conversa fiada' – notice their tone, body language, and choice of words.
Mentally prepare for situations where you might be asked about others' personal lives.
fallback_phrases: Não estou a par dos detalhes.
Não sei nada sobre isso, lamento.
likely_responses: Ah, então não sabes de nada?
Está bem, está bem, não faz mal.
Tu és muito discreto/a, não é?

goals: Recognise attempts at 'conversa fiada' (gossip) in social interactions.
Employ discreet and polite language to deflect personal questions about others.
Maintain social harmony in a community while protecting personal privacy.
Understand the cultural nuances of discretion and 'dar corda' (encouraging) gossip in Madeira.

situations.title: Helping a Newcomer — in Portuguese
situations.summary: The full circle: explaining Madeira life to a newly-arrived foreigner in Portuguese — the systems, the customs, the words — and translating culture in both directions.

phrase_patterns.base: Aqui na Madeira, é comum {fazer algo}.
phrase_patterns.translation: Here in Madeira, it's common to {do something}.

slots.name: fazer algo
options: ir à praia no fim de semana

variants.text: É um costume aqui na Madeira {fazer algo}.
variants.translation: It's a custom here in Madeira to {do something}.

phrase_patterns.base: Para {resolver problema}, precisa de {documento ou informação}.
phrase_patterns.translation: To {solve problem}, you need {document or information}.

slots.name: resolver problema
slots.description: a problem to solve or task to complete

slots.name: documento ou informação
slots.description: a document or piece of information required
options: o teu passaporte e um comprovativo de morada
a tua identificação e o NISS

phrase_patterns.base: Não se preocupe, é {fácil/normal} habituar-se aos {costumes/sistemas}.
phrase_patterns.translation: Don't worry, it's {easy/normal} to get used to the {customs/systems}.

phrase_patterns.base: É um bocado diferente do que se vê em {país/região}.
phrase_patterns.translation: It's a bit different from what you see in {country/region}.

vocabulary.translation: Tax Identification Number (Número de Identificação Fiscal)
vocabulary.note: Essential for almost any administrative task in Portugal, including Madeira.

vocabulary.translation: Social Security Identification Number (Número de Identificação da Segurança Social)
vocabulary.note: Required for employment and accessing social security benefits.
A utility bill or rental contract is usually accepted.
Madeiran regionalism for 'batata'.
Common term for espresso in Portugal, including Madeira.

dialogues.title: Explaining the NIF
dialogues.context: A local Madeiran, Ana, is having coffee with a newly arrived expat, Mark, who is trying to sort out his documentation.

lines.speaker: Mark
lines.text: Olá Ana! Estou a ficar um bocado frustrado com toda a burocracia aqui. O NIF, o NISS... É tudo tão confuso!
lines.translation: Hi Ana! I'm getting a bit frustrated with all the bureaucracy here. The NIF, the NISS... It's all so confusing!

lines.speaker: Ana
lines.text: Pois é, no início parece um bicho de sete cabeças, mas não te preocupes, é mais fácil do que parece. Já foste às Finanças para o NIF?
lines.translation: Yeah, at first it seems like a big monster, but don't worry, it's easier than it looks. Have you been to the Tax Office for the NIF yet?

lines.speaker: Mark
lines.text: Ainda não. Onde é que fica? E o que é que preciso de levar?
lines.translation: Not yet. Where is it? And what do I need to bring?

lines.speaker: Ana
lines.text: Fica ali perto do Mercado dos Lavradores. Precisas do teu passaporte e um comprovativo de morada. Se não tiveres, podes levar uma declaração da tua senhoria ou do hotel, se lá estiveres.
lines.translation: It's near the Farmers' Market. You need your passport and proof of address. If you don't have one, you can take a declaration from your landlady or the hotel, if you're staying there.

lines.speaker: Mark
lines.text: Ah, ok. E o NISS? É no mesmo sítio?
lines.translation: Ah, okay. And the NISS? Is it in the same place?

lines.speaker: Ana
lines.text: Não, o NISS é na Segurança Social. Mas primeiro trata do NIF, que é o mais importante para tudo. Depois, com o NIF, já é mais simples.
lines.translation: No, the NISS is at Social Security. But first, deal with the NIF, which is the most important for everything. Then, with the NIF, it's simpler.

lines.speaker: Mark
lines.text: E paciência, um bocado de paciência é sempre bom com a burocracia portuguesa, ou não?
lines.translation: And patience, a bit of patience is always good with Portuguese bureaucracy, right?

dialogues.title: Local Customs
dialogues.context: At a café, a local, Sofia, is chatting with a newcomer, Liam, about daily life in Madeira.

lines.speaker: Liam
lines.text: Estou a adorar a Madeira, mas ainda estou a habituar-me a algumas coisas. Por exemplo, a hora do almoço. É sempre tão tarde!
lines.translation: I'm loving Madeira, but I'm still getting used to some things. For example, lunchtime. It's always so late!

lines.speaker: Sofia
lines.text: Ah, sim! É um bocado diferente do que se vê noutros países, não é? Aqui é comum almoçar entre a uma e as duas da tarde. E o jantar, mais tarde ainda!
lines.translation: Oh, yes! It's a bit different from what you see in other countries, isn't it? Here it's common to have lunch between one and two in the afternoon. And dinner, even later!

lines.speaker: Liam
lines.text: Pois é. E o café? Toda a gente bebe bica a qualquer hora do dia. É impressionante!
lines.translation: Yeah. And coffee? Everyone drinks espresso at any time of day. It's impressive!

lines.speaker: Sofia
lines.text: É verdade! A bica faz parte do nosso dia a dia. É quase um ritual social. E se fores a um café, não te assustes se o pessoal te cumprimentar. Aqui somos muito de conversar com quem está à nossa volta.
lines.translation: It's true! Espresso is part of our daily life. It's almost a social ritual. And if you go to a café, don't be surprised if people greet you. Here we're very much about talking to those around us.

lines.speaker: Liam
lines.text: Isso é ótimo, gosto disso! Mas às vezes sinto que não percebo tudo o que dizem, com o sotaque e as expressões locais...
lines.translation: That's great, I like that! But sometimes I feel like I don't understand everything they say, with the accent and the local expressions...

lines.speaker: Sofia
lines.text: Não te preocupes, é normal! Com o tempo, vais habituar-te. Se precisares, pergunta sempre. Estamos cá para ajudar!
lines.translation: Don't worry, that's normal! With time, you'll get used to it. If you need, always ask. We're here to help!

cultural_notes.title: The Importance of NIF and NISS
cultural_notes.body: In Portugal, including Madeira, the NIF (Número de Identificação Fiscal - Tax Identification Number) and NISS (Número de Identificação da Segurança Social - Social Security Identification Number) are fundamental. The NIF is required for almost every administrative action: opening a bank account, signing a rental contract, buying property, getting a phone plan, even buying a car. The NISS is essential for employment and accessing social services. It's crucial for newcomers to understand how to obtain these and their pervasive necessity.

cultural_notes.title: Patience with Bureaucracy
cultural_notes.body: Portuguese bureaucracy can be slow and requires patience. Documents might be requested multiple times, and processes might take longer than expected. It's common to hear 'com paciência' (with patience) when dealing with administrative tasks. Maintaining a polite and calm demeanor, and being prepared with all necessary documents (and copies!), can help navigate these systems more smoothly.

roleplay.scenario: You are at a local café in Funchal, and a new expat, David, approaches you, looking a bit overwhelmed. He's struggling to understand how to set up his utilities (water, electricity) in his new apartment.

nodes.npc_text: Olá! Desculpa incomodar, mas pareces ser local. Estou a tentar ligar a água e a eletricidade no meu apartamento novo e estou um bocado perdido. Podes dar-me uma ajuda?
nodes.npc_translation: Hello! Sorry to bother you, but you seem to be a local. I'm trying to connect the water and electricity in my new apartment and I'm a bit lost. Can you help me?

options.text: Claro que sim! É um bocado confuso no início. Já tens o NIF?
options.translation: Of course! It's a bit confusing at first. Do you already have your NIF?
options.feedback: Good opening! You've acknowledged his struggle and immediately identified a key requirement.

options.text: Sim, claro! É normal sentir-se assim. Onde é que é o teu apartamento?
options.translation: Yes, of course! It's normal to feel that way. Where is your apartment?
options.feedback: A kind and empathetic start, but you might need to get to the core requirements soon.

nodes.npc_text: O NIF? Sim, já o tenho! Demorou um bocado, mas consegui. Porquê? É preciso para a água e a eletricidade?
nodes.npc_translation: The NIF? Yes, I have it! It took a while, but I got it. Why? Is it needed for water and electricity?

options.text: Sim, é fundamental. Para a água, tens de ir à Águas do Funchal. Para a eletricidade, é na EEM.
options.translation: Yes, it's essential. For water, you have to go to Águas do Funchal. For electricity, it's at EEM.
options.feedback: Excellent, you've provided the specific agencies needed.

options.text: Sim, é preciso para quase tudo aqui. Mas não te preocupes, com o NIF já tens meio caminho andado.
options.translation: Yes, it's needed for almost everything here. But don't worry, with the NIF you're halfway there.
options.feedback: Good reassurance, but try to be more specific about the next steps.

nodes.npc_text: Fica na zona da Ajuda. É um apartamento alugado. O senhorio disse que eu tinha de tratar disso tudo sozinho.
nodes.npc_translation: It's in the Ajuda area. It's a rented apartment. The landlord said I had to deal with all of that myself.

options.text: Ah, ok. Então, primeiro, precisas do NIF, se ainda não o tiveres. Para a água, é na Águas do Funchal e para a luz, na EEM.
options.translation: Ah, okay. So, first, you need your NIF, if you don't have it yet. For water, it's at Águas do Funchal and for electricity, at EEM.
options.feedback: You've correctly identified the NIF as a priority and named the agencies.

nodes.npc_text: Pois, mas ainda não sei onde ir ou o que levar. É tudo novo para mim.
nodes.npc_translation: Right, but I still don't know where to go or what to bring. It's all new to me.

options.text: Ok, então, para a água, tens de ir à Águas do Funchal. Para a eletricidade, é na EEM. Leva o teu NIF, o passaporte e o contrato de arrendamento.
options.translation: Okay, so for water, you have to go to Águas do Funchal. For electricity, it's at EEM. Bring your NIF, passport, and rental contract.
options.feedback: Excellent, you've provided specific locations and required documents.

nodes.npc_text: Águas do Funchal e EEM... Ok. E que documentos é que eles pedem? É que o meu português ainda não é o melhor para perceber tudo.
nodes.npc_translation: Águas do Funchal and EEM... Okay. And what documents do they ask for? My Portuguese isn't the best to understand everything.

options.text: Sim, é normal. Vão pedir-te o NIF, o teu passaporte ou cartão de cidadão, e o contrato de arrendamento do apartamento. É bom levar também um comprovativo de morada, se tiveres.
options.translation: Yes, that's normal. They will ask for your NIF, your passport or citizen card, and the apartment rental contract. It's good to also bring proof of address, if you have one.
options.feedback: Great, you've listed the essential documents clearly.

nodes.npc_text: Ah, ok, isso ajuda imenso! Muito obrigado! Se calhar, vou tentar ir lá esta semana. É um bocado de burocracia, mas com estas informações já me sinto mais à vontade.
nodes.npc_translation: Ah, okay, that helps immensely! Thank you very much! Maybe I'll try to go there this week. It's a bit of bureaucracy, but with this information, I feel more comfortable.

options.text: De nada! Qualquer coisa, é só perguntar. E sim, um bocado de paciência ajuda sempre com a burocracia portuguesa!
options.translation: You're welcome! Anything else, just ask. And yes, a bit of patience always helps with Portuguese bureaucracy!
options.feedback: A friendly and helpful closing, reinforcing a key cultural point.

options.text: Ainda bem que pude ajudar! Boa sorte com isso e não te preocupes, é fácil habituar-se.
options.translation: Glad I could help! Good luck with that and don't worry, it's easy to get used to it.
options.feedback: A positive and encouraging final remark.

mission.title: Explaining Utility Setup to a Newcomer
prep: Review the names of key public service entities in Funchal (e.g., Águas do Funchal, EEM).
Practice explaining the purpose of the NIF and NISS.
Rehearse giving directions and listing required documents clearly in Portuguese.
fallback_phrases: Desculpa, não sei bem como explicar isso em português.
Não tenho a certeza, mas posso tentar descobrir para ti.
É um bocado complicado, mas é preciso ter paciência.
likely_responses: E que documentos é que preciso de levar?

goals: Successfully explain the process for setting up utilities (water, electricity) to a newcomer in Portuguese, including required documents and relevant agencies.
Use appropriate vocabulary related to bureaucracy and public services.
Offer cultural context and reassurance regarding the administrative process in Madeira.

review_items.answer: Aqui na Madeira, é comum {fazer algo}.

review_items.answer: Não se preocupe, é {fácil/normal} habituar-se aos {costumes/sistemas}.

review_items.prompt: Say in Portuguese: It's a bit different from what you see in {country/region}.

situations.title: Talking with the Older Generation
situations.summary: Conversations with older Madeirans: faster, more idiomatic, more indirect — respect forms, following stories of the old days, and asking the questions that open people up.

phrase_patterns.translation: It's always good to know the history.

phrase_patterns.base: Como é que era {a vida} {por cá} antigamente?
phrase_patterns.translation: How was life around here back then?

phrase_patterns.base: Ainda se lembra de quando {aqui não havia nada}?
phrase_patterns.translation: Do you still remember when there was nothing here?

variants.text: Ainda se lembra de quando tudo era diferente?
variants.translation: Do you still remember when everything was different?

variants.text: O que é que sente mais falta desses tempos?
variants.translation: What do you miss most about those times?
variants.note: More direct, asks about feelings.

vocabulary.note: Madeira regionalism
Portugal regionalism (Lisbon/Madeira common)
Common agreement interjection in Portugal
Madeira regionalism for 'batatas'

dialogues.title: A Bica e as Histórias Antigas
dialogues.context: At a small, traditional café in Funchal, the learner is having a bica and strikes up a conversation with Dona Fátima, an older woman who is a regular.

lines.speaker: Dona Fátima
lines.text: Ah, uma bica a esta hora. É o que me anima p'ra continuar o dia.
lines.translation: Ah, an espresso at this hour. It's what cheers me up to continue the day.

lines.speaker: Learner
lines.text: É verdade, Dona Fátima. Nada como um bom café. Há quanto tempo vem aqui?
lines.translation: That's true, Dona Fátima. Nothing like a good coffee. How long have you been coming here?

lines.speaker: Dona Fátima
lines.text: Ui, minha filha/meu filho, desde que me lembro! Este café já era o ponto de encontro da gente do bairro antigamente. As coisas mudaram imenso, mas este cantinho mantém-se.
lines.translation: Oh, my dear (daughter/son), as long as I can remember! This café was already the meeting point for the neighborhood folks back then. Things have changed immensely, but this little corner remains.

lines.speaker: Learner
lines.text: Que interessante! Como é que era o bairro por cá antigamente? Deve ter muitas histórias.
lines.translation: How interesting! What was the neighborhood like around here back then? You must have many stories.

lines.speaker: Dona Fátima
lines.text: Pois é. Antigamente, éramos todos uma família. As portas estavam sempre abertas, e as crianças brincavam na rua sem perigo. Não havia telemóveis, falávamos uns com os outros, cara a cara.
lines.translation: Indeed. Back then, we were all one family. Doors were always open, and children played in the street without danger. There were no mobile phones, we talked to each other, face to face.

lines.speaker: Learner
lines.text: Sempre é bom saber a história. Parece que havia um sentido de comunidade muito forte.
lines.translation: It's always good to know the history. It seems there was a very strong sense of community.

lines.speaker: Dona Fátima
lines.text: Com certeza. E o trabalho era duro, mas havia mais tempo p'ra conversar. As levadas eram a nossa rede social, sabe? Era lá que se encontrava a gente.
lines.translation: Certainly. And work was hard, but there was more time to talk. The levadas were our social network, you know? That's where people met.

cultural_notes.title: Respect and Politeness with Elders
cultural_notes.body: When speaking with older Madeirans, it's crucial to show respect. Use 'o senhor' or 'a senhora' even if they use informal 'tu' with you. Listen patiently to their stories, as they often contain valuable insights into local history and culture. Avoid interrupting and express genuine interest with phrases like 'Que interessante!' or 'É verdade!'. Asking about 'antigamente' (in the old days) is a common way to open up conversation and show you value their experience.

cultural_notes.title: The Art of Madeiran Storytelling
cultural_notes.body: Older Madeirans often tell stories in a less direct, more narrative way. They might not answer a question immediately but instead launch into an anecdote or a description of the past. This is a form of communication that values the journey of the story as much as the factual information. Phrases like 'Pois é' (Indeed) or 'Com certeza' (Certainly) are good ways to acknowledge and encourage them to continue, even if you're not fully following every detail.

roleplay.scenario: You are at a local market in Funchal, buying some 'semilhas' (potatoes) from Sr. Manuel, an older vendor. He starts reminiscing about how things used to be.

nodes.npc_text: Estas semilhas, ó meu caro/minha cara, já não são o que eram! Antigamente, eram cultivadas com tanto sacrifício, naquelas terras mais acima. Era um trabalho duro, mas dava gosto.
nodes.npc_translation: These potatoes, my dear, they're not what they used to be! Back then, they were cultivated with such sacrifice, in those higher lands. It was hard work, but it was satisfying.

options.text: É verdade, Sr. Manuel. Como era o trabalho no campo antigamente? Devia ser muito diferente.
options.translation: That's true, Sr. Manuel. What was farm work like back then? It must have been very different.
options.feedback: Excellent! You showed interest and asked an open-ended question about the past, encouraging him to elaborate.

options.text: Pois é, a vida mudou imenso. Mas estas semilhas parecem ótimas, obrigado.
options.translation: Indeed, life has changed immensely. But these potatoes look great, thank you.
options.feedback: Good acknowledgment, but you closed the door a bit on further conversation. You could have asked more about the past.

nodes.npc_text: Ah, era outra coisa! Subia-se a pé com as enxadas às costas, de sol a sol. A gente ajudava-se uns aos outros, na 'mútua'. Era cansativo, mas havia um espírito de entreajuda que hoje já não se vê tanto.
nodes.npc_translation: Ah, it was something else! We'd walk up carrying hoes on our backs, from sunup to sundown. People helped each other, in 'mútua' (mutual aid). It was tiring, but there was a spirit of mutual help that you don't see as much today.

options.text: Que tempos difíceis! E essa 'mútua' era mesmo importante para a comunidade, não era?
options.translation: What difficult times! And that 'mutual aid' was really important for the community, wasn't it?
options.feedback: Perfect! You showed empathy and asked a follow-up about a specific cultural practice, demonstrating active listening.

options.text: Devia ser muito diferente do que é hoje. É bom ouvir estas histórias, Sr. Manuel.
options.translation: It must have been very different from today. It's good to hear these stories, Sr. Manuel.
options.feedback: Good, you acknowledged the difference and expressed appreciation. This keeps him talking without a specific new question.

nodes.npc_text: Sim, sim, a Madeira mudou muito. Hoje é tudo mais fácil, mas também mais impessoal, não é? Mas vá, leve as semilhas que estão fresquinhas!
nodes.npc_translation: Yes, yes, Madeira has changed a lot. Today everything is easier, but also more impersonal, isn't it? But go on, take the potatoes, they're fresh!

options.text: Ainda se lembra de algum momento em particular que marcou essa mudança, Sr. Manuel?
options.translation: Do you still remember any particular moment that marked that change, Sr. Manuel?
options.feedback: Good attempt to bring him back to storytelling. He might pick up on a specific memory now.

options.text: Sim, a Madeira mudou muito. Obrigado, Sr. Manuel, tenha um bom dia.
options.translation: Yes, Madeira has changed a lot. Thank you, Sr. Manuel, have a good day.
options.feedback: A polite and appropriate exit if you need to end the conversation. You acknowledged his point and thanked him.

nodes.npc_text: Era essencial! Sem a 'mútua', muitos não teriam sobrevivido. Partilhava-se tudo, a comida, o trabalho, as alegrias e as tristezas. Era a nossa segurança social, digamos assim.
nodes.npc_translation: It was essential! Without 'mutual aid', many wouldn't have survived. Everything was shared, food, work, joys and sorrows. It was our social security, so to speak.

options.text: É uma pena que se perca um bocado essa união. Obrigado por partilhar, Sr. Manuel.
options.translation: It's a shame that a bit of that unity is lost. Thank you for sharing, Sr. Manuel.
options.feedback: A heartfelt and respectful way to conclude the conversation, showing appreciation for his insights.

options.text: Sempre é bom saber a história. O que é que sente mais falta desses tempos?
options.translation: It's always good to know the history. What do you miss most about those times?
options.feedback: Excellent! You asked about his feelings, which often leads to deeper, more personal reflections and strengthens the connection.

nodes.npc_text: Falta-me a simplicidade, sabe? E o calor humano. Hoje é tudo mais rápido, mas por vezes sinto que nos perdemos uns dos outros. Mas obrigado por ouvir, é bom falar de outras coisas.
nodes.npc_translation: I miss the simplicity, you know? And human warmth. Today everything is faster, but sometimes I feel we've lost touch with each other. But thank you for listening, it's good to talk about other things.

options.text: Obrigado por partilhar, Sr. Manuel. É sempre bom aprender com a vossa experiência. Tenha um bom dia.
options.translation: Thank you for sharing, Sr. Manuel. It's always good to learn from your experience. Have a good day.
options.feedback: A perfect, warm, and respectful way to end the conversation, leaving a positive impression.

mission.title: Connect with a Madeiran Elder
prep: Identify an opportunity to chat with an older Madeiran (e.g., at a market, café, or shop).
Prepare a few open-ended questions about 'antigamente' (the old days), focusing on local life or traditions.
Practice using polite forms like 'o senhor'/'a senhora' and interjections like 'Pois é' or 'Que interessante!'
Be ready to listen patiently, even if the story meanders.
fallback_phrases: Foi um prazer ouvir as suas histórias.
likely_responses: É bom que os jovens se interessem.

goals: Initiate and sustain a polite conversation with an older Madeiran.
Use appropriate formal address ('o senhor'/'a senhora') and respectful language.
Ask open-ended questions to encourage storytelling about the past and local history.
Demonstrate active listening and appreciation for their experiences and insights.

situations.title: Week 23 Stress Test
situations.summary: A full social weekend: a festa with a toast, news talk at the café, a delicate condolence visit and an evening with older neighbours — social integration under pressure.

phrase_patterns.base: Os meus pêsames pela vossa perda.

variants.translation: I'm terribly sorry for your loss.
variants.note: A slightly softer, more empathetic tone.
A more general expression of sympathy.

slots.name: tópico
options: o que se passou na câmara

variants.translation: Do you know the news about {topic}?

phrase_patterns.base: Se precisares de alguma coisa, avisa.
phrase_patterns.translation: If you need anything, let me know.

variants.text: Conta comigo para o que for preciso.
variants.translation: Count on me for whatever is needed.
variants.note: More emphatic offer of support.

variants.text: Estou aqui para ajudar no que puder.
variants.translation: I'm here to help in any way I can.

vocabulary.note: Always plural.
Can refer to a small gathering or a large public festival.

vocabulary.note: Specific to Madeira and Azores.
Madeiran regionalism.

dialogues.title: Festa no Bairro
dialogues.context: A neighborhood festa in a local bar in Madeira. Friends are celebrating a small victory.

lines.speaker: Marta
lines.text: Então, malta! Um brinde à nossa equipa, que conseguiu o primeiro lugar no torneio de sueca!
lines.translation: Alright, folks! A toast to our team, who got first place in the 'sueca' tournament!

lines.speaker: Tiago
lines.text: Pois é! Bem merecido! Saúde a todos!
lines.translation: That's right! Well deserved! Cheers everyone!

lines.speaker: Lena
lines.text: Mais uma rodada de poncha para celebrar?
lines.translation: Another round of poncha to celebrate?

lines.speaker: Marta
lines.text: Claro! E que venham mais vitórias! Estou a ver que a noite vai ser longa!
lines.translation: Of course! And may more victories come! I can see it's going to be a long night!

lines.speaker: Tiago
lines.text: Ah, e a propósito, Lena, conseguiste arranjar aqueles bilhetes p'ra o concerto da próxima semana?
lines.translation: Ah, and by the way, Lena, did you manage to get those tickets for next week's concert?

dialogues.title: Notícias no Café
dialogues.context: At a local café, two older neighbours are discussing recent events over coffee.

lines.speaker: Sr. Manuel
lines.text: Bom dia, D. Rosa. Já ouviu falar daquele problema com a levada nova lá p'ra cima?
lines.translation: Good morning, Mrs. Rosa. Have you heard about that problem with the new levada up there?

lines.speaker: D. Rosa
lines.text: Diga? Não ouvi bem... O café tá uma barulheira hoje. Qual levada?
lines.translation: Say that again? I didn't hear well... The café is so noisy today. Which levada?

lines.speaker: Sr. Manuel
lines.text: A que estão a construir perto da serra. Dizem que há uns atrasos e desvios no orçamento.
lines.translation: The one they're building near the mountain. They say there are some delays and budget overruns.

lines.speaker: D. Rosa
lines.text: Ah, essa! Pois é, o meu genro já me tinha falado nisso. É uma vergonha, não é? Sempre a mesma história...
lines.translation: Ah, that one! Indeed, my son-in-law had already told me about it. It's a disgrace, isn't it? Always the same story...

lines.speaker: Sr. Manuel
lines.text: Exato. E com o dinheiro que aquilo vai custar, podiam era arranjar as estradas da nossa zona que estão uma lástima.
lines.translation: Exactly. And with the money that's going to cost, they could fix the roads in our area, which are a disaster.

cultural_notes.title: Toasting Etiquette in Portugal
cultural_notes.body: When making a toast ('um brinde'), it's customary to make eye contact with everyone whose glass you clink, or at least with the person you're toasting. You should also wait until everyone has clinked glasses before taking your first sip. Common toasts include 'Saúde!' (Cheers/To health!) or 'À nossa!' (To us!). At informal gatherings, just 'Saúde!' is perfectly acceptable.

cultural_notes.title: Condolence Visits: Sensitivity and Support
cultural_notes.body: Visiting someone who has lost a loved one is a delicate social situation. It's important to express genuine sympathy ('Os meus pêsames' or 'Sinto muito pela sua perda') and offer practical support ('Se precisar de alguma coisa, avise'). Keep the visit relatively brief, unless invited to stay longer. Avoid asking intrusive questions about the cause of death or dwelling on sad memories unless the bereaved person initiates it. The goal is to show support and respect for their grief, not to solve their problems or make them feel worse. A simple, heartfelt presence is often appreciated most.

roleplay.scenario: You are visiting your neighbour, D. Laura, whose sister recently passed away. You want to offer your condolences and support.

nodes.npc_text: Ah, bom dia. Entre, entre. Obrigada por ter vindo.
nodes.npc_translation: Ah, good morning. Come in, come in. Thank you for coming.

options.text: D. Laura, os meus pêsames pela sua perda. Sinto imenso.
options.translation: D. Laura, my condolences for your loss. I'm terribly sorry.
options.feedback: This is a direct and appropriate expression of sympathy. Good start.

options.text: Bom dia, D. Laura. Como está a correr tudo? Não a via há um tempo.
options.translation: Good morning, D. Laura. How is everything going? I haven't seen you in a while.
options.feedback: While polite, this avoids the reason for your visit and might seem insensitive given the circumstances.

nodes.npc_text: Ah, pois... Como é que havia de estar? A minha irmã partiu há uma semana. É difícil, sabe?
nodes.npc_translation: Ah, well... How do you think I'd be? My sister passed away a week ago. It's hard, you know?

options.text: Peço desculpa, D. Laura. Não queria ser insensível. Os meus pêsames, a sério.
options.translation: I'm sorry, D. Laura. I didn't mean to be insensitive. My sincere condolences.
options.feedback: Good recovery. Acknowledging your mistake and offering sincere condolences is important.

nodes.npc_text: Obrigada. Ela era uma pessoa muito especial. Deixa muitas saudades. Quer um café?
nodes.npc_translation: Thank you. She was a very special person. She'll be greatly missed. Would you like a coffee?

options.text: Sim, por favor. Se precisar de alguma coisa, seja para as compras ou para o que for, avise-me. Estou aqui para ajudar.
options.translation: Yes, please. If you need anything, whether for shopping or whatever, let me know. I'm here to help.
options.feedback: Excellent. You've accepted her offer and made a concrete, helpful offer in return.

options.text: Não, obrigada. Só queria passar para deixar os meus sentimentos. Tenho de ir.
options.translation: No, thank you. I just wanted to stop by to offer my condolences. I have to go.
options.feedback: While brief visits are appropriate, declining hospitality and rushing off might seem a bit abrupt, though still acceptable.

nodes.npc_text: É muito amável da sua parte. Realmente, ainda não tive cabeça para nada. Agradeço imenso a sua disponibilidade.
nodes.npc_translation: That's very kind of you. Honestly, I haven't had the head for anything yet. I really appreciate your availability.

options.text: Não tem de quê, D. Laura. Desabafar também ajuda, se precisar de falar. Estou por cá.
options.translation: You're welcome, D. Laura. Venting also helps, if you need to talk. I'm around.
options.feedback: This offers emotional support, which is very thoughtful. It shows you're not just offering practical help but also a listening ear.

options.text: Pois, imagino. A vida continua, não é? É preciso ter força.
options.translation: Yes, I imagine. Life goes on, doesn't it? One needs to be strong.
options.feedback: This is a common, but sometimes unhelpful, platitude. It might unintentionally dismiss her grief. Better to focus on her feelings directly.

nodes.npc_text: Muito obrigada por ter vindo. Faça o seu caminho. Adeus.
nodes.npc_translation: Thank you very much for coming. Go on your way. Goodbye.

nodes.npc_text: Obrigada, querida. É bom saber. Vou pensar nisso. Obrigada por tudo.
nodes.npc_translation: Thank you, dear. It's good to know. I'll think about it. Thank you for everything.

nodes.npc_text: Sim, é verdade. Mas é difícil. Enfim... Obrigada por ter vindo.
nodes.npc_translation: Yes, that's true. But it's hard. Anyway... Thank you for coming.

mission.title: Offer Condolences and Support
prep: Practice expressing sincere condolences without being overly intrusive.
Prepare a specific offer of help, such as running errands or providing a listening ear.
Be mindful of body language and tone of voice to convey empathy.

likely_responses: É muito amável da sua parte.

goals: Successfully express condolences in a sensitive and appropriate manner.
Offer practical and emotional support to someone grieving.
Navigate a delicate social situation with empathy and cultural awareness.

situations.title: A Full Day in Town
situations.summary: Chaining real life: the bank, Finanças, the market, a phone call and café encounters — switching register and context all day without dropping the thread.

phrase_patterns.base: Bom dia. Precisava de uma informação sobre {assunto}, se faz favor.
phrase_patterns.translation: Good morning. I needed some information about {topic}, please.

variants.text: Bom dia. Vinha pedir uma informação sobre {assunto}, se me pode ajudar.
variants.translation: Good morning. I came to ask for information about {topic}, if you can help me.
variants.note: Slightly more indirect and polite.

variants.translation: I would like to confirm my {data}.
variants.note: A more direct but still polite request.

phrase_patterns.base: Está a precisar de mais alguma coisa, ou tá tudo?
phrase_patterns.translation: Do you need anything else, or is that everything?

vocabulary.translation: Tax office (Portuguese Ministry of Finance branch)
vocabulary.note: Common name for the local tax authority office.

vocabulary.translation: Tax Identification Number (Número de Identificação Fiscal)
vocabulary.note: Essential for any official transaction in Portugal.
Used for withdrawing cash from an ATM or bank.
Madeiran regionalism for 'batata'.
Standard Portuguese word for a butcher shop.
Common in Lisbon and Madeira for an espresso; 'cimbalino' in Porto.
Important for proof of purchase or payment, especially for tax purposes.

dialogues.title: At the Bank Counter
dialogues.context: A customer is at the bank counter, needing to withdraw a large sum and clarify a transaction.

lines.speaker: Customer
lines.text: Bom dia, o senhor podia ajudar-me, se faz favor? Precisava de levantar um bocado de dinheiro e confirmar uma transferência.
lines.translation: Good morning, could you help me, please? I needed to withdraw some money and confirm a transfer.

lines.speaker: Bank Clerk
lines.text: Bom dia. Claro, diga. Quanto queria levantar e qual a referência da transferência?
lines.translation: Good morning. Of course, tell me. How much would you like to withdraw and what's the transfer reference?

lines.speaker: Customer
lines.text: São duzentos euros, por favor. E a transferência é aquela que fiz na semana passada, para a imobiliária. O valor era mil e quinhentos.
lines.translation: It's two hundred euros, please. And the transfer is the one I made last week, to the real estate agency. The amount was one thousand five hundred.

lines.speaker: Bank Clerk
lines.text: Pois, estou a ver. Está tudo em ordem com a transferência. E aqui tem os duzentos euros. Precisa de mais alguma coisa?
lines.translation: Right, I see. Everything is in order with the transfer. And here are your two hundred euros. Do you need anything else?

lines.speaker: Customer
lines.text: P'ra já, é tudo. Muito obrigado pela ajuda!
lines.translation: For now, that's all. Thank you very much for your help!

dialogues.title: At the Market Stall
dialogues.context: A customer is buying vegetables from a local vendor at the market.

lines.speaker: Customer
lines.text: Bom dia! Tem umas semilhas boas hoje?
lines.translation: Good morning! Do you have good potatoes today?

lines.speaker: Vendor
lines.text: Bom dia! Tenho sim, fresquinhas da terra. Queres um quilo ou dois?
lines.translation: Good morning! Yes, I do, fresh from the land. Do you want a kilo or two?

lines.speaker: Customer
lines.text: Dê-me um quilo, se faz favor. E um molho de couves, que estas parecem ótimas.
lines.translation: Give me a kilo, please. And a bunch of kale, these look great.

lines.speaker: Vendor
lines.text: Pois é, estas couves são mesmo boas. Mais alguma coisa, meu caro?
lines.translation: Indeed, this kale is really good. Anything else, my dear?

lines.speaker: Customer
lines.text: Não, tá bom assim. Quanto é que fica tudo?
lines.translation: No, that's good. How much is everything?

lines.speaker: Vendor
lines.text: Fica três euros e cinquenta. Obrigado e volta sempre!
lines.translation: That's three euros fifty. Thank you and come back anytime!

cultural_notes.title: Navigating Public Services (Finanças)
cultural_notes.body: Dealing with public services like Finanças (the tax office) in Portugal often requires patience and a polite, formal approach. It's common to use 'o senhor/a senhora' even if the official is younger, especially when initiating an interaction. Be prepared to wait, and always have your NIF (Número de Identificação Fiscal) and an identification document (like a passport or residence card) ready. Questions might be indirect, and answers can sometimes be vague, requiring persistence and clear, concise follow-up questions.

cultural_notes.title: Market Interactions and Localisms
cultural_notes.body: When shopping at local markets in Madeira, it's common to hear and use regional words like 'semilha' for potato. Interactions are generally informal and friendly ('tu' form), especially with regular vendors. Don't be surprised by common interjections like 'Pois é!' (Indeed!) or 'Meu caro/minha cara' (My dear) as terms of endearment or friendly address. It's a great opportunity to practice natural, conversational Portuguese.

roleplay.scenario: You are at the Finanças office in Funchal to clarify a notification you received about a property tax payment. You need to understand what the notification means and what action you need to take.

nodes.npc_text: Bom dia. Em que posso ajudá-lo/a?
nodes.npc_translation: Good morning. How can I help you?

options.text: Bom dia. Recebi uma notificação sobre o IMI e não percebi bem o que devo fazer.
options.translation: Good morning. I received a notification about IMI (property tax) and I didn't quite understand what I need to do.
options.feedback: Excellent start! You clearly state your problem and use a polite, indirect phrasing.

options.text: Olá. Vim cá por causa de um papel das Finanças.
options.translation: Hi. I came here because of a paper from Finanças.
options.feedback: This is a bit too informal and vague for Finanças. Try to be more specific and polite.

nodes.npc_text: Pois, mas que papel é que é? Tem a referência consigo?
nodes.npc_translation: Right, but what paper is it? Do you have the reference with you?

options.text: Ah, sim, peço desculpa. É uma notificação sobre o IMI. Não sei se é para pagar ou só informativo.
options.translation: Ah, yes, I'm sorry. It's a notification about IMI. I don't know if it's for payment or just informative.
options.feedback: Good recovery! You've provided more detail and apologized for the initial vagueness.

nodes.npc_text: Certo. Consegue dar-me o seu NIF e mostrar-me a notificação, se faz favor?
nodes.npc_translation: Okay. Can you give me your NIF and show me the notification, please?

options.text: Claro, aqui tem o meu NIF e a notificação. É esta aqui, com a data de há duas semanas.
options.translation: Of course, here's my NIF and the notification. It's this one, dated two weeks ago.
options.feedback: Perfect! You've provided the requested information clearly and promptly.

nodes.npc_text: Pois, estou a ver. Esta notificação é apenas um aviso de que a segunda prestação do IMI vai ser emitida em breve. Não é para pagar já. Apenas para o/a informar.
nodes.npc_translation: Right, I see. This notification is just a warning that the second installment of IMI will be issued soon. It's not for immediate payment. Just to inform you.

options.text: Ah, entendi. Então não preciso de fazer nada agora. Muito obrigado pelo esclarecimento!
options.translation: Ah, I understand. So I don't need to do anything now. Thank you very much for the clarification!
options.feedback: Great! You confirmed your understanding and thanked the official appropriately.

options.text: Mas então, quando é que tenho de pagar? E como?
options.translation: But then, when do I have to pay? And how?
options.feedback: Good follow-up questions. It's important to clarify all doubts.

nodes.npc_text: A data limite para pagamento virá no próximo documento, que deverá receber daqui a umas semanas. Pode pagar em qualquer multibanco ou nas Finanças, como habitualmente.
nodes.npc_translation: The payment deadline will be on the next document, which you should receive in a few weeks. You can pay at any ATM or at Finanças, as usual.

options.text: Ok, muito obrigado. Fico mais tranquilo/a assim. Tenha um bom dia!
options.translation: Okay, thank you very much. I feel more at ease now. Have a good day!
options.feedback: Excellent! You've successfully navigated the interaction and expressed gratitude.

nodes.npc_text: De nada, bom dia e volte sempre que precisar.
nodes.npc_translation: You're welcome, good day and come back whenever you need.

mission.title: Clarify a Finanças Notification
prep: Review vocabulary related to taxes and administrative tasks (NIF, IMI, notificação, recibo).
Practice formal address ('o senhor/a senhora') and indirect requests ('Precisava de...', 'Poderia...?').
Mentally prepare for potential waiting times and bureaucratic language.
Ensure you have your NIF and the physical notification document with you.
fallback_phrases: Desculpe, não percebi. Poderia repetir, se faz favor?
Não estou a entender bem o que devo fazer.
likely_responses: Qual é o seu NIF, se faz favor?
É apenas um aviso, não é para pagar já.
O pagamento será feito como habitualmente.

goals: Successfully interpret and respond to administrative inquiries in a formal setting.
Formulate polite and clear questions to clarify complex information.
Demonstrate understanding of common bureaucratic processes in Portugal.
Manage a multi-turn conversation in a formal register without losing the thread.

situations.title: Hosting End-to-End
situations.summary: One hosting day, every conversation: briefing the cleaner, a guest check-in, a plumber mid-stay, a neighbour’s heads-up about noise, and the checkout — all in Portuguese.

phrase_patterns.base: Bem-vindos à Madeira! A chave está {local}.
phrase_patterns.translation: Welcome to Madeira! The key is {location}.

phrase_patterns.base: Há um problema com o {componente} na casa de banho.
phrase_patterns.translation: There's a problem with the {component} in the bathroom.

phrase_patterns.base: Peço desculpa pelo barulho, estamos a {ação}.
phrase_patterns.translation: I apologize for the noise, we are {action}.

variants.text: Desculpe o incómodo, já vamos acabar.
variants.translation: Sorry for the inconvenience, we'll finish soon.

vocabulary.note: Common anglicism in Portugal
Common anglicism in Portugal

dialogues.title: Briefing the Cleaner
dialogues.context: The owner is meeting the cleaner at the property before the next guests arrive.

lines.speaker: Proprietário
lines.text: Olá, D. Fátima. Então, a casa está pronta p'ra a limpeza.
lines.translation: Hello, D. Fátima. So, the house is ready for cleaning.

lines.speaker: D. Fátima
lines.text: Bom dia, Sr. Miguel. Pois, já vi que sim. Os hóspedes saíram há pouco, certo?
lines.translation: Good morning, Sr. Miguel. Yes, I see. The guests left a little while ago, right?

lines.speaker: Proprietário
lines.text: Exato. E os próximos chegam às quatro da tarde. É preciso caprichar, como sempre.
lines.translation: Exactly. And the next ones arrive at four in the afternoon. It needs to be spotless, as always.

lines.speaker: D. Fátima
lines.text: Não se preocupe, Sr. Miguel. Em três horas está tudo a brilhar. Há alguma coisa específica?
lines.translation: Don't worry, Sr. Miguel. In three hours everything will be sparkling. Is there anything specific?

lines.speaker: Proprietário
lines.text: Ah, sim. O chuveiro do quarto principal está um bocado lento a escoar. Veja se consegue desentupir um pouco.
lines.translation: Ah, yes. The shower in the main bedroom is a bit slow to drain. See if you can unblock it a little.

lines.speaker: D. Fátima
lines.text: Certo, vou ver. P'ra já!
lines.translation: Right, I'll check. See you later!

dialogues.title: Guest Check-in
dialogues.context: The owner is checking in new guests at the property.

lines.speaker: Proprietário
lines.text: Boa tarde! Bem-vindos à Madeira! São o Sr. e a Sra. Silva, certo?
lines.translation: Good afternoon! Welcome to Madeira! You are Mr. and Mrs. Silva, right?

lines.speaker: Hóspede (Sr. Silva)
lines.text: Isso mesmo! Boa tarde. Muito obrigado por nos receber.
lines.translation: That's right! Good afternoon. Thank you very much for having us.

lines.speaker: Proprietário
lines.text: De nada! A viagem correu bem? A chave está aqui, na caixa de segurança ao lado da porta.
lines.translation: You're welcome! Did the journey go well? The key is here, in the safe box next to the door.

lines.speaker: Hóspede (Sra. Silva)
lines.text: Sim, correu tudo bem. Que vista fantástica! É ainda melhor ao vivo.
lines.translation: Yes, everything went well. What a fantastic view! It's even better in person.

lines.speaker: Proprietário
lines.text: Pois é! É um dos pontos fortes da casa. Vou só explicar-vos rapidamente o funcionamento da televisão e do Wi-Fi.
lines.translation: Indeed! It's one of the house's strong points. I'll just quickly explain how the TV and Wi-Fi work.

lines.speaker: Hóspede (Sr. Silva)
lines.text: Perfeito. E se tivermos alguma dúvida durante a estadia?
lines.translation: Perfect. And if we have any questions during our stay?

lines.speaker: Proprietário
lines.text: Podem ligar-me ou enviar mensagem a qualquer altura. O meu número está no frigorífico.
lines.translation: You can call me or send a message anytime. My number is on the fridge.

cultural_notes.title: Directness with Service Providers
cultural_notes.body: In Madeira, as in mainland Portugal, communication with service providers like cleaners or plumbers tends to be direct and focused on the task. While politeness is always valued, there's no need for excessive preamble. Clearly stating the issue or the task at hand is efficient and appreciated. Using 'D. Fátima' or 'Sr. Miguel' (Dona/Senhor + first name) is a common, respectful way to address people in these roles when there's a recurring professional relationship.

cultural_notes.title: Managing Noise and Neighbour Relations
cultural_notes.body: Living in close quarters, especially in residential areas, means being mindful of noise. It's common courtesy to inform neighbours if there might be unusual noise (e.g., a repair, a small gathering) or to apologize if noise has occurred. This helps maintain good community relations. Phrases like 'Peço desculpa pelo barulho' (I apologize for the noise) or 'Desculpe o incómodo' (Sorry for the inconvenience) are important for showing respect and consideration.

roleplay.scenario: A guest has just called you, the property owner, to report a plumbing issue: the toilet flush (autoclismo) in one of the bathrooms isn't working properly. You need to gather more information, reassure the guest, and arrange for a plumber.

nodes.npc_text: Olá, sou eu, o proprietário. Ligou-me a dizer que há um problema, certo? Diga lá o que se passa.
nodes.npc_translation: Hello, it's me, the owner. You called me to say there's a problem, right? Tell me what's happening.

options.text: Sim, o autoclismo da casa de banho do quarto principal não está a funcionar bem.
options.translation: Yes, the toilet flush in the main bedroom's bathroom isn't working well.
options.feedback: Good, you clearly stated the problem. Now to ask for details.

options.text: Há um problema com a água na casa de banho.
options.translation: There's a problem with the water in the bathroom.
options.feedback: This is a bit vague. Try to be more specific to help the owner understand the issue quickly.

nodes.npc_text: Certo, mas pode ser mais específico? O que é que se passa exatamente com a água?
nodes.npc_translation: Okay, but can you be more specific? What exactly is happening with the water?

options.text: O autoclismo da casa de banho principal não está a deitar água suficiente.
options.translation: The flush in the main bathroom isn't releasing enough water.
options.feedback: Much better, clear and specific. Good job clarifying!

nodes.npc_text: Ah, entendi. O autoclismo não está a encher ou não deita água? É importante para eu saber qual o canalizador a chamar.
nodes.npc_translation: Ah, I see. Is the flush not filling or not releasing water? It's important for me to know which plumber to call.

options.text: Ele enche, mas quando carregamos, a água não desce com força. Parece entupido.
options.translation: It fills, but when we press, the water doesn't flush with force. It seems blocked.
options.feedback: Excellent detail. This helps the owner diagnose and dispatch the right help.

options.text: Não sei bem, só sei que não funciona.
options.translation: I don't really know, I just know it doesn't work.
options.feedback: Try to provide as much detail as possible, even if you're not an expert. What do you observe?

nodes.npc_text: Compreendo. Consegue ver se está a vazar água para fora ou se o problema é apenas dentro da sanita?
nodes.npc_translation: I understand. Can you see if water is leaking outside or if the problem is just inside the toilet bowl?

options.text: Não, não está a vazar. Apenas a água não desce com força na sanita.
options.translation: No, it's not leaking. Just the water doesn't flush with force in the toilet.
options.feedback: Good, that's crucial information. Now the owner knows it's likely an internal blockage.

nodes.npc_text: Muito obrigado pela informação. Vou já contactar o canalizador. Ele deve conseguir ir aí dentro de uma hora. Posso dar-lhe a chave ou prefere que ele bata à porta?
nodes.npc_translation: Thank you very much for the information. I'll contact the plumber right away. He should be able to get there within an hour. Can I give him the key or do you prefer he knocks on the door?

options.text: Não se preocupe, estamos cá. Ele pode bater à porta.
options.translation: Don't worry, we're here. He can knock on the door.
options.feedback: Good, you've confirmed your availability. This simplifies things for the owner and plumber.

options.text: Se puder dar-lhe a chave, agradeço. Vamos sair por um bocado.
options.translation: If you can give him the key, I'd appreciate it. We're going out for a bit.
options.feedback: This is a valid option, showing flexibility. Make sure the owner knows how to access it.

nodes.npc_text: Certo. Nesse caso, vou deixar a chave na caixa de segurança e dar-lhe o código. O canalizador ligar-lhe-á quando estiver a caminho. Peço desculpa pelo incómodo.
nodes.npc_translation: Right. In that case, I'll leave the key in the safe box and give him the code. The plumber will call you when he's on his way. I apologize for the inconvenience.

nodes.npc_text: Perfeito. Vou avisar o Sr. João que pode bater à porta. Ele é muito eficiente. Peço desculpa pelo incómodo e obrigado pela compreensão.
nodes.npc_translation: Perfect. I'll let Sr. João know he can knock on the door. He's very efficient. I apologize for the inconvenience and thank you for your understanding.

Mentally rehearse asking clarifying questions about a problem.

Quando é que o canalizador pode vir?

Manage guest expectations and provide timely updates regarding repairs.

Coordinate effectively with external service personnel (e.g., plumber) and guests.

situations.title: Improvising When Plans Collapse
situations.summary: The ferry is cancelled, the road is closed, the guests are early: replanning out loud with others, negotiating alternatives, and staying fluent when nothing goes to script.

phrase_patterns.translation: It looks like we'll have to {look_for_alternatives}.

variants.text: Pois, parece que vamos ter de improvisar.
variants.translation: Well, it looks like we'll have to improvise.

variants.text: Não faz mal, arranja-se sempre alguma coisa.
variants.translation: It's okay, something always works out.

phrase_patterns.base: Isso complica um bocado as coisas, não é?
phrase_patterns.translation: That complicates things a bit, doesn't it?

vocabulary.note: Commonly used in Portugal for minor difficulties.

vocabulary.translation: to bypass, to get around (a problem)
to find a way around, to sort it out
vocabulary.note: Idiomatic expression.

dialogues.title: Ferry Cancelled to Porto Santo
dialogues.context: Ana and Pedro were planning a weekend trip to Porto Santo, but the ferry 'Lobo Marinho' was cancelled due to bad weather. They are now at a café in Funchal, discussing alternatives.

lines.speaker: Ana
lines.text: Não acredito! O Lobo Marinho foi cancelado outra vez? Que chatice!
lines.translation: I don't believe it! The Lobo Marinho was cancelled again? What a bother!

lines.speaker: Pedro
lines.text: Pois é, Ana. Por causa do mar agitado. É um imprevisto, mas temos de contornar.
lines.translation: That's right, Ana. Because of the rough sea. It's an unforeseen event, but we have to get around it.

lines.speaker: Ana
lines.text: E agora? O que é que fazemos com o fim de semana? Já tínhamos tudo planeado para Porto Santo.
lines.translation: And now? What do we do with the weekend? We already had everything planned for Porto Santo.

lines.speaker: Pedro
lines.text: Não há problema, encontramos uma alternativa. Que tal explorarmos o norte da ilha? Podíamos ir a São Vicente ou Santana.
lines.translation: No problem, we'll find an alternative. How about exploring the north of the island? We could go to São Vicente or Santana.

lines.speaker: Ana
lines.text: Hum... é uma ideia. Mas e o alojamento? Já tínhamos reservado o de Porto Santo.
lines.translation: Hmm... it's an idea. But what about the accommodation? We had already booked the one in Porto Santo.

lines.speaker: Pedro
lines.text: Ligamos para lá e tentamos cancelar sem custos. Para o norte, podemos ver um alojamento local de última hora. É sempre mais fácil.
lines.translation: We'll call them and try to cancel without cost. For the north, we can look for last-minute local accommodation. It's always easier.

lines.speaker: Ana
lines.text: Está bem, Pedro. É o que temos. Pelo menos não ficamos em casa. O que achas de irmos ver as grutas de São Vicente?
lines.translation: Okay, Pedro. It is what it is. At least we won't stay home. What do you think of going to see the São Vicente caves?

lines.speaker: Pedro
lines.text: Excelente ideia! E depois um almoço com espetada e bolo do caco por lá? Fica combinado.
lines.translation: Excellent idea! And then lunch with espetada and bolo do caco there? It's a plan.

cultural_notes.title: Flexibility is Key in Island Life
cultural_notes.body: Living on an island, especially one with a dynamic climate like Madeira, often requires a high degree of flexibility. Weather conditions can frequently impact travel plans, whether it's ferry cancellations to Porto Santo, road closures due to landslides (derrocadas), or even flight delays. Locals are accustomed to these 'imprevistos' and often have a pragmatic approach, quickly brainstorming alternatives rather than dwelling on the disruption. Phrases like 'É o que temos' (It is what it is) or 'Dá-se a volta' (We'll find a way around it) reflect this adaptability.

cultural_notes.title: Negotiating Alternatives Politely
cultural_notes.body: When plans collapse, the way you propose and negotiate alternatives is important. While directness is generally appreciated in Portugal, softening suggestions with phrases like 'O que achas de...?' (What do you think of...?), 'Talvez pudéssemos...' (Perhaps we could...), or expressing understanding ('Isso complica um bocado...') helps maintain a positive atmosphere. Avoid sounding demanding or overly critical of the changed circumstances. A calm, problem-solving attitude is valued.

roleplay.scenario: You are at a café in Funchal, waiting for your friends, Marta and Tiago, who are driving from the north of the island to meet you for lunch. You receive a message from the café owner that a main road is closed due to a small landslide, and your friends might be delayed or need a new route. You need to call them and figure out a new plan.

nodes.npc_text: Bom dia! Desculpe interromper, mas recebi agora uma notícia: a estrada para o Faial está cortada. Os seus amigos vinham de lá, não era?
nodes.npc_translation: Good morning! Sorry to interrupt, but I just got news: the road to Faial is closed. Your friends were coming from there, weren't they?

options.text: Ah, não! Que percalço! Sim, vinham do Faial. Vou ligar-lhes já.
options.translation: Oh, no! What a setback! Yes, they were coming from Faial. I'll call them now.
options.feedback: Good, you acknowledged the problem and stated your immediate action. 'Percalço' is a great word here.

options.text: Cortada? Isso complica tudo. Mas eles devem ter um plano B, não?
options.translation: Closed? That complicates everything. But they must have a Plan B, right?
options.feedback: You expressed concern and a slight expectation for them to solve it. Now, take action by calling them.

nodes.npc_text: (Você liga para a Marta. Ela atende, um bocado aflita.) Marta: Olá! Estamos parados, a estrada está mesmo cortada. Não sabemos o que fazer, estamos a ver no GPS.
nodes.npc_translation: (You call Marta. She answers, a bit stressed.) Marta: Hello! We're stopped, the road is really closed. We don't know what to do, we're checking the GPS.

options.text: Pois, o senhor do café avisou-me. Que tal darem a volta pela Ribeira Brava e virmos ter a um sítio mais a meio?
options.translation: Yeah, the café owner told me. How about you go around via Ribeira Brava and we meet somewhere more in the middle?
options.feedback: Excellent, you're proposing a concrete alternative route and meeting point. This shows initiative.

options.text: Calma, Marta. Há sempre uma solução. Conseguem ir por outra estrada, mesmo que seja mais longa?
options.translation: Calm down, Marta. There's always a solution. Can you take another road, even if it's longer?
options.feedback: Good, you're reassuring your friend and prompting them to think about alternatives. Now, be ready to suggest one.

nodes.npc_text: (Marta e Tiago conversam. Marta volta ao telemóvel.) Marta: Sim, o Tiago está a ver. Pela Ribeira Brava é capaz de demorar mais uma hora. Onde é que nos encontramos então?
nodes.npc_translation: (Marta and Tiago talk. Marta gets back on the phone.) Marta: Yes, Tiago is checking. Via Ribeira Brava might take another hour. Where should we meet then?

options.text: Que tal irmos todos almoçar à Taberna da Poncha, em São Vicente? Fica mais perto para vocês e é sempre bom.
options.translation: How about we all go for lunch at Taberna da Poncha, in São Vicente? It's closer for you and it's always good.
options.feedback: Great, you've chosen a specific, well-known Madeiran spot that's convenient for them. This is good local knowledge.

options.text: Se demoram uma hora, talvez seja melhor almoçarmos mais para o oeste. Podíamos ir a um sítio em Câmara de Lobos?
options.translation: If it takes an hour, maybe it's better to have lunch further west. Could we go to a place in Câmara de Lobos?
options.feedback: Good, you're adapting the plan based on the new travel time and suggesting a new area. Be ready to name a specific place.

nodes.npc_text: (Marta fala com Tiago novamente.) Marta: Boa ideia! A Taberna da Poncha é ótima. Então, vamos para lá. Chegamos em cerca de uma hora e meia, talvez. Vemo-nos lá!
nodes.npc_translation: (Marta talks to Tiago again.) Marta: Good idea! Taberna da Poncha is great. So, we're heading there. We'll arrive in about an hour and a half, maybe. See you there!

options.text: Combinado! Vou avisar o senhor do café que mudámos de planos. Até já!
options.translation: Agreed! I'll let the café owner know we've changed plans. See you soon!
options.feedback: Perfect! You've confirmed the plan and remembered to inform the service staff, showing good social awareness. This is a solid resolution.

mission.title: Rescuing the Day: The Unexpected Detour
prep: Review vocabulary for travel, delays, and problem-solving.
Practice phrases for proposing alternatives and asking for opinions.
Mentally prepare for unexpected changes and how to react calmly in Portuguese.
fallback_phrases: Não faz mal, arranja-se sempre alguma coisa.
Tudo se arranja, não se preocupe.

goals: Discuss and react to unexpected changes in plans.
Propose and negotiate alternative solutions and meeting points.
Express understanding and flexibility when plans collapse.
Maintain fluency and appropriate register in a dynamic, real-life conversation.

review_items.prompt: Say in Portuguese: It looks like we'll have to {look_for_alternatives}.
Say in Portuguese: No problem, {we_will_find_a_solution}.
Say in Portuguese: What do you think of {proposal}?
Say in Portuguese: That complicates things a bit, doesn't it?
review_items.answer: Isso complica um bocado as coisas, não é?

review_items.prompt: Recall the Portuguese for: cancelled
Recall the Portuguese for: alternative
Recall the Portuguese for: unforeseen event, unexpected hitch
Recall the Portuguese for: setback, mishap
Listen to the dialogue at natural speed and answer: what is being asked for?

situations.title: Fast, Noisy, Real
situations.summary: Extreme listening: overlapping speakers in a loud café, a bad phone line, a mumbled aside — extracting what matters, confirming it, and answering at speed.

phrase_patterns.base: Não percebi bem o que disse.
phrase_patterns.translation: I didn't quite understand what you said.

variants.note: Shorter, more direct.
Use with 'tu' form.
Very informal, for when you really missed something.

slots.name: item
options: um bolo do caco com manteiga

phrase_patterns.base: Desculpe, a linha está um bocado má.
phrase_patterns.translation: Sorry, the line is a bit bad.

vocabulary.note: Common in Portugal and Madeira.
Madeiran regionalism for 'batata'.
Very common in European Portuguese.

dialogues.title: Café Chaos
dialogues.context: You're at a very busy café in Funchal during peak hours, trying to order. The barista is fast, and there's a lot of background noise.

lines.speaker: Barista
lines.text: Próximo! O que vai ser? Temos imensa gente hoje, despachem-se!
lines.translation: Next! What will it be? We have loads of people today, hurry up!

lines.speaker: Cliente
lines.text: Desculpe, não percebi bem. Pode repetir, por favor?
lines.translation: Sorry, I didn't quite understand. Can you repeat, please?

lines.speaker: Barista
lines.text: Disse 'o que vai ser'! Bolo do caco, bica, poncha? O que é?
lines.translation: I said 'what will it be'! Bolo do caco, espresso, poncha? What is it?

lines.speaker: Cliente
lines.text: Ah, pois, sim! Queria um bolo do caco com manteiga de alho e uma bica cheia, por favor.
lines.translation: Ah, yes, right! I'd like a bolo do caco with garlic butter and a full espresso, please.

lines.speaker: Barista
lines.text: Certo. Um bolo do caco com alho e uma bica. Mais alguma coisa?
lines.translation: Right. A bolo do caco with garlic and an espresso. Anything else?

dialogues.title: Bad Phone Line
dialogues.context: You're on the phone trying to confirm details for a delivery, but the line quality is very poor.

lines.speaker: Funcionário
lines.text: A sua encomenda... uhm... será entregue... (chiado)... na terça-feira.
lines.translation: Your order... uhm... will be delivered... (static)... on Tuesday.

lines.speaker: Cliente
lines.text: Desculpe, a linha está um bocado má. Não percebi a data. É na terça?
lines.translation: Sorry, the line is a bit bad. I didn't catch the date. Is it on Tuesday?

lines.speaker: Funcionário
lines.text: Sim, na terça-feira... (ruído)... entre as nove e o meio-dia.
lines.translation: Yes, on Tuesday... (noise)... between nine and midday.

lines.speaker: Cliente
lines.text: Pois, percebi. Entre as nove e o meio-dia, então. Obrigado!
lines.translation: Yes, I understood. Between nine and midday, then. Thank you!

cultural_notes.title: Asking for Clarification Politely
cultural_notes.body: In Portugal, when you don't understand something, especially due to noise or fast speech, it's common and polite to say 'Não percebi bem' (I didn't quite understand) or 'Pode repetir, por favor?' (Can you repeat, please?). In very informal contexts, a short 'Diga?' or 'Como?' can also be used, similar to 'Pardon?' or 'What?' in English. Avoid using 'você' for direct polite address; instead, use the third person verb form or 'o senhor / a senhora'.

cultural_notes.title: The Versatility of 'Pois' and 'Pois é'
cultural_notes.body: 'Pois' and 'Pois é' are very common interjections in European Portuguese. 'Pois' can mean 'yes', 'of course', 'that's right', or simply acknowledge what someone has said. 'Pois é' is similar, often used to confirm something already known, express agreement, or sometimes a slight resignation. They are excellent fillers and markers of active listening in conversation.

roleplay.scenario: You are at a very busy 'pastelaria' (bakery/cafe) in Funchal. You want to order a specific type of bolo do caco and a drink, but the place is packed, and the person serving you is speaking quickly and a bit indistinctly.

nodes.npc_text: Próximo! Diga lá, o que é que vai ser? Temos imensa gente hoje, não consigo ouvir nada!
nodes.npc_translation: Next! Tell me, what'll it be? We have loads of people today, I can't hear anything!

options.text: Olá, queria um bolo do caco com manteiga de alho e uma bica, por favor.
options.translation: Hello, I'd like a bolo do caco with garlic butter and an espresso, please.
options.feedback: Good, you tried to place the order clearly despite the noise.

options.text: Desculpe, não percebi bem. Pode repetir?
options.translation: Sorry, I didn't quite understand. Can you repeat?
options.feedback: Excellent, you immediately asked for clarification. This is a key skill in noisy environments.

nodes.npc_text: Disse 'Próximo, o que vai ser?' Há muita confusão, não é? O que é que quer?
nodes.npc_translation: I said 'Next, what will it be?' It's very confusing, isn't it? What do you want?

options.text: Sim, é verdade. Queria um bolo do caco com manteiga de alho e uma bica.
options.translation: Yes, it's true. I'd like a bolo do caco with garlic butter and an espresso.
options.feedback: Well done, you understood and placed your order.

nodes.npc_text: Um bolo do caco com manteiga de alho e uma... o quê? Não ouvi a bebida, o barulho é imenso!
nodes.npc_translation: A bolo do caco with garlic butter and a... what? I didn't hear the drink, the noise is immense!

options.text: Uma bica! Uma bica normal, por favor.
options.translation: An espresso! A normal espresso, please.
options.feedback: You repeated the item clearly. Good job focusing on the specific missing information.

options.text: Desculpe, é uma bica. A fila está enorme, não consigo ouvir bem também.
options.translation: Sorry, it's an espresso. The queue is huge, I can't hear well either.
options.feedback: You clarified and showed empathy for the situation, which is good social interaction.

nodes.npc_text: Certo, então é um bolo do caco com manteiga de alho e uma bica. É isso? Mais alguma coisa?
nodes.npc_translation: Right, so it's a bolo do caco with garlic butter and an espresso. Is that it? Anything else?

options.text: Não, obrigado. É só isso.
options.translation: No, thank you. That's all.
options.feedback: Clear and concise. You confirmed the order and finished the interaction.

options.text: Pois, sim, é isso mesmo. Obrigado!
options.translation: Yes, that's right. Thank you!
options.feedback: Using 'Pois, sim' is very natural for confirmation in Portugal. Well done.

nodes.npc_text: São três euros e cinquenta, por favor. O seu pedido fica ali no balcão, à espera.
nodes.npc_translation: That's three euros fifty, please. Your order will be there on the counter, waiting.

mission.title: Order Confirmation in the Chaos
prep: Practice asking for repetition politely: 'Não percebi bem', 'Pode repetir, por favor?'.
Rehearse confirming an order by repeating it back: 'Então, é {item}, certo?'.
Mentally prepare for fast speech and significant background noise.
fallback_phrases: A fila está enorme, não consigo ouvir bem.

goals: Successfully order food/drink in a noisy, fast-paced environment.
Use clarification phrases effectively when faced with unclear or incomplete audio.
Confirm order details to ensure accuracy and avoid mistakes.

review_items.answer: Não percebi bem o que disse.

review_items.prompt: Say in Portuguese: Can you repeat, please?
Say in Portuguese: So, it's {item}, right?
Say in Portuguese: Yes, I understand now.
