media.caption: The secret to understanding FAST Portuguese — European Portuguese (Talk the Streets)

situations.title: Your Madeira Story
situations.summary: Presenting yourself as a resident: the polished version of why Madeira, what you do, how the island changed you — told with colour, connectors and humour.

phrase_patterns.base: Cheguei à Madeira há {tempo_atras} e, desde então, {acao_principal}.
phrase_patterns.translation: I arrived in Madeira {time_ago} and, since then, {main_activity}.

phrase_patterns.base: O que me trouxe cá foi {razao_principal}, mas o que me fez ficar foi {razao_para_ficar}.
phrase_patterns.translation: What brought me here was {main_reason}, but what made me stay was {reason_to_stay}.

slots.name: razao_principal
options: a busca por qualidade de vida

variants.text: O que me trouxe cá foi uma coisa, mas o que me prendeu foi outra.
variants.translation: What brought me here was one thing, but what kept me here was another.
variants.note: More colloquial way to express a similar idea.

phrase_patterns.base: A ilha mudou a minha perspetiva sobre {aspecto_da_vida}.
phrase_patterns.translation: The island changed my perspective on {aspect_of_life}.

phrase_patterns.base: Sinto-me um verdadeiro {sentimento_residente}, apesar de não ter nascido cá.
phrase_patterns.translation: I feel like a true {resident_feeling}, despite not being born here.

vocabulary.note: Often used to describe people or places in Madeira.
A common reason cited for moving to Madeira.
Often described as calmer or slower in Madeira.
Key concept for this lesson, describing feeling part of the community.
Iconic Madeiran walking trails, often mentioned when talking about the island's charm.
Common pt-PT expression.
Common interjection for agreement or acknowledgment in pt-PT.

dialogues.title: Telling Your Madeira Story
dialogues.context: At a local café, a new acquaintance, Sofia, asks about your journey to Madeira.

lines.speaker: Sofia
lines.text: Então, conte-me lá, como é que veio parar à Madeira? Já cá mora há muito tempo?
lines.translation: So, tell me, how did you end up in Madeira? Have you been living here long?

lines.speaker: Learner
lines.text: Pois, cheguei há uns cinco anos. Vim p'ra cá por causa de uma oportunidade de trabalho, mas o que me fez ficar foi mesmo o ritmo de vida e a natureza.
lines.translation: Well, I arrived about five years ago. I came here because of a job opportunity, but what made me stay was really the pace of life and the nature.

lines.speaker: Sofia
lines.text: Ah, pois é, isso acontece com muitos! A ilha tem um encanto especial. E como se sente agora, já se sente um madeirense?
lines.translation: Ah, that's right, that happens to many! The island has a special charm. And how do you feel now, do you already feel Madeiran?

lines.speaker: Learner
lines.text: Olhe, sinto-me um madeirense de coração, sem dúvida! A ilha mudou a minha perspetiva sobre o que é ter qualidade de vida. E já não troco o bolo do caco por nada!
lines.translation: Look, I feel Madeiran at heart, no doubt! The island changed my perspective on what it means to have quality of life. And I wouldn't trade bolo do caco for anything now!

lines.speaker: Sofia
lines.text: Ah, isso é que é bom de ouvir! Sinal que está mesmo integrado. É sempre um prazer ver quem se apaixona pela nossa ilha.
lines.translation: Ah, that's good to hear! A sign that you're truly integrated. It's always a pleasure to see those who fall in love with our island.

cultural_notes.title: Expressing Attachment to Madeira
cultural_notes.body: Madeirans are generally very proud of their island and appreciate when foreigners express genuine affection and integration. Using phrases like 'madeirense de coração' (Madeiran at heart) or mentioning specific local elements like 'as levadas' or 'o bolo do caco' can show a deeper connection and appreciation, often leading to a warmer reception. It's common to share personal anecdotes about how the island has positively impacted one's life.

cultural_notes.title: The Role of Humour and Connectors
cultural_notes.body: In casual conversations, a touch of self-deprecating humour or lightheartedness is common and can help build rapport. Using connectors like 'Pois', 'Então', 'Olhe', 'Sem dúvida' makes your speech sound more natural and fluent, characteristic of L5 (B2) level. These words help to structure your thoughts and signal your engagement in the conversation.

roleplay.scenario: You're at a community event, and a local resident, João, asks about your experience living in Madeira. He's curious about what you do and how you've adapted.

nodes.npc_text: Olá! Não me lembro de o ver por cá antes. É novo na ilha, ou já cá mora há algum tempo? O que o trouxe à nossa pérola do Atlântico?
nodes.npc_translation: Hello! I don't remember seeing you around before. Are you new to the island, or have you been living here for a while? What brought you to our Pearl of the Atlantic?

options.text: Já cá estou há uns bons anos, João. Vim pela aventura, e acabei por ficar pela paz e beleza da ilha.
options.translation: I've been here for a good few years, João. I came for the adventure, and ended up staying for the peace and beauty of the island.
options.feedback: Excellent start! You've directly answered his questions and added a personal touch.

options.text: Cheguei há pouco tempo, mas já estou a adorar tudo! Vim de férias e decidi que queria mudar de vida.
options.translation: I arrived recently, but I'm already loving everything! I came on holiday and decided I wanted to change my life.
options.feedback: Good, but you could elaborate a bit more on what specifically made you stay, linking to the island's charm.

nodes.npc_text: Pois é, a Madeira tem esse poder! E o que faz por cá? Conseguiu adaptar-se ao nosso ritmo, um bocado mais lento que o do continente, não é?
nodes.npc_translation: That's right, Madeira has that power! And what do you do here? Have you managed to adapt to our pace, a bit slower than the mainland's, right?

options.text: Sou professor, e sim, o ritmo de vida aqui é mais calmo, o que me permite desfrutar mais da natureza e das pessoas. Sinto-me bastante integrado.
options.translation: I'm a teacher, and yes, the pace of life here is calmer, which allows me to enjoy nature and people more. I feel quite integrated.
options.feedback: Perfect! You've answered directly, mentioned your profession without an article, and used 'integrado'.

options.text: Trabalho remotamente, o que é ótimo para aproveitar a ilha. O ritmo é diferente, mas para melhor, sem dúvida!
options.translation: I work remotely, which is great for enjoying the island. The pace is different, but for the better, no doubt!
options.feedback: Very good, you've highlighted a common reason for foreigners to move and expressed a positive opinion.

nodes.npc_text: Que coragem! E o que o convenceu a ficar? Houve algum momento em que pensou: 'É aqui que quero viver'?
nodes.npc_translation: What courage! And what convinced you to stay? Was there a moment when you thought: 'This is where I want to live'?

options.text: Sim, foi quando fiz a primeira levada. Aquelas paisagens e a sensação de paz... A ilha mudou a minha perspetiva sobre a vida.
options.translation: Yes, it was when I did my first levada walk. Those landscapes and the feeling of peace... The island changed my perspective on life.
options.feedback: Excellent! You've used a specific Madeiran reference and a phrase pattern about changing perspective.

options.text: Acho que foi a hospitalidade das pessoas, e claro, a poncha! Sinto-me em casa aqui.
options.translation: I think it was the hospitality of the people, and of course, the poncha! I feel at home here.
options.feedback: Great use of local cultural references and expressing a feeling of belonging.

nodes.npc_text: Fico contente por ouvir isso! É bom ter gente que valoriza o que temos. E qual é a sua coisa favorita na Madeira, depois de todos estes anos?
nodes.npc_translation: I'm glad to hear that! It's good to have people who value what we have. And what's your favorite thing about Madeira, after all these years?

options.text: Diria que é a mistura de natureza exuberante com a simpatia das pessoas. É uma combinação única que me faz sentir um verdadeiro madeirense de coração.
options.translation: I'd say it's the mix of lush nature with the kindness of the people. It's a unique combination that makes me feel like a true Madeiran at heart.
options.feedback: Perfect! You've combined several positive aspects and used a key phrase pattern. Conversation ends naturally here.

options.text: Sem dúvida, as levadas e o bolo do caco! E o facto de poder ir à praia e à montanha no mesmo dia. É fantástico!
options.translation: Without a doubt, the levadas and the bolo do caco! And the fact that I can go to the beach and the mountains on the same day. It's fantastic!
options.feedback: Excellent! You've used specific Madeiran references and expressed enthusiasm. Conversation ends naturally here.

nodes.npc_text: Pois é! É isso mesmo que nos prende à ilha. Fico feliz que se sinta assim. Bem-vindo à família madeirense!
nodes.npc_translation: That's right! That's exactly what ties us to the island. I'm happy you feel that way. Welcome to the Madeiran family!

options.text: Muito obrigado, João! É um prazer fazer parte dela.
options.translation: Thank you very much, João! It's a pleasure to be a part of it.
options.feedback: A warm and appropriate closing. Well done!

options.text: Obrigado! É um sentimento recíproco.
options.translation: Thanks! The feeling is mutual.
options.feedback: A concise and polite response showing mutual appreciation. Good job!

mission.title: Share Your Madeira Story at a Social Gathering
prep: Mentally prepare a 1-2 minute 'pitch' about your journey to Madeira, what you do, and how the island has impacted you.
Practise using at least two `phrase_patterns` from this lesson in your story.
Think of specific Madeiran details (e.g., levadas, bolo do caco, poncha) to include naturally.
Rehearse expressing your feelings of integration and appreciation for the island.
fallback_phrases: Peço desculpa, não percebi bem. Pode repetir, por favor?
Ah, interessante! Pode explicar um bocado melhor?
Não tenho a certeza de como expressar isso em português ainda.
likely_responses: Ah, que interessante! E o que o fez ficar?
Gosta mesmo da ilha, não é? Dá para ver!
E o que faz por cá? Conseguiu adaptar-se bem?
É bom ouvir isso! A ilha tem um encanto especial.

goals: Articulate your personal story of living in Madeira with confidence and detail.
Use appropriate connectors and descriptive language to enhance your narrative.
Express feelings and opinions about the island, including its impact on your life, with nuance.
Engage in a natural and extended conversation about your integration into local life, demonstrating a B2 level of fluency.

review_items.prompt: Say in Portuguese: I arrived in Madeira {time_ago} and, since then, {main_activity}.
review_items.answer: Cheguei à Madeira há {tempo_atras} e, desde então, {acao_principal}.

review_items.prompt: Say in Portuguese: What brought me here was {main_reason}, but what made me stay was {reason_to_stay}.
review_items.answer: O que me trouxe cá foi {razao_principal}, mas o que me fez ficar foi {razao_para_ficar}.

review_items.prompt: Say in Portuguese: The island changed my perspective on {aspect_of_life}.
review_items.answer: A ilha mudou a minha perspetiva sobre {aspecto_da_vida}.

review_items.prompt: Say in Portuguese: I feel like a true {resident_feeling}, despite not being born here.
review_items.answer: Sinto-me um verdadeiro {sentimento_residente}, apesar de não ter nascido cá.

review_items.prompt: Recall the Portuguese for: welcoming, cozy
Recall the Portuguese for: quality of life
Recall the Portuguese for: the pace of life
Recall the Portuguese for: integrated
Listen to the dialogue at natural speed and answer: what is being asked for?

situations.title: The Long Conversation
situations.summary: An evening of unscripted talk: sustaining a multi-topic conversation for an hour — stories, opinions, jokes, disagreements and plans — as an equal at the table.

phrase_patterns.base: Por acaso, estava a pensar no que me disseste sobre {topic}...
phrase_patterns.translation: By the way, I was thinking about what you told me about {topic}...

slots.name: topic
slots.description: a subject previously discussed or mentioned
options: a receita do bolo do caco

variants.text: Sabes, fiquei a pensar no que me disseste sobre {topic}...
variants.translation: You know, I kept thinking about what you told me about {topic}...
variants.note: Slightly more emphatic 'you know' opening

phrase_patterns.base: Concordo contigo, mas por outro lado, também acho que {alternative_view}.
phrase_patterns.translation: I agree with you, but on the other hand, I also think that {alternative_view}.

slots.name: alternative_view
options: a situação é mais complexa do que parece
depende muito do ponto de vista de cada um

variants.text: Percebo o teu ponto de vista, mas tenho uma perspetiva um bocado diferente.
variants.translation: I understand your point of view, but I have a slightly different perspective.
variants.note: More formal way to express disagreement

phrase_patterns.base: Isso faz-me lembrar uma história engraçada que me aconteceu com {person_or_situation}.
phrase_patterns.translation: That reminds me of a funny story that happened to me with {person_or_situation}.

slots.name: person_or_situation
slots.description: a person or situation that triggers a memory

phrase_patterns.base: E tu, o que achas sobre {current_topic}?
phrase_patterns.translation: And you, what do you think about {current_topic}?

variants.text: Qual é a tua opinião sobre {current_topic}?
variants.note: More direct way to ask for an opinion

vocabulary.translation: to vent, to get something off one's chest
vocabulary.note: Common in conversations about personal feelings
Often used in more structured discussions
Used when wanting to keep talking
Common expression for shared humour
Useful for navigating delicate subjects
Essential for expressing opinions and understanding others

dialogues.title: Evening Chat at the Café
dialogues.context: At a local café in Funchal, two friends, Ana and Carlos, are catching up after work, discussing a recent local event.

lines.speaker: Ana
lines.text: Então, Carlos, o que achaste daquele festival de folclore no fim de semana? Fui lá e achei um bocado confuso, p'ra ser sincera.
lines.translation: So, Carlos, what did you think of that folklore festival at the weekend? I went there and found it a bit confusing, to be honest.

lines.speaker: Carlos
lines.text: Pois é, Ana, percebo o que dizes. Eu também fui. As danças eram bonitas, sim, mas a organização... ui, parecia que ninguém sabia bem o que estava a fazer. Mas os trajes, esses estavam impecáveis!
lines.translation: Well, Ana, I understand what you're saying. I went too. The dances were beautiful, yes, but the organization... oh, it seemed like no one really knew what they were doing. But the costumes, those were impeccable!

lines.speaker: Ana
lines.text: Exato! Os trajes eram lindos. E a comida, provaste alguma coisa? Aquelas espetadas estavam divinais, não achas?
lines.translation: Exactly! The costumes were beautiful. And the food, did you try anything? Those espetadas were divine, don't you think?

lines.speaker: Carlos
lines.text: Ah, as espetadas! Claro que sim! Comi umas duas, estavam mesmo no ponto. É daquelas coisas que nos fazem esquecer a confusão toda, não é?
lines.translation: Ah, the espetadas! Of course! I ate about two, they were perfectly cooked. It's one of those things that makes us forget all the confusion, isn't it?

lines.speaker: Ana
lines.text: É verdade. Mas, por acaso, estava a pensar no que me disseste sobre a nova política de resíduos. Achas que vai mesmo funcionar aqui na ilha?
lines.translation: It's true. But, by the way, I was thinking about what you told me about the new waste policy. Do you really think it will work here on the island?

lines.speaker: Carlos
lines.text: Hum, é um assunto melindroso, Ana. Tenho as minhas dúvidas, p'ra ser honesto. Vai depender muito da colaboração de todos. Mas temos de tentar, não é? É pelo bem da nossa ilha.
lines.translation: Hmm, it's a sensitive topic, Ana. I have my doubts, to be honest. It will depend a lot on everyone's cooperation. But we have to try, don't we? It's for the good of our island.

cultural_notes.title: Navigating Disagreement in Portuguese Conversations
cultural_notes.body: In Portuguese culture, especially in social settings, direct confrontation is often avoided. When expressing disagreement, it's common to preface your opinion with phrases that acknowledge the other person's point of view, such as 'Percebo o que dizes' (I understand what you're saying) or 'Concordo contigo, mas...' (I agree with you, but...). This softens the impact and maintains harmony. Humour can also be used to defuse tension or gently challenge an idea without causing offence.

cultural_notes.title: The Art of Storytelling and Anecdotes
cultural_notes.body: Sharing personal stories and anecdotes is a vital part of social interaction in Madeira. It's a way to connect, entertain, and illustrate a point. Don't be afraid to link a current topic to a past experience or a funny incident. Phrases like 'Isso faz-me lembrar...' (That reminds me...) are excellent ways to introduce your own narrative and contribute to the 'long conversation'.

roleplay.scenario: You are at a neighbour's house for an informal evening gathering. Your neighbour, Dona Fátima, is known for her lively conversations and strong opinions. She's just brought up a local issue.

nodes.npc_text: Então, o que achas disto dos novos horários dos autocarros na Ponta do Sol? Para mim, é um disparate! Chega a ser ridículo como eles mudam as coisas sem pensar nas pessoas que precisam de trabalhar.
nodes.npc_translation: So, what do you think about these new bus schedules in Ponta do Sol? For me, it's nonsense! It's ridiculous how they change things without thinking about the people who need to work.

options.text: Percebo o teu ponto de vista, Dona Fátima. Realmente, para quem usa o autocarro todos os dias, deve ser complicado.
options.translation: I understand your point of view, Dona Fátima. Indeed, for those who use the bus every day, it must be complicated.
options.feedback: Good job acknowledging her perspective first. Now you can elaborate or offer your own thoughts.

options.text: Eu acho que é uma medida necessária para otimizar os recursos, mas concordo que a comunicação podia ser melhor.
options.translation: I think it's a necessary measure to optimize resources, but I agree that communication could be better.
options.feedback: You've offered a nuanced opinion and found common ground. Be prepared for her response!

nodes.npc_text: Exatamente! E depois, o que acontece? As pessoas chegam atrasadas ao trabalho, ou perdem as consultas. É uma falta de respeito! E tu, usas muito os transportes públicos?
nodes.npc_translation: Exactly! And then what happens? People arrive late for work, or miss appointments. It's a lack of respect! And you, do you use public transport much?

options.text: Eu não uso tanto, mas tenho amigos que dependem deles e queixam-se imenso. Isso faz-me lembrar uma vez que...
options.translation: I don't use it as much, but I have friends who depend on them and complain a lot. That reminds me of a time when...
options.feedback: Excellent! You've linked it to a personal connection and are ready to share an anecdote, keeping the conversation flowing.

options.text: Não, eu costumo andar de carro, mas consigo imaginar a frustração. Talvez devessem ter feito uma consulta pública antes.
options.translation: No, I usually drive, but I can imagine the frustration. Maybe they should have done a public consultation beforehand.
options.feedback: You've offered a solution and shown empathy. This is a good way to contribute constructively.

nodes.npc_text: Otimizar recursos? Mas à custa de quem? As pessoas não são números! Mas sim, a comunicação é sempre um problema. Por falar nisso, já viste as notícias sobre o novo projeto de turismo rural na serra?
nodes.npc_translation: Optimize resources? But at whose expense? People are not numbers! But yes, communication is always a problem. Speaking of which, have you seen the news about the new rural tourism project in the mountains?

options.text: Sim, vi algo. Parece interessante, mas tenho algumas reservas sobre o impacto ambiental.
options.translation: Yes, I saw something. It seems interesting, but I have some reservations about the environmental impact.
options.feedback: You've transitioned to a new topic and offered a thoughtful, slightly critical opinion. Good for L5.

options.text: Não, não vi. Conta-me mais! É perto de alguma levada que conheço?
options.translation: No, I haven't seen it. Tell me more! Is it near a levada I know?
options.feedback: You've shown interest and asked for more details, keeping her engaged. Good for sustaining conversation.

nodes.npc_text: Ah, conta, conta! Adoro uma boa história! Foi algo relacionado com atrasos também?
nodes.npc_translation: Ah, tell me, tell me! I love a good story! Was it also related to delays?

options.text: Pois, foi uma vez que o meu amigo ia para o aeroporto e o autocarro avariou a meio caminho. Quase perdia o voo!
options.translation: Well, it was once when my friend was going to the airport and the bus broke down halfway. He almost missed his flight!
options.feedback: A good, relatable anecdote. This ends the specific branch, but the conversation could continue informally.

nodes.npc_text: Pois, isso é que é! Mas eles nunca perguntam a ninguém. É sempre de cima para baixo. Mas olha, não vamos esticar a conversa só sobre isto. Já provaste os bolinhos de mel que fiz?
nodes.npc_translation: Yes, that's it! But they never ask anyone. It's always top-down. But look, let's not prolong the conversation just about this. Have you tried the honey cakes I made?

options.text: Ainda não, mas têm um aspeto delicioso! Adoro bolinhos de mel. Qual é o segredo da tua receita, Dona Fátima?
options.translation: Not yet, but they look delicious! I love honey cakes. What's the secret to your recipe, Dona Fátima?
options.feedback: You've smoothly transitioned to a new, lighter topic and shown interest in her skills. This is excellent social interaction.

nodes.npc_text: Sim, é um assunto melindroso, não é? Por um lado, traz investimento, mas por outro... a natureza é tão frágil. Mas diz-me, o que achas que podiam fazer para mitigar isso?
nodes.npc_translation: Yes, it's a sensitive topic, isn't it? On one hand, it brings investment, but on the other... nature is so fragile. But tell me, what do you think they could do to mitigate that?

options.text: Talvez pudessem implementar mais energias renováveis no projeto e limitar o número de visitantes por dia.
options.translation: Perhaps they could implement more renewable energy in the project and limit the number of visitors per day.
options.feedback: You've offered concrete suggestions, showing critical thinking and problem-solving skills, perfect for L5.

nodes.npc_text: É mais para os lados da Serra de Água, sim. É um conceito de 'glamping', sabes? Tendas de luxo no meio da natureza. Eu acho que é uma boa ideia, p'ra atrair um turismo diferente.
nodes.npc_translation: It's more towards the Serra de Água, yes. It's a 'glamping' concept, you know? Luxury tents in the middle of nature. I think it's a good idea, to attract a different kind of tourism.

options.text: Ah, 'glamping'! Já ouvi falar. Pode ser interessante, sim, mas espero que respeitem a paisagem natural.
options.translation: Ah, 'glamping'! I've heard of it. It could be interesting, yes, but I hope they respect the natural landscape.
options.feedback: You've understood the concept and offered a balanced opinion, showing concern for the environment. Good for L5.

mission.title: Participate Actively in a Social Gathering
prep: Review the phrase patterns for introducing topics, agreeing, and disagreeing politely.
Practice using vocabulary related to opinions and conversation flow.
Think of a short, personal anecdote you could share if the opportunity arises.
Mentally prepare to ask open-ended questions to keep the conversation going.
fallback_phrases: Não percebi bem, o que queres dizer com isso?
Isso é interessante, nunca tinha pensado por esse lado.
likely_responses: Olha, por acaso, aconteceu-me uma coisa parecida...
Não tinha pensado nisso, é um bom ponto.
Mas será que isso vai mesmo resultar?

goals: Sustain a multi-topic conversation for an extended period.
Express opinions, agreement, and polite disagreement on various subjects.
Introduce personal anecdotes and link them to the ongoing discussion.
Transition smoothly between different topics while maintaining engagement.
Understand and respond appropriately to nuanced opinions and potential humour.

review_items.prompt: Say in Portuguese: By the way, I was thinking about what you told me about {topic}...
review_items.answer: Por acaso, estava a pensar no que me disseste sobre {topic}...

review_items.prompt: Say in Portuguese: I agree with you, but on the other hand, I also think that {alternative_view}.
review_items.answer: Concordo contigo, mas por outro lado, também acho que {alternative_view}.

review_items.prompt: Say in Portuguese: That reminds me of a funny story that happened to me with {person_or_situation}.
review_items.answer: Isso faz-me lembrar uma história engraçada que me aconteceu com {person_or_situation}.

review_items.prompt: Say in Portuguese: And you, what do you think about {current_topic}?
review_items.answer: E tu, o que achas sobre {current_topic}?

review_items.prompt: Recall the Portuguese for: to vent, to get something off one's chest
Recall the Portuguese for: to exchange views/ideas
Recall the Portuguese for: to prolong the conversation
Recall the Portuguese for: to have a good laugh
Listen to the dialogue at natural speed and answer: what is being asked for?

situations.title: Graduation: The Integrated Resident
situations.summary: The final: a messy, multi-context, full-speed day drawing on all six months — service counters, neighbours, officialdom, hosting and the long table — the integrated-resident test.

phrase_patterns.base: Poderia dar uma vista de olhos a isto, por favor?
phrase_patterns.translation: Could you take a look at this, please?

variants.text: Pode dar uma olhadela a isto?
variants.translation: Can you take a quick look at this?
variants.note: More casual, common with peers or service staff you know.

phrase_patterns.base: Não se preocupe, eu trato disso.
phrase_patterns.translation: Don't worry, I'll take care of it.

variants.translation: You can leave it, I'll handle it.

vocabulary.translation: to sort out, to deal with, to dispatch
vocabulary.note: Very common in Portugal for handling tasks/bureaucracy.

vocabulary.translation: paperwork (informal, often implies a lot or tedious)

vocabulary.note: Common term for espresso in Portugal, especially Lisbon/Madeira.
Reduction of 'para já'.

dialogues.title: At the Junta de Freguesia
dialogues.context: You're at the local parish council (Junta de Freguesia) to clarify a document for your residency.

lines.speaker: Funcionário
lines.text: Bom dia. Diga lá!
lines.translation: Good morning. How can I help?

lines.speaker: Learner
lines.text: Bom dia. Eu queria só confirmar um assunto sobre este documento, se faz favor. É a minha prova de morada.
lines.translation: Good morning. I just wanted to confirm something about this document, please. It's my proof of address.

lines.speaker: Funcionário
lines.text: Ah, sim. Deixe-me ver... Pois é, falta aqui uma assinatura. Terá de voltar com ela já carimbada.
lines.translation: Ah, yes. Let me see... Right, it's missing a signature here. You'll have to come back with it already stamped.

lines.speaker: Learner
lines.text: Ah, pois. Entendido. Onde é que consigo o carimbo?
lines.translation: Ah, right. Understood. Where can I get the stamp?

lines.speaker: Funcionário
lines.text: Tem de ir à Conservatória do Registo Civil, ali na Rua da Carreira. Mas não se preocupe, é rápido.
lines.translation: You have to go to the Civil Registry Office, there on Rua da Carreira. But don't worry, it's quick.

lines.speaker: Learner
lines.text: P'ra já! Muito obrigado pela ajuda.
lines.translation: Right away! Thank you very much for the help.

dialogues.title: A Quick Chat with a Neighbour
dialogues.context: You bump into your neighbour, Dona Alice, while leaving the Junta de Freguesia.

lines.speaker: Dona Alice
lines.text: Ora viva! Tudo bem? A despachar a papelada, pois é?
lines.translation: Oh, hello! All well? Dealing with paperwork, I see?

lines.speaker: Learner
lines.text: Dona Alice! Tudo bem, sim. É mesmo isso, a tentar resolver uns assuntos.
lines.translation: Dona Alice! All well, yes. Exactly, trying to sort out some matters.

lines.speaker: Dona Alice
lines.text: Pois é, a vida do imigrante. Olha, na sexta-feira à noite vamos ter um pequeno convívio lá em casa, com um bolo do caco e poncha fresquinha. Apareça!
lines.translation: That's right, the immigrant's life. Look, on Friday evening we're having a small get-together at home, with bolo do caco and fresh poncha. Come along!

lines.speaker: Learner
lines.text: Que bom! Muito obrigado pelo convite, Dona Alice. Vou tentar, sim. Que horas?
lines.translation: How nice! Thank you very much for the invitation, Dona Alice. I'll try, yes. What time?

lines.speaker: Dona Alice
lines.text: A partir das oito, quando puder. É só chegar. Até já!
lines.translation: From eight o'clock, whenever you can. Just arrive. See you soon!

cultural_notes.title: Navigating Portuguese Bureaucracy
cultural_notes.body: In Portugal, administrative processes often require multiple steps and in-person visits. It's common to be told to get a stamp, an extra document, or return another day. Patience and politeness are key. Using phrases like 'se faz favor' (please) and 'muito obrigado pela ajuda' (thank you for the help) can smooth interactions. The term 'despachar' is frequently used for sorting out such matters.

cultural_notes.title: Madeiran Social Invitations
cultural_notes.body: Invitations from neighbours or friends are often informal and spontaneous. 'Apareça!' (Come along!) or 'É só chegar' (Just arrive) are common ways to extend an open invitation, without a strict RSVP. It's a sign of integration when you receive such invitations, often including local specialities like 'bolo do caco' (Madeiran flatbread) and 'poncha' (local drink).

roleplay.scenario: You're trying to tie up loose ends on a busy day. You need to pick up a specific item from a local shop, but they don't have it. Then, you receive an unexpected call from the Junta de Freguesia about your document. Finally, you have to confirm your attendance at your neighbour's social gathering.

nodes.npc_text: Bom dia! Posso ajudar?
nodes.npc_translation: Good morning! Can I help you?

options.text: Bom dia. Queria saber se têm o {item} em stock, por favor.
options.translation: Good morning. I wanted to know if you have the {item} in stock, please.
options.feedback: Good, you're polite and direct. Now, choose an item to ask about.

options.text: Olá. Estou à procura de um {item} específico.
options.translation: Hello. I'm looking for a specific {item}.
options.feedback: Also good, slightly less formal but still clear. Now, choose an item.

nodes.npc_text: Um {item}? Deixe-me verificar... Ah, lamento, mas não temos esse {item} em particular. Está esgotado de momento. Quer que veja se consigo encomendar?
nodes.npc_translation: A {item}? Let me check... Ah, I'm sorry, but we don't have that particular {item}. It's out of stock at the moment. Do you want me to see if I can order it?

options.text: Pois, não vale a pena encomendar, preciso dele p'ra já. Onde é que eu poderia encontrá-lo, talvez?
options.translation: Right, no point in ordering, I need it right away. Where might I find it, perhaps?
options.feedback: Excellent use of 'p'ra já' and asking for alternatives.

options.text: Não, não precisa de encomendar. É urgente. Muito obrigado na mesma.
options.translation: No, no need to order. It's urgent. Thank you anyway.
options.feedback: Clear and polite, but you could try to get more information.

nodes.npc_text: Hum... talvez na loja 'Tudo Mais', ali na rua de trás. Tente lá. (O seu telemóvel toca.)
nodes.npc_translation: Hmm... perhaps at 'Tudo Mais' shop, just on the street behind. Try there. (Your phone rings.)

options.text: Muito obrigado pela indicação! Tenha um bom dia. (Atende o telemóvel.)
options.translation: Thank you very much for the tip! Have a good day. (Answers the phone.)
options.feedback: Good, polite exit and transition.

options.text: Ah, obrigado! Vou lá ver. (Atende o telemóvel.)
options.translation: Ah, thanks! I'll go check. (Answers the phone.)
options.feedback: A bit more informal, but still effective. Good transition.

nodes.npc_text: Olá, é da Junta de Freguesia. Ligamos a propósito do seu documento. Esquecemos de lhe dizer que precisa também de uma cópia do seu Cartão de Cidadão. Pode trazer hoje?
nodes.npc_translation: Hello, it's from the Junta de Freguesia. We're calling about your document. We forgot to tell you that you also need a copy of your Citizen Card. Can you bring it today?

options.text: Pois é, claro. Já estou a caminho da Conservatória, mas passo aí depois com a cópia. Obrigado por avisar!
options.translation: Right, of course. I'm already on my way to the Registry Office, but I'll stop by afterwards with the copy. Thanks for letting me know!
options.feedback: Excellent! You've shown you're organised and proactive.

options.text: Ah, sim. Sem problema. Passo aí mais logo com isso. Até já.
options.translation: Ah, yes. No problem. I'll stop by later with that. See you soon.
options.feedback: Good, clear and polite. 'Mais logo' is a good choice for 'later today'.

nodes.npc_text: (Desliga o telemóvel. De repente, lembra-se da Dona Alice e do convite para sexta-feira.)
nodes.npc_translation: (You hang up the phone. Suddenly, you remember Dona Alice and the invitation for Friday.)

options.text: Tenho de enviar uma mensagem à Dona Alice a confirmar que vou ao convívio.
options.translation: I need to send a message to Dona Alice confirming I'm going to the get-together.
options.feedback: Good, proactive social follow-up.

options.text: Melhor ligar à Dona Alice para confirmar a minha presença na sexta-feira.
options.translation: Better call Dona Alice to confirm my presence on Friday.
options.feedback: A call is often preferred for more personal confirmations.

nodes.npc_text: (A Dona Alice responde com um 'Ótimo, ficamos à sua espera!'. Missão cumprida: burocracia e social, tudo despachado.)
nodes.npc_translation: (Dona Alice replies with 'Great, we'll be waiting for you!'. Mission accomplished: bureaucracy and social, all sorted.)

mission.title: Integrated Resident: The Daily Hustle
prep: Review vocabulary for administrative tasks and social interactions.
Practice polite requests and confirmations.
Mentally prepare for unexpected changes in plans.
fallback_phrases: Desculpe, não percebi. Pode repetir, por favor?
Pode falar um pouco mais devagar?
likely_responses: Pois é, a vida é assim!

goals: Successfully navigate a multi-context daily scenario, including administrative and social interactions.
Demonstrate flexibility in responding to unexpected changes and requests.
Utilise appropriate register and politeness strategies in varied contexts.
Confirm social engagements and manage logistical information effectively.

review_items.prompt: Say in Portuguese: Is everything {status}?
Say in Portuguese: Could you take a look at this, please?
review_items.answer: Poderia dar uma vista de olhos a isto, por favor?

review_items.prompt: Say in Portuguese: So, when are we {verb}?
Say in Portuguese: Don't worry, I'll take care of it.
review_items.answer: Não se preocupe, eu trato disso.

review_items.prompt: Recall the Portuguese for: to sort out, to deal with, to dispatch
Recall the Portuguese for: paperwork (informal, often implies a lot or tedious)
Recall the Portuguese for: social gathering, fellowship
Recall the Portuguese for: matter, topic, issue
Listen to the dialogue at natural speed and answer: what is being asked for?

situations.title: Calling Finanças
situations.summary: Phoning the tax office (Finanças) about your NIF or a tax matter: waiting, explaining your situation, and understanding what document you must bring.

phrase_patterns.base: Estou a ligar sobre o meu {assunto}.

variants.text: Ligo para saber sobre o meu {assunto}.
variants.translation: I'm calling to find out about my {topic}.

phrase_patterns.base: Preciso de saber que documentos preciso de trazer.
phrase_patterns.translation: I need to know what documents I need to bring.

variants.text: Pode dizer-me quais documentos são precisos?
variants.translation: Can you tell me which documents are needed?

phrase_patterns.base: Tenho um problema com a minha {situação}.
phrase_patterns.translation: I have a problem with my {situation}.

phrase_patterns.base: É sobre o meu NIF, está {estado} ou não consigo {ação}.
phrase_patterns.translation: It's about my NIF, it's {state} or I can't {action}.

vocabulary.note: Common name for Autoridade Tributária e Aduaneira
Número de Identificação Fiscal, essential for everything in Portugal
Portuguese national ID card

dialogues.title: Initial Call to Finanças
dialogues.context: You're calling the Finanças (tax office) in Madeira because you have an issue with your NIF.

lines.speaker: Atendimento Finanças
lines.text: Autoridade Tributária e Aduaneira, boa tarde. Em que posso ajudar?
lines.translation: Tax and Customs Authority, good afternoon. How can I help?

lines.speaker: Você
lines.text: Boa tarde. Ligo sobre o meu NIF. Está inativo e preciso de o ativar.
lines.translation: Good afternoon. I'm calling about my NIF. It's inactive and I need to activate it.

lines.speaker: Atendimento Finanças
lines.text: Pois, entendo. Para ativação de NIF, terá mesmo de se dirigir a um balcão de atendimento. Não conseguimos fazer isso por telefone.
lines.translation: Right, I understand. For NIF activation, you'll really have to go to a service desk. We can't do that over the phone.

lines.speaker: Você
lines.text: Ah, pois. E que documentos preciso de levar?
lines.translation: Ah, right. And what documents do I need to bring?

lines.speaker: Atendimento Finanças
lines.text: Precisa do seu Cartão de Cidadão ou passaporte, e um comprovativo de morada recente. E, se for o caso, a declaração de início de atividade.
lines.translation: You need your Citizen Card or passport, and a recent proof of address. And, if applicable, your business registration statement.

lines.speaker: Você
lines.text: Certo. Muito obrigado pela informação.
lines.translation: Okay. Thank you very much for the information.

dialogues.title: Clarifying a Tax Declaration Issue
dialogues.context: You're calling Finanças again, this time about a problem with your tax declaration.

lines.speaker: Atendimento Finanças
lines.text: Finanças, diga.
lines.translation: Finanças, speak.

lines.speaker: Você
lines.text: Boa tarde. Tenho um problema com a minha declaração de IRS do ano passado. Acho que há um erro.
lines.translation: Good afternoon. I have a problem with my IRS declaration from last year. I think there's an error.

lines.speaker: Atendimento Finanças
lines.text: Pois, para questões de retificação de declarações, é algo que normalmente se resolve online no Portal das Finanças. Já tentou por lá?
lines.translation: Right, for declaration rectification issues, it's something usually resolved online on the Finanças Portal. Have you tried there?

lines.speaker: Você
lines.text: Já tentei, mas não consigo. É um bocado complicado p'ra mim. Será que posso agendar um atendimento presencial?
lines.translation: I've tried, but I can't. It's a bit complicated for me. Can I schedule an in-person appointment?

lines.speaker: Atendimento Finanças
lines.text: Sim, claro. Tem de aceder ao Portal das Finanças e procurar por 'Agendamento'. Lá pode escolher o balcão e a hora que lhe der mais jeito.
lines.translation: Yes, of course. You have to access the Finanças Portal and look for 'Appointment'. There you can choose the office and time that suits you best.

lines.speaker: Você
lines.text: Ah, muito bem. Então vou tentar fazer isso. Obrigada.
lines.translation: Ah, very good. So I'll try to do that. Thank you.

cultural_notes.title: Patience with Bureaucracy
cultural_notes.body: Dealing with government offices (like Finanças) in Portugal often requires patience. Phone lines can be busy, and some issues require an in-person visit. Always be prepared to wait a bit or be redirected. It's common to be told to resolve issues online first.

cultural_notes.title: The Importance of NIF
cultural_notes.body: The NIF (Número de Identificação Fiscal) is your tax identification number and is absolutely crucial for almost all transactions in Portugal, from opening a bank account to buying groceries (if you want to add it to a receipt for tax purposes). Keep it handy and know its status.

roleplay.scenario: You are calling the Finanças office in Funchal, Madeira, because your NIF seems to be inactive or incorrect, and you cannot use it for an important transaction. You need to understand how to fix it and what documents to bring.

nodes.npc_text: Autoridade Tributária e Aduaneira, boa tarde. Em que posso ajudar?
nodes.npc_translation: Tax and Customs Authority, good afternoon. How can I help?

options.text: Boa tarde. Ligo sobre um problema com o meu NIF.
options.translation: Good afternoon. I'm calling about a problem with my NIF.
options.feedback: A good, direct start. 'Ligo sobre' is perfect for stating the reason for your call.

options.text: Diga. O meu NIF não está a funcionar.
options.translation: Speak. My NIF isn't working.
options.feedback: A bit informal with 'Diga' for a first contact with a service, but acceptable. Make sure to be polite next time.

nodes.npc_text: Pois não. Qual é o problema específico com o seu NIF?
nodes.npc_translation: Right. What is the specific problem with your NIF?

options.text: Parece que está inativo. Não consigo utilizá-lo para nada.
options.translation: It seems to be inactive. I can't use it for anything.
options.feedback: Clearly stating the problem helps the agent direct you correctly.

options.text: Não sei, está incorreto ou algo do género.
options.translation: I don't know, it's incorrect or something like that.
options.feedback: Try to be more specific if you can, but this is understandable. The agent will likely ask for more details.

nodes.npc_text: Compreendo. Para resolver problemas de NIF inativo ou incorreto, terá de se dirigir pessoalmente a um balcão de atendimento. Não é possível fazer isso por telefone.
nodes.npc_translation: I understand. To resolve issues with an inactive or incorrect NIF, you will need to go in person to a service desk. It's not possible to do that over the phone.

options.text: Ah, pois. E que documentos preciso de levar comigo?
options.translation: Ah, right. And what documents do I need to bring with me?
options.feedback: Excellent follow-up question. Always ask what to bring before going to a government office.

options.text: Certo. Tenho de ir lá. Obrigada.
options.translation: Okay. I have to go there. Thank you.
options.feedback: This ends the call, but you missed an opportunity to ask about the required documents. You'll need to call again or risk a wasted trip.

nodes.npc_text: Para ativar ou corrigir o NIF, precisará do seu Cartão de Cidadão ou passaporte, e um comprovativo de morada recente. Leve os originais, por favor.
nodes.npc_translation: To activate or correct your NIF, you will need your Citizen Card or passport, and a recent proof of address. Please bring the originals.

options.text: Certo. Cartão de Cidadão e comprovativo de morada. Muito obrigada pela ajuda.
options.translation: Okay. Citizen Card and proof of address. Thank you very much for your help.
options.feedback: Confirming the information is a good way to ensure you understood correctly.

options.text: Só o Cartão de Cidadão? Não preciso de mais nada?
options.translation: Only the Citizen Card? I don't need anything else?
options.feedback: It's good to clarify, but the agent did mention 'comprovativo de morada'. Listen carefully.

nodes.npc_text: Não, como referi, precisa do Cartão de Cidadão E um comprovativo de morada. São os dois documentos essenciais. E os originais, sim.
nodes.npc_translation: No, as I mentioned, you need your Citizen Card AND a proof of address. Those are the two essential documents. And the originals, yes.

options.text: Peço desculpa, entendi mal. Muito obrigada por esclarecer.
options.translation: I apologize, I misunderstood. Thank you very much for clarifying.
options.feedback: Apologizing when you've misunderstood is polite and appropriate.

nodes.npc_text: De nada. Tenha um bom dia.
nodes.npc_translation: You're welcome. Have a good day.

mission.title: Prepare for a Finanças Visit
prep: Review the key vocabulary for Finanças and documents.
Practice asking about the required documents.
Mentally prepare for the possibility of needing to visit the office in person.
fallback_phrases: Não percebi bem. Pode explicar de novo?

Precisa do seu Cartão de Cidadão e comprovativo de morada.
Não é possível fazer isso por telefone.

phrase_patterns.base: Preciso de um atestado de residência para {motivo}.
phrase_patterns.translation: I need a certificate of residency for {reason}.

slots.name: motivo
slots.description: The reason for needing the certificate

variants.text: Queria pedir um atestado de residência, se faz favor.
variants.translation: I'd like to ask for a certificate of residency, please.

variants.text: O que é que preciso de trazer?
variants.translation: What do I need to bring?

slots.description: The specific purpose for the certificate

vocabulary.note: A formal document issued by the local parish council (Junta de Freguesia).
The lowest tier of local government in Portugal, responsible for local administrative tasks.
Often a utility bill (água, luz, gás) or a bank statement.
The national identity card for Portuguese citizens, also used by foreign residents with a valid residence permit.
A formal document or form used to make an official request.
Essential for almost all administrative and financial activities in Portugal.

dialogues.title: Applying for Residency Certificate
dialogues.context: At the Junta de Freguesia office in Madeira. The learner needs a residency certificate for administrative purposes.

lines.speaker: Atendente
lines.text: Bom dia. Diga, em que posso ajudar?
lines.translation: Good morning. Tell me, how can I help?

lines.speaker: Learner
lines.text: Bom dia. Preciso de um atestado de residência, se faz favor.
lines.translation: Good morning. I need a certificate of residency, please.

lines.speaker: Atendente
lines.text: Pois, claro. E para que é que precisa do atestado? É para algum fim específico?
lines.translation: Yes, of course. And what do you need the certificate for? Is it for a specific purpose?

lines.speaker: Learner
lines.text: É para a segurança social, para a inscrição.
lines.translation: It's for social security, for registration.

lines.speaker: Atendente
lines.text: Certo. Nesse caso, vai precisar do seu documento de identificação e de um comprovativo de morada, como uma fatura da água ou da luz, em seu nome.
lines.translation: Right. In that case, you'll need your identification document and proof of address, like a water or electricity bill, in your name.

lines.speaker: Learner
lines.text: Tenho aqui o meu título de residência e um recibo da água.
lines.translation: I have my residence permit and a water bill here.

lines.speaker: Atendente
lines.text: Ótimo, é mesmo isso. Preencha este requerimento, faz favor. Já está quase.
lines.translation: Great, that's exactly it. Please fill out this form. It'll be ready soon.

cultural_notes.title: The Role of the Junta de Freguesia
cultural_notes.body: In Portugal, the Junta de Freguesia (Parish Council) is the local administrative body responsible for many day-to-day citizen services. This includes issuing certificates like 'atestados de residência' (certificates of residency), which are often required for various bureaucratic processes such as applying for a NIF, registering with social security, or enrolling children in school. It's a fundamental point of contact for residents, especially for those new to a parish.

cultural_notes.title: Proof of Address (Comprovativo de Morada)
cultural_notes.body: When asked for 'comprovativo de morada' (proof of address) in Portugal, the most commonly accepted documents are recent utility bills (faturas de água, luz, gás) or bank statements. These documents must be in your name and show your current address. It's always a good idea to have a recent one (less than 3 months old) available when dealing with official matters.

roleplay.scenario: You are at the Junta de Freguesia counter. You need a certificate of residency to enroll your child in a local school. The attendant is asking for details and documents.

nodes.npc_text: Boa tarde. Em que posso ser útil?
nodes.npc_translation: Good afternoon. How can I be of assistance?

options.text: Boa tarde. Eu preciso de um atestado de residência, por favor.
options.translation: Good afternoon. I need a certificate of residency, please.
options.feedback: Excellent start! Direct and polite.

options.text: Olá. Queria um atestado de morada.
options.translation: Hello. I wanted an address certificate.
options.feedback: Good attempt, but 'atestado de residência' is the correct term. 'Atestado de morada' might be understood but isn't standard.

nodes.npc_text: Certo. E qual é o motivo? Para que é que precisa dele?
nodes.npc_translation: Right. And what's the reason? What do you need it for?

options.text: É para a escola do meu filho, para a inscrição.
options.translation: It's for my son's school, for registration.
options.feedback: Perfect, clear and concise explanation.

options.text: Preciso para a escola, para o meu filho.
options.translation: I need it for school, for my son.
options.feedback: Understood, but 'para a inscrição' (for registration) adds more precision.

nodes.npc_text: Um atestado de residência, certo? E para que é que precisa dele?
nodes.npc_translation: A certificate of residency, right? And what do you need it for?

options.text: Sim, isso mesmo. É para a escola do meu filho, para a inscrição.
options.translation: Yes, that's right. It's for my son's school, for registration.
options.feedback: Good job correcting yourself and providing the full reason.

nodes.npc_text: Ah, para a escola. Entendido. Então, para isso, vai precisar do seu cartão de cidadão ou título de residência e de um comprovativo de morada, como uma fatura da água, luz ou telefone, em seu nome.
nodes.npc_translation: Ah, for school. Understood. So, for that, you'll need your citizen card or residence permit and proof of address, like a water, electricity, or phone bill, in your name.

options.text: Tenho aqui o meu título de residência e um recibo da água.
options.translation: I have my residence permit and a water bill here.
options.feedback: Excellent! You have the correct documents.

options.text: Só tenho o meu passaporte e um contrato de arrendamento.
options.translation: I only have my passport and a rental agreement.
options.feedback: The passport is fine for ID, but a rental agreement isn't always accepted as proof of address. Be prepared for a follow-up question.

nodes.npc_text: O passaporte serve para a identificação. Quanto ao comprovativo de morada, o contrato de arrendamento pode não ser suficiente. Não tem uma fatura recente da água ou da luz em seu nome?
nodes.npc_translation: The passport works for identification. As for proof of address, the rental agreement might not be enough. Don't you have a recent water or electricity bill in your name?

options.text: Ah, não. Não tenho. Posso trazer amanhã?
options.translation: Oh, no. I don't. Can I bring it tomorrow?
options.feedback: Good, you're asking for a solution. This is a common real-life scenario.

options.text: Não, só tenho mesmo o contrato.
options.translation: No, I only have the contract.
options.feedback: You might need to find another document. The attendant will likely advise you to return.

nodes.npc_text: Pois, sem um comprovativo de morada aceite, não consigo emitir o atestado. Se tiver a fatura da água ou da luz, pode vir amanhã. O atendimento é das 9h às 16h.
nodes.npc_translation: Well, without an accepted proof of address, I can't issue the certificate. If you have the water or electricity bill, you can come tomorrow. Service is from 9 am to 4 pm.

options.text: Está bem, muito obrigado. Volto amanhã.
options.translation: Alright, thank you very much. I'll come back tomorrow.
options.feedback: You've successfully navigated the situation, even with a minor setback. Good politeness.

nodes.npc_text: Ótimo! Preencha este pequeno requerimento, faz favor. Em cinco minutos está pronto.
nodes.npc_translation: Great! Please fill out this small form. It will be ready in five minutes.

options.text: Muito obrigado/a!
options.translation: Thank you very much!
options.feedback: Success! You got your certificate.

dialogues.title: Opening an Account at the Bank
dialogues.context: A client is at a bank branch in Funchal, asking to open a new account and about the process.

lines.speaker: Balconista
lines.text: Bom dia! Em que posso ajudar?
lines.translation: Good morning! How can I help?

lines.speaker: Cliente
lines.text: Bom dia. Queria abrir uma conta, por favor.
lines.translation: Good morning. I'd like to open an account, please.

lines.speaker: Balconista
lines.text: Pois não. Já tem algum documento consigo?
lines.translation: Of course. Do you have any documents with you?

lines.speaker: Cliente
lines.text: Sim, tenho o meu passaporte e o NIF.
lines.translation: Yes, I have my passport and NIF.

lines.speaker: Balconista
lines.text: Ótimo. E um comprovativo de morada?
lines.translation: Great. And proof of address?

lines.speaker: Cliente
lines.text: Ah, um comprovativo de morada... hum, tenho uma conta da luz, serve?
lines.translation: Ah, proof of address... um, I have an electricity bill, does that work?

lines.speaker: Balconista
lines.text: Serve sim. E também vamos precisar de um comprovativo de rendimentos.
lines.translation: Yes, it works. And we'll also need proof of income.

lines.speaker: Cliente
lines.text: Ah, certo. Um recibo de vencimento, por exemplo?
lines.translation: Ah, right. A payslip, for example?

lines.speaker: Balconista
lines.text: Exatamente. Depois disso, preenchemos os formulários e escolhemos o tipo de cartão.
lines.translation: Exactly. After that, we fill out the forms and choose the card type.

lines.speaker: Cliente
lines.text: E acesso à app, é possível?
lines.translation: And app access, is that possible?

lines.speaker: Balconista
lines.text: Sim, claro. Configuramos tudo na hora.
lines.translation: Yes, of course. We set everything up right away.

cultural_notes.title: Required Documents for Bank Accounts in Portugal
cultural_notes.body: To open a bank account in Portugal, you typically need several documents: an identification document (passport or Portuguese ID card), a NIF (Número de Identificação Fiscal - tax identification number), proof of address (comprovativo de morada, e.g., a utility bill or residence certificate), and proof of income (comprovativo de rendimentos, e.g., a payslip or employment contract).

cultural_notes.title: The Multibanco Network
cultural_notes.body: Multibanco is Portugal's interbank network, offering a wide range of services beyond just cash withdrawals. You can pay bills, top up mobile phones, buy concert tickets, and even pay taxes at any Multibanco ATM. It's a very convenient and widely used system across the country, including Madeira.

roleplay.scenario: You are at a bank counter in Funchal, trying to open a new account. The bank clerk is guiding you through the process.

nodes.npc_text: Bom dia! Em que posso ajudar?
nodes.npc_translation: Good morning! How can I help?

options.text: Queria abrir uma conta bancária, por favor.
options.translation: I'd like to open a bank account, please.
options.feedback: Excellent, direct and polite.

options.text: Preciso de informações sobre como abrir uma conta.
options.translation: I need information on how to open an account.
options.feedback: A good way to start if you're unsure, leading to the same information.

nodes.npc_text: Claro. Para abrir uma conta, preciso do seu documento de identificação, NIF, comprovativo de morada e comprovativo de rendimentos.
nodes.npc_translation: Of course. To open an account, I need your identification document, NIF, proof of address, and proof of income.

options.text: Tenho tudo comigo.
options.translation: I have everything with me.
options.feedback: Great, you're prepared!

options.text: O que é um comprovativo de morada?
options.translation: What is proof of address?
options.feedback: Good question to clarify an important document.

nodes.npc_text: Um comprovativo de morada pode ser uma fatura da água, luz ou telefone em seu nome, ou um atestado de residência da Junta de Freguesia.
nodes.npc_translation: Proof of address can be a water, electricity, or phone bill in your name, or a residence certificate from the Parish Council.

options.text: Ah, sim, tenho uma fatura da luz.
options.translation: Ah, yes, I have an electricity bill.
options.feedback: Perfect, you've identified the correct document.

options.text: Não tenho nenhum desses agora.
options.translation: I don't have any of those right now.
options.feedback: It's important to be honest, but this will halt the process for now.

nodes.npc_text: Ótimo. Agora, quanto ao cartão, prefere um de débito ou de crédito? E quer acesso ao homebanking e à app?
nodes.npc_translation: Great. Now, regarding the card, do you prefer debit or credit? And do you want access to homebanking and the app?

options.text: Um cartão de débito, por favor, e sim, acesso à app e ao homebanking.
options.translation: A debit card, please, and yes, access to the app and homebanking.
options.feedback: You've clearly stated your preferences.

options.text: Só um cartão de débito, por enquanto.
options.translation: Just a debit card, for now.
options.feedback: A valid choice if you want to keep it simple.

nodes.npc_text: Perfeito. Vamos preencher os formulários, e depois o cartão será enviado para a sua morada. O acesso à app configuramos já.
nodes.npc_translation: Perfect. We'll fill out the forms, and then the card will be sent to your address. We'll set up app access now.

options.text: Muito obrigado/a! Parece simples.
options.translation: Thank you very much! It seems simple.
options.feedback: A polite and appreciative response.

options.text: Quanto tempo demora até o cartão chegar?
options.translation: How long does it take for the card to arrive?
options.feedback: A practical question to manage expectations.

nodes.npc_text: Normalmente, o cartão chega em 5 a 7 dias úteis. Pode levantá-lo aqui ou recebê-lo em casa.
nodes.npc_translation: Normally, the card arrives in 5 to 7 business days. You can pick it up here or receive it at home.

options.text: Receber em casa é melhor. Obrigado/a!
options.translation: Receiving it at home is better. Thank you!
options.feedback: You've made your preference clear.

nodes.npc_text: Sem os documentos completos, não consigo prosseguir. Por favor, volte quando os tiver.
nodes.npc_translation: Without the complete documents, I cannot proceed. Please come back when you have them.

nodes.npc_text: De nada! Se tiver mais alguma questão, diga.
nodes.npc_translation: You're welcome! If you have any more questions, just ask.

lines.speaker: Rececionista
lines.text: Bom dia, em que posso ajudar?

cultural_notes.title: The 'Centro de Saúde' in Portugal
cultural_notes.body: The 'centro de saúde' is the primary point of contact for public healthcare in Portugal, including Madeira. It's where you register to access the national health service (SNS), get a family doctor, and have routine appointments. It's not typically for emergencies, which go to hospitals.

cultural_notes.title: The 'Médico de Família'
cultural_notes.body: Having a 'médico de família' (family doctor) is central to the Portuguese public health system. They manage your general health, refer you to specialists, and prescribe medication. There can sometimes be a waiting list to be assigned one, especially in more populated areas.

roleplay.scenario: You are at the reception desk of a health centre in Funchal, Madeira. You need to register and ask about getting a family doctor.

nodes.npc_text: Bom dia. Em que posso ajudar?
nodes.npc_translation: Good morning. How can I help?

options.text: Bom dia. Queria registar-me aqui no centro de saúde.
options.translation: Good morning. I'd like to register here at the health centre.
options.feedback: Excellent start! Clear and polite.

options.text: Preciso de me inscrever para ter um médico.
options.translation: I need to sign up to have a doctor.
options.feedback: Good, but 'registar-me' is more common for initial registration. 'Inscrever-me' is also understood.

nodes.npc_text: Certo. Tem o seu cartão de cidadão e um comprovativo de morada consigo?
nodes.npc_translation: Alright. Do you have your citizen card and proof of address with you?

options.text: Sim, tenho tudo aqui. E também preciso de um médico de família.
options.translation: Yes, I have everything here. And I also need a family doctor.
options.feedback: Perfect! You provided the info and added your next request naturally.

options.text: Tenho o cartão de cidadão, mas não sei o que é um comprovativo de morada.
options.translation: I have my citizen card, but I don't know what a proof of address is.
options.feedback: Good, it's okay to ask for clarification. You're using 'não sei' correctly.

nodes.npc_text: É um documento que prova onde mora, como uma conta da água ou da eletricidade. Tem alguma dessas?
nodes.npc_translation: It's a document that proves where you live, like a water or electricity bill. Do you have one of those?

options.text: Ah, sim! Tenho a conta da luz. Está aqui.
options.translation: Oh, yes! I have the electricity bill. It's here.
options.feedback: Great, you understood and confirmed. 'Conta da luz' is a good example.

nodes.npc_text: Muito bem. Então, preencha, por favor, este formulário. Depois vemos a questão do médico de família.
nodes.npc_translation: Very good. So, please fill out this form. Afterwards, we'll look into the family doctor matter.

options.text: Pois, claro. Muito obrigado.
options.translation: Yes, of course. Thank you very much.
options.feedback: A polite and natural way to end the interaction for now. 'Pois, claro' is typically Portuguese.

options.text: E quanto tempo demora para ter o médico?
options.translation: And how long does it take to get the doctor?
options.feedback: A good follow-up question. You're asking about the process.

nodes.npc_text: Isso pode demorar um bocado, depende da disponibilidade. Mas assim que tiver o registo feito, já pode marcar consultas com qualquer médico de serviço.
nodes.npc_translation: That can take a while, it depends on availability. But once you're registered, you can already book appointments with any on-duty doctor.

options.text: Ah, está bem. Muito obrigado pela informação.
options.translation: Ah, okay. Thank you very much for the information.
options.feedback: You acknowledged the information and thanked them politely. A good, natural ending.

dialogues.context: A new resident calls the electricity company (EEM) in Madeira to set up service for their new home.

lines.speaker: Atendente
lines.text: EEM, bom dia. Em que posso ajudar?
lines.translation: EEM, good morning. How can I help?

lines.speaker: Cliente
lines.text: Bom dia. Queria ligar a luz na minha nova morada, por favor.
lines.translation: Good morning. I'd like to connect the electricity at my new address, please.

lines.speaker: Atendente
lines.text: Pois não. Qual é a morada completa?
lines.translation: Of course. What's the full address?

lines.speaker: Cliente
lines.text: É na Rua do Pescador, número quarenta e dois, primeiro andar, Caniço.
lines.translation: It's on Fisherman's Street, number forty-two, first floor, Caniço.

lines.speaker: Atendente
lines.text: Rua do Pescador, 42, 1º andar. Certo. E já tem a leitura do contador?
lines.translation: Fisherman's Street, 42, 1st floor. Right. And do you already have the meter reading?

lines.speaker: Cliente
lines.text: Sim, é cento e quarenta e cinco.
lines.translation: Yes, it's one hundred and forty-five.

lines.speaker: Atendente
lines.text: Obrigada. E como quer pagar a fatura? Prefere débito direto ou multibanco?
lines.translation: Thank you. And how do you want to pay the bill? Do you prefer direct debit or ATM payment?

lines.speaker: Cliente
lines.text: Débito direto, por favor.
lines.translation: Direct debit, please.

lines.speaker: Atendente
lines.text: Muito bem. A ligação da luz fica ativa hoje mesmo.
lines.translation: Very good. The electricity connection will be active today.

cultural_notes.title: Addressing Service Staff
cultural_notes.body: In Portugal, when speaking to service staff (e.g., utility companies, shops), it's common and polite to use the third-person singular verb forms (treating them as 'o senhor' or 'a senhora') even if they use 'tu' with you. This shows respect. Phrases like 'Queria ligar...' (I wanted to connect...) are also a polite, indirect way to state your request.

cultural_notes.title: Meter Readings and Payment Methods
cultural_notes.body: When setting up or transferring utility services, you will almost always be asked for the current meter reading ('leitura do contador'). It's a good idea to take a photo of the meter. For bill payment, 'débito direto' (direct debit) is the most common and convenient method for recurring bills. 'Multibanco' refers to the national ATM network and is a common way to pay bills manually using a reference number at an ATM or via online banking.

roleplay.scenario: You are calling the local water company (EMAS) in Funchal to set up water service for your new apartment. You need to provide your address, the meter reading, and choose a payment method.

nodes.npc_text: EMAS, bom dia. Em que posso ajudar?
nodes.npc_translation: EMAS, good morning. How can I help?

options.text: Bom dia. Queria ligar a água na minha nova morada.
options.translation: Good morning. I'd like to connect the water at my new address.
options.feedback: Excellent start! You clearly stated your purpose.

nodes.npc_text: Pois não. Qual é a morada completa, por favor?
nodes.npc_translation: Of course. What's the full address, please?

options.text: É na Rua das Hortas, número dez, rés do chão.
options.translation: It's on Hortas Street, number ten, ground floor.
options.feedback: Good job providing the address clearly. Remember to include the floor if applicable.

options.text: A morada é Rua das Hortas, dez.
options.translation: The address is Hortas Street, ten.
options.feedback: That's good, but try to be more specific, like 'número dez'.

nodes.npc_text: Rua das Hortas, número dez, certo? E qual o andar, por favor?
nodes.npc_translation: Hortas Street, number ten, right? And which floor, please?

options.text: É o rés do chão.
options.translation: It's the ground floor.
options.feedback: Perfect! 'Rés do chão' is the ground floor.

nodes.npc_text: Muito bem. E já tem a leitura do contador da água?
nodes.npc_translation: Very good. And do you already have the water meter reading?

options.text: Sim, é duzentos e três.
options.translation: Yes, it's two hundred and three.
options.feedback: Excellent, you gave the reading clearly.

options.text: Não, ainda não tenho a leitura.
options.translation: No, I don't have the reading yet.
options.feedback: This is a common issue. See how the agent responds.

nodes.npc_text: Então, pode verificar e ligar-nos de novo, ou podemos enviar um técnico para fazer a leitura. Prefere qual?
nodes.npc_translation: Then, you can check and call us back, or we can send a technician to take the reading. Which do you prefer?

options.text: Vou verificar e ligo mais tarde.
options.translation: I will check and call later.
options.feedback: Good, you chose to handle it yourself. The call ends here for now.

nodes.npc_text: Obrigada. E como quer pagar a fatura? Prefere débito direto ou multibanco?
nodes.npc_translation: Thank you. And how do you want to pay the bill? Do you prefer direct debit or ATM payment?

options.text: Prefiro débito direto, por favor.
options.translation: I prefer direct debit, please.
options.feedback: Débito direto is often the easiest option for regular bills.

options.text: Multibanco, por favor.
options.translation: ATM payment, please.
options.feedback: Multibanco is also a very common and flexible payment method.

nodes.npc_text: Certo. A ligação da água fica ativa hoje. Obrigada pela sua chamada.
nodes.npc_translation: Right. The water connection will be active today. Thank you for your call.

options.text: Muito obrigada. Bom dia.
options.translation: Thank you very much. Good day.
options.feedback: A polite way to end the call. Well done!

situations.title: The Boiler Is Not Working
situations.summary: Phoning a technician because the boiler / water heater (esquentador) has stopped and there is no hot water for guests — describing the fault, arranging a visit.

phrase_patterns.base: Olá, o {aparelho} não está a funcionar.

dialogues.title: Calling for a Boiler Repair
dialogues.context: A guest house owner is calling a repair service because the boiler isn't working and guests have no hot water.

lines.speaker: Service
lines.text: Bom dia, assistência técnica. Diga!
lines.translation: Good morning, technical assistance. How can I help?

lines.speaker: Host
lines.text: Olá, bom dia. O meu esquentador não está a funcionar. Não há água quente nenhuma.
lines.translation: Hello, good morning. My water heater isn't working. There's no hot water at all.

lines.speaker: Service
lines.text: Pois não. Qual é a sua morada, por favor?
lines.translation: Oh, I see. What's your address, please?

lines.speaker: Host
lines.text: É na Rua do Castanheiro, número quinze. É urgente, tenho hóspedes.
lines.translation: It's on Rua do Castanheiro, number fifteen. It's urgent, I have guests.

lines.speaker: Service
lines.text: Certo. Podemos enviar um técnico hoje à tarde, entre as duas e as quatro. Serve?
lines.translation: Understood. We can send a technician this afternoon, between two and four. Does that work?

lines.speaker: Host
lines.text: Sim, serve perfeitamente. Muito obrigada!
lines.translation: Yes, that works perfectly. Thank you very much!

cultural_notes.title: Addressing Service Professionals
cultural_notes.body: In Portugal, when speaking to service professionals (like technicians, shop assistants, or receptionists), it's common to use 'o senhor' / 'a senhora' or simply use the third person verb conjugation as a polite form of address, rather than 'você'. Starting with 'Olá, bom dia' and ending with 'Muito obrigada' is standard polite practice.

cultural_notes.title: Expressing Urgency
cultural_notes.body: If a situation is time-sensitive, like having guests without hot water, it's appropriate to state 'É urgente' (It's urgent) or explain the reason for the urgency. This helps the service provider understand the priority.

roleplay.scenario: You are calling a local company in Madeira because the boiler in your rental property is not working and there is no hot water for your guests. You need to explain the problem and arrange for a technician to visit.

nodes.npc_text: Bom dia, empresa de assistência técnica. Diga!
nodes.npc_translation: Good morning, technical assistance company. How can I help?

options.text: Olá, bom dia. O meu esquentador não está a funcionar.
options.translation: Hello, good morning. My water heater isn't working.
options.feedback: A good clear start, stating the problem directly.

options.text: Preciso de um técnico para o esquentador.
options.translation: I need a technician for the water heater.
options.feedback: Also good, but slightly less detailed than the first option.

nodes.npc_text: Pois não. E qual é a avaria exatamente? Não tem água quente?
nodes.npc_translation: Oh, I see. And what's the fault exactly? You don't have hot water?

options.text: Exatamente, não há água quente nenhuma.
options.translation: Exactly, there's no hot water at all.
options.feedback: Clear confirmation of the problem.

options.text: Sim, não sai água quente da torneira.
options.translation: Yes, no hot water comes out of the tap.
options.feedback: A good alternative, adding a bit more detail.

nodes.npc_text: Certo. E qual é a sua morada, por favor?
nodes.npc_translation: Understood. And what's your address, please?

options.text: É na Rua das Flores, número vinte e cinco.
options.translation: It's on Rua das Flores, number twenty-five.
options.feedback: Providing the address clearly is key.

options.text: A morada é Rua das Flores, vinte e cinco.
options.translation: The address is Rua das Flores, twenty-five.
options.feedback: A slightly more concise way to give the address.

nodes.npc_text: Obrigado. Temos um técnico disponível hoje à tarde, entre as três e as cinco. Serve?
nodes.npc_translation: Thank you. We have a technician available this afternoon, between three and five. Does that work?

options.text: Sim, serve perfeitamente. Muito obrigada!
options.translation: Yes, that works perfectly. Thank you very much!
options.feedback: Confirming the appointment is a great way to finish the call.

options.text: Hoje à tarde é um bocado difícil. Não pode ser amanhã de manhã?
options.translation: This afternoon is a bit difficult. Can it not be tomorrow morning?
options.feedback: It's good to negotiate if the time doesn't suit, but be polite.

nodes.npc_text: Amanhã de manhã temos um espaço entre as nove e as onze. Está bem?
nodes.npc_translation: Tomorrow morning we have a slot between nine and eleven. Is that okay?

options.text: Sim, está ótimo. Muito obrigada!
options.translation: Yes, that's great. Thank you very much!
options.feedback: Excellent, you've successfully arranged a new time.

situations.title: Briefing the Cleaner
situations.summary: Telling the cleaner (a senhora da limpeza) what to do before the next check-in: turnaround, laundry, what to restock and when to come.
